Team, Today, Donald Trump returns to Washington as president, ready to unleash his dangerous MAGA agenda on America. Book bans. Reproductive freedom under attack. Democracy on the line. _**It all starts now. But here’s the good news: we can fight back immediately.**_ I’m Josh Weil, a public school teacher and Democrat running in Florida’s 6th Congressional District. This is the first major election of 2025, and we have the power to flip this seat and destroy Trump’s razor-thin majority in Congress. Make no mistake: this isn’t just about one seat. A win here stops Trump’s Project 2025 plan before it even takes off. [**That’s why I’m asking for your support today. We need to show strength right out of the gate—and the quickest way to do that is by reaching today's fundraising goal. Will you chip in $25, $50, or whatever you can right now—split evenly with Serve America?**](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/100681145/523555867/-423390815?refcode=250120_Serve_Weil_Inauguration&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMjUvMS83NDc1OCIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICIwZTg3NzgxOC00M2Q3LWVmMTEtODhkMC0wMDIyNDgyYTlkOTIiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=8pd-BJCIQ5WzFPRv002d9CC_UppowQB3LqclvvGbf4Y=) [DONATE](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/100681152/523555868/-2031701896?refcode=250120_Serve_Weil_Inauguration&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMjUvMS83NDc1OCIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICIwZTg3NzgxOC00M2Q3LWVmMTEtODhkMC0wMDIyNDgyYTlkOTIiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=8pd-BJCIQ5WzFPRv002d9CC_UppowQB3LqclvvGbf4Y=) This is about standing up for everything we believe in: free speech, public education, and a democracy that works for everyone. Trump’s allies, like my opponent, Randy Fine, are counting on us to give up. Let’s prove them wrong by FLIPPING this seat into Democratic hands in 2025. **This is THE most immediate way to make an impact.[Chip in now—split with Serve America—to power our campaign and fight back against Trump’s agenda.](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/100681155/523555869/-423390815?refcode=250120_Serve_Weil_Inauguration&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMjUvMS83NDc1OCIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICIwZTg3NzgxOC00M2Q3LWVmMTEtODhkMC0wMDIyNDgyYTlkOTIiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=8pd-BJCIQ5WzFPRv002d9CC_UppowQB3LqclvvGbf4Y=)** This is our moment. name: George Latimer
subject: We’re stronger together
disclaimer_text: Paid for by George Latimer for Congress
body:
George Latimer is a lifelong Westchester resident who has built a career in public service by delivering unparalleled progressive results for local residents. Now, he's taking that work to the next level by running to represent New York's 16th District in Congress. If you’d like to unsubscribe, click here. [https://secure.ngpvan.com/p/-Phg2RhDIEGJVPbb5vK87g2?unsubscribedata=BQ1xDvWOwpalV3XRlWC4rFsu7GDMowHXpQAn%2BNjfXGEBP3yChtwcQelLnuCG%2FCK51dpvGG%2FLUDbaAMTt4oDsBW8DnR%2BabsYU0UiA%2BT%2BeLdx9TgV4I3UZh6aHvKCziOTlGR6flsm98nsZWEUOkLKFVQX3zzKOxvwc7cWluACYeoyn524RTKniSBDX%2FO0Uy%2F2DkiySmRjwt0Z1D89VYlMZ9CAdLOlrfofvbYMd62XGK0YjliK91Tatbf5vn7ksJHESiFfxLdFc0Lq%2FKyoUzKlYc7axwpQk%2Fd4oO9bQr%2BOWD2eidBpZc%2BbeO8i1Nn1ygYv2WMe%2BzzpvmnQ1u7nSa3O%2FZDHRrBS2R6u8xLawXOUzi8si7GAG5kAjQ2vXDN3gbZBBchYYGCBGLrNtDB8YCe%2BzyBA3f02RoZhD5YO64jkU1%2BwSkmzucICpHHIWOJrO9h%2Br]

# [#]
Peter, today, on Martin Luther King Jr. Day, we honor the life and legacy of Dr. King by continuing his fight for justice, equality, and opportunity for all.

Today, we also observe one of the time-honored traditions of our great nation, the peaceful transfer of presidential power.

Peter, while our current political environment is fraught with division and uncertainty, I believe righteousness will prevail — if we work for it.

In the words of Dr. King: “The ultimate measure of a man is not where he stands in moments of comfort and convenience, but where he stands at times of challenge and controversy.”

I won’t sugar-coat it, Peter: the next 4 years will be a challenge, but we will get through them by standing together.

I remain committed to fighting relentlessly for every resident of this district. I have promised to deliver real results for NY-16, and I will follow through with that promise — no matter the obstacles. This fight is far from over, so will you join me in expanding Democratic progress by chipping in $ or any amount today? [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/latimer-em-fr-jan25?amount=]

DONATE NOW » [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/latimer-em-fr-jan25]

Thank you,

George
https://www.facebook.com/LatimerforNY [https://www.facebook.com/LatimerforNY] https://twitter.com/LatimerforNY [https://twitter.com/LatimerforNY]
https://secure.actblue.com/donate/latimer-em-footer-23 [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/latimer-em-footer-23]
For over three decades, George Latimer has been a champion for progressive policies and leaders, helping lead the effort to make Westchester, a formerly solid Republican County, into a blue firewall that has withstood multiple red waves in recent years. George’s unparalleled track record in advancing Democratic goals includes successfully cutting regressive taxes, fighting for civil rights and racial equality, defending a woman’s right to choose, standing up to the NRA to keep guns off our streets, and voting for marriage equality.

name: Elizabeth Nader
subject: Today’s the day!
body:
Peter, It’s Inauguration Day, friends! Or, as I like to think of it, “Return to Common Sense Day!” Donald Trump has been sw
|
---
| Peter, It’s Inauguration Day, friends! Or, as I like to think of it, “Return to Common Sense Day!” Donald Trump has been sworn in and is officially the 47th President of the United States! I’m blessed to be here in Washington for the festivities and there is definitely a new feeling in the air that was missing the last four years. Hope, optimism, and patriotism. I’ve never seen more red, white, and blue in our nation’s Capitol. The Left, predictably, are melting down, though not quite as spectacularly as they did in 2017. It’s hard to keep throwing tantrums that one-up the previous. Nancy Pelosi and other top Democrats boycotted the inauguration. Not that we’ll miss them... Artist Carrie Underwood is withstanding withering criticism for her accepting an invitation to perform at the inauguration. She performed with grace and dignity despite technical glitches. But we can ignore the tantrums because, friends, freedom is back. Common sense is back. Border security is coming back. Wokism’s days are numbered. Tough on crime policies are coming back. Economic recovery is on the way. And why are we about to experience the return of common sense? Because millions of common sense Americans stood up to the elite media, academia, and those pointing their fingers and telling us what we *should* think and instead we voted for our families, our livelihood, and our country. THAT is the power of democracy at work. The powerful Hollywood elite may have a lot – wealth, fame, and the freedom to do what they want with little consequence. But they learned in 2024 that they don’t have the power to shape American leadership. That power belongs to the people. Always has, and always will. So let’s celebrate – and let’s prepare to usher in a similar common sense wave to Trenton _next_ January! After you watch, shoot me an email and tell me your favorite part of the inaugural festivities! God bless America! For New Jersey, Elizabeth Nader
---
|
---
| President

name: Team AOC
subject: Hoping to reach you before noon
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Alexandria Ocasio-Cortez for Congress
body:
#
We’re not approaching living under an oligarchy — we’re already fully here.
Donald Trump will be inaugurated at noon today, and is raking in a huge amount of money from corporations and extremely wealthy donors:
**$5.** That’s how much we’re asking you to [give today to help us reach our goal](https://act.ocasiocortez.com/go/14659?c0=aoc-email-20250120.LhYhi4&refcode=em20250120-7949-P_FND_RES_OPP&amounts=5%2C20%2C50%2C100%2C250%2C500%2C1000&t=1&akid=7949%2E2526796%2EQqrx-F) to raise $100,000 before Trump is sworn in at noon.
$5 may not seem like a lot of money compared to Republican corporate mega-donors and super PACs, but **when we put all of our grassroots donations together, it amounts to a really powerful movement.**
Unlike Donald Trump, Alexandria doesn’t attend fancy cocktail party fundraisers to curry favor with billionaires. She operates independently from either Party’s establishment.
We need to show widespread support for Alexandria so she can continue doing what she was elected to do: **demand better.**
[ Will you make a grassroots contribution today to help us continue to fight for meaningful change and prepare to fight back against Trump these next four years?](https://act.ocasiocortez.com/go/14659?c0=aoc-email-20250120.LhYhi4&refcode=em20250120-7949-P_FND_RES_OPP&amounts=5%2C20%2C50%2C100%2C250%2C500%2C1000&t=2&akid=7949%2E2526796%2EQqrx-F)
**[Contribute $5](https://act.ocasiocortez.com/go/14659?c0=aoc-email-20250120.LhYhi4&refcode=em20250120-7949-P_FND_RES_OPP&amounts=5%2C20%2C50%2C100%2C250%2C500%2C1000&t=21&akid=7949%2E2526796%2EQqrx-F)**
Whether you can afford to donate today or not, we’re so grateful that you’re a part of this campaign.
In solidarity,
Team AOC

**[Donate](https://secure.actblue.com/donate/aoc-email-20250120?refcode=em20250120-7949-P_FND_RES_OPP&t=22&refcode2=7949_2526796_Qqrx-F&akid=7949%2E2526796%2EQqrx-F)** | **[Merch](https://shop.ocasiocortez.com/?utm_source=em-footer-20250120-7949-P_FND_RES_OPP&t=23&akid=7949%2E2526796%2EQqrx-F)**
---|---

---|---|---
Email us: [speak@ocasiocortez.com](mailto: speak@ocasiocortez.com)
This email was sent to dpwillis67@gmail.com. Email is the most important way we keep in touch with people like you, so thank you for reading to the end. Small donors like you keep us going. To contribute via check, please address to Alexandria Ocasio-Cortez for Congress, PO Box 680080, Corona, NY 11368.

name: Joni Ernst [R-IA]
subject: Donald Trump was just sworn in
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Joni for Iowa
body:
To help usher in the new administration, I’m hoping to break a Trump endorsement record to celebrate Donald Trump’s Inauguration and first day back in the White House.

| |
---
| | | It's official, Friend! Donald Trump was just sworn in as the 47th President of the United States.

**This is a historic day for our country, and I 'm excited to get to work on DAY ONE of Trump's new administration to get America back on track!**

NONE of this would've been possible without your support, Friend. Every survey you participated in, dollar you donated, and endorsement you sent Donald Trump's way helped make this victory possible!

**To help usher in the new administration, I 'm hoping to break a Trump endorsement record to celebrate Donald Trump's inauguration and first day back in the White House.**

_**[So, will you take just a moment to submit your endorsement of Donald Trump and his administration?](https://e.trumptrainnews.com/rd/9z2z0js3di9sqqp7d5a61gnlshusuc1s3oqtnc45lv8_rp22sh2s8i6epj7c5g6ao36chh2bs "So, will you take just a moment to submit your endorsement of Donald Trump and his administration?")**_
---
| | | [ENDORSE DONALD TRUMP](https://e.trumptrainnews.com/rd/9z2zd48g657jqg5aepbvkngmf0njpo410p9jq875u28_rp22sh2s8i6epj7c5g6ao36chh2bs "ENDORSE DONALD TRUMP")
---
If we can collect 5,000 more endorsements before midnight, we'll break our record and be able to send Team Trump a list of top supporters who are ready to support the administration in the days to come.

_And I know you 'll want to be one of them, Friend!_

I look forward to seeing your name when we pull our final endorsement count at midnight.

Thank you for your support,

Joni Ernst

---

---

name: Gabe Amo
subject: Reflecting on Dr. MLK Day
body:
Hi Friend, The celebration of the Reverend Dr. Martin Luther King, Jr. holiday is one of the most meaningful observances that we share in the United States.| | | [](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/100681907/523557554/-1283252511?nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQNTAvMS8xMDU0NTUiLA0KICAiRGlzdHJpYnV0aW9uVW5pcXVlSWQiOiAiOTZlN2YzOGUtNDRkNy1lZjExLTg4ZDAtMDAyMjQ4MmE5ZDkyIiwNCiAgIkVtYWlsQWRkcmVzcyI6ICJkcHdpbGxpczY3QGdtYWlsLmNvbSINCn0%3D&hmac=NqetQWSzl8l9rOoGh7rawIeZaSMT1Mbx7Cgbep7f97A=&emci=07333a2b-42d7-ef11-88d0-0022482a9d92&emdi=96e7f38e-44d7-ef11-88d0-0022482a9d92&ceid=12466492)
---
| | Hi Friend,
---
The celebration of the Reverend Dr. Martin Luther King, Jr. holiday is one of the most meaningful observances that we share in the United States. While we have made progress from the time of Dr. King’s time here on Earth, the mission remains unfinished to address the causes to which he gave his life: racial equality, economic opportunity, eradication of poverty, and ending wars. We still have work to do and on MLK Day, in our way, we can take a moment to reflect on how we can work towards Dr. King’s goals.
---
---
This holiday – which I see as a “day on, not a day off” – allows us to recommit to the values of our nation and in every segment of our population calls us to ask as Dr. King did, “Where do we go from here, chaos or community?” I know that what unites us, not just as Democrats, but as Americans, is that our answer to that question is community.
---
In countless ways, I would not have the great honor and privilege of serving Rhode Islanders in the U.S. House of Representatives if it were not for the civil rights struggle led by Dr. King and his contemporaries in the struggle. I know that is the same for many elected officials, labor leaders, party activists, and committed people who recognize that we are responsible for coming together to build the beloved community of Dr. King’s dream. In my work in Congress, I’m inspired to serve our state in pursuing good wages and fair conditions for workers, affordable housing, gun violence prevention, retirement security, affordable health care, global diplomacy, climate change solutions, and environmental justice.
---
Now, as our nation is experiencing a transition in power that makes those goals harder, I’m again called to Dr. King’s words:
---
_“The ultimate measure of a man is not where he stands in a time of convenience and comfort, but where he stands at times of challenge and controversy.”_
---
At a time when our country is seemingly divided, this is certainly not the easiest of times to be engaged in the work of politics and government. But this is when our engagement matters the most. I hope that as we observe the holiday this year, we take heart of Dr. King’s call to lead with love – love for our neighbors, state, country, and world:
---
_“Darkness cannot drive out darkness, only light can do that. Hate cannot drive out hate, only love can do that.”_
---
Have a reflective and purposeful Martin Luther King, Jr. Holiday.
---
\- Congressman Gabe Amo
---
| | |
---

name: House GOP =?UTF-8?Q?=E2=80=94?= via Team Scalise
subject: IT’S OFFICIAL >> Donald J. Trump is now our 47th President of the United States!
disclaimer_text: Paid for by TEAM SCALISE, a joint fundraising committee authorized by and to benefit SCALISE FOR CONGRESS and EYE OF THE TIGER PAC
body:
Help us show our excitement – sign our congratulations card!

| |
---
| | | **CONFIRMED: The Oath of Office has just been taken and Donald J. Trump is now our 47th President of the United States!**

It's truly an honor, Mr. President.

name: Jocelyn Benson
subject: MLK Day
disclaimer_text: PAID FOR BY JOCELYN BENSON FOR SECRETARY OF STATE
body:
We must strive to honor the legacy of Dr. King today and every day.
# [](https://act.jocelynbenson.com/go/5?t=1&akid=40%2E181023%2E9cSDU-)
_"Now is the time to make real the promises of democracy... We cannot walk alone. And as we walk, we must make the pledge that we shall always march ahead. We cannot turn back."_

— Martin Luther King Jr., Detroit, June 23, 1963
Team, on this MLK day, I want to share with you these very special words Dr. King said right here in Michigan.
On this cold day of remembrance and responsibility, it is painfully clear that the fights of the past are not over.
We’ve emerged from a hard-fought election not only with the disappointment of loss, but with the realization that the incoming president plans to put an end to the American Experiment starting Day One. That his allies are already at work, installing a regime built to wheel and deal away the rights Americans fought and died for.
That billionaires, whose staggering wealth and power still isn’t enough to satisfy them, have snatched up our sacred institutions as their latest trophies. Now, they are coming for our rights and our freedoms.
I’m writing to you today to say that despite what might come in the next four, six, or eight years, I’ll be there every step of the way in this new, uncertain time. We cannot do this work alone, and every single ally of democracy in this state must be prepared to stand up and fight.
Leaders like MLK knew that their work was the work of a lifetime, it goes beyond one year, one president, even one decade. We as a nation have been on the brink before. Generations in this struggle have been handed bitter losses after what felt like lasting victories. We are all in this together, for the long haul.
My north star the fact that we are doing the work every day to ensure we live in an inclusive, fair society where we honor every voice, protect every vote, keep each other safe, and recognize our common humanity.
We must always be willing to speak out, speak up, and ensure all voices are heard. It’s the only way we can enact change, and it’s the only way we can make our lives better. I hope we all have the courage to speak up when the time is right, just as Dr. King did. **We must remember that the power of the people is always greater than the people in power.**
Today, and every day, may we reflect on the life taken, the life he led, and commit ourselves to continuing his work for justice and equality. The people of this state will dig deep and show the nation what Michiganders do in a fight -- we never give up.
I’m here. I’m ready. I’m with you. And I’m not backing down.
-- Jocelyn

* * *
Before you go... we need your help. Since her 2018 election, Secretary Benson has fought for free and fair elections. She’s on the front lines of defending and strengthening our democracy. **[Will you contribute today to show your support?](https://secure.actblue.com/donate/jb-em-20250120-1?refcode=em20250120-1&refcode2=40_181023_9cSDU-&akid=40%2E181023%2E9cSDU-)** **[Donate »](https://secure.actblue.com/donate/jb-em-20250120-1?refcode=em20250120-1&refcode2=40_181023_9cSDU-&akid=40%2E181023%2E9cSDU-)**
---

* * *
This email was sent to dpwillis67@gmail.com. Email is a critical way we communicate with grassroots supporters like you. However, if you would like to change your subscription preferences, here are some options:
* [Receive Fewer Emails](https://act.jocelynbenson.com/signup/receive-fewer-emails/?t=2&akid=40%2E181023%2E9cSDU-)
* [Update Your Information](https://act.jocelynbenson.com/signup/update-your-information/)
* [Unsubscribe](https://act.jocelynbenson.com/unsubscribe/unsubscribe_2024/?t=3&akid=40%2E181023%2E9cSDU-)

Jocelyn Benson for Secretary of State
PO BOX 21368,
Detroit, MI 48221
PAID FOR BY JOCELYN BENSON FOR SECRETARY OF STATE

name: Jeff Merkley
subject: I will attend Trump’s inauguration, but let me tell you why:
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Jeff Merkley for Oregon
body:
The peaceful transfer of power is an essential factor for the stability, legitimacy, and resilience of our democratic institutions that have been under attack in recent years.
| | [](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/100682349/523558636/150430950?nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQNDEvMS84OTMyNSIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICJiMDIyZjU4Mi00NWQ3LWVmMTEtODhkMC0wMDIyNDgyYTlkOTIiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=h3RGPTWTaP5lxcCwTCqKmPFhXb3OCSno1TFfn4hgp-o=&emci=91ece3bd-34d7-ef11-88d0-0022482a9d92&emdi=b022f582-45d7-ef11-88d0-0022482a9d92&ceid=7731215)
---

| | Today, Donald Trump will be inaugurated as our next President, and I will be in attendance. I didn't come to this decision lightly. I went back and forth on whether or not to attend the inauguration of a man who is already mobilizing a destructive MAGA agenda. But I believe it's my responsibility to bear witness to the peaceful transfer of power, a pillar of our democracy. The peaceful transfer of power is an essential factor for the stability, legitimacy, and resilience of our democratic institutions that have been under attack in recent years. I disagree with President Trump on many policies, but I support the legitimacy of his election and inauguration. This process is vital to reinforce much-needed trust in our electoral system. **Make no mistake: I will fight the MAGA Republican agenda—one that threatens to undermine critical protections for working families, women, democracy, and our planet.** But today, I will be in attendance to witness the peaceful transfer of power from one administration to the next. name: Josh Gottheimer
subject: today is an opportunity to recommit
disclaimer_text: PAID FOR BY JOSH GOTTHEIMER FOR GOVERNOR
body:
# [#] Today, we remember Dr. Martin Luther King Jr and the Civil Rights Movement.

The vision Dr. King had for our country was never deterred by politics or partisanship. Rather, Dr. King was guided by the belief that every person — no matter their skin color — deserves equal rights.

Dr. King told us what America could be, and I believe in his dream. I’m determined to make his dream, and the dream of millions of Americans, a reality.

There is still much more work to do. We must recommit ourselves to taking action together and I know that in the greatest country in the world, our best days will always be ahead.

In honor of Dr. King on this national day of service, I implore you to not view today as a day off, but a day on — it’s an opportunity to recommit to the spirit of service and the fight for a more just world.

Thank you,

Josh
Josh is running to be the next Governor of New Jersey in 2025 – the “Lower Taxes, Lower Costs Governor.” Since 2016, his campaign lawn signs have always said the same thing: “Lower Taxes. Jersey Values” – and that’s what he’s done, from expanding the Child Tax Credit to fighting to restore SALT to clawing dollars back to Jersey for first responders. If you'd like to support his campaign click here [https://secure.ngpvan.com/zwqVCKyxzki-GzysVtmLBA2] , or to unsubscribe click here. name: Marcy Kaptur
subject: Celebrating Martin Luther King Jr. Day
disclaimer_text: PAID FOR BY KAPTUR FOR CONGRESS
body:
Marcy Kaptur for Congress [https://prod.cdn.everyaction.com/images/van/NGP/NGP33/1/95655/images/Kaptur%20Logo.png]

Friend, the final book that Dr. Martin Luther King Jr. published in his lifetime was titled Where Do We Go From Here: Chaos or Community?

It's a question that resonates as urgently today as it did nearly sixty years ago.

The paths in front of us today couldn't be more divergent. We can give in to chaos, division, fear, and inequality — or we can do the difficult and rewarding work of building community, taking care of each other, and creating a better world.

In the final years before his life was cut tragically short, Dr. King continued to be a champion for civil rights and racial equality. But his moral vision didn't stop there. He spoke out with resounding clarity against income inequality and was a leading advocate for peace around the world.

Dr. King believed in a future without discrimination, poverty, or injustice. That's the future we're still fighting for today. I'm grateful to have you with me, friend.

— Marcy
PAID FOR BY KAPTUR FOR CONGRESS

name: The Collective PAC
subject: honoring Dr. King's legacy
disclaimer_text: Paid for by the Collective PAC; not authorized by any candidate or candidate's committee
body:
| | If you want to help The Collective PAC build Black political power up and down the ballot, and across the country, then you’re in the right place. If you'd like to unsubscribe [click here.](https://an.collectivepac.org/ss/c/u001.qUXRBnqZ7T8nxbpAcIVwoUsWLkUMWlo_0JmZttSlm3-gP0Xd1LL-Lub7Gk22PHy9biRkT0ZFLBZo_8f8EnE7Jowo6A0R8oop8Rrw_kitS3sMwrJn2ozruxmkvaG936_l1duoWABImS72zia5WVvFdXg0GFHHiORZVQTtWILlH4DJBYfJP9SzvMsnYFFqVtBAQwXLzf9dF7m0O4rhhU6mYjNYqrqWRr7yvs3VtzHto2_yVG6VWtgvqoPjolZUUQNrbM6VZD7cHMAJM3AVsWHRDRxHH3yOfMg6ItO5oxEHsKYpuH6z-zxvpYJFFuk6NVRfnUplzZNEAiMFTOTHsl8TSLU4Kc-CIADhLHO2E2zmIApIyflM1Mg3EGtqAxprmwHrm93GvOtGvbDg4ZtkmhhrKPptiNHYgs_p84MCjQT-UP2OsnfnjFmXhL-CXUjIkV-Q6A3ULnLjn6MVUOafXyZ4Dm3QenJ6DAgqIBjHumW6O6M/4db/s09CmJUrTjCPa9lTjto8uA/h0/h001.2aB7VFUFaqESA1ShHDAmAYXTCDd4AW3wf8sCeIJ68Po) | | Today, as we commemorate the life and legacy of Martin Luther King Jr., we are reminded why we do what we do — because, Peter, **the time is always right to do what is right.**

Recently, we have been confronted with the harsh reality of attacks on our democracy from elected "leaders." But in these moments of despair, we must turn to Dr. King’s wisdom and remember that change does not roll in on the wheels of inevitability; it comes through continuous struggle. Even in the face of setbacks and hostility, we must keep moving forward, knowing that every small step builds toward a better tomorrow.

At The Collective PAC, we are committed to carrying forward Dr. King’s legacy through our work to empower Black voices at all levels of government across the country. Even in the face of bigotry, we will never waiver in our mission to create a government that is representative of _all_ the people it serves.

Every day, we strive to create the country Dr. King dreamed of — and we couldn’t be more grateful to have you standing with us. In solidarity and with renewed resolve, let us walk on in the days ahead with an audacious faith in the future, in our unyielding pursuit of what is right.

Together, we shall overcome.

— The Collective PAC
* * *

[](https://an.collectivepac.org/ss/c/u001.iIBo0N5fSFzEFL40Tn-vtbMETCUYClkOrOh9-SNJfqtMtM9o7MpfQTM1SqECAoa9PnuvrCzoBy5DfIGWsplANbnjpBAv4hJoqiZg5D3GXPpR6LpIvt2np0PLWbMsmbfH96_FzoxqO4JFL5XsraY0TzB_ZsWbQ1bWDnRg8cX5DjruvvlHASNOiBs98g_NZW81QBC5egq-H7MzOgRGwr8D-Z3o9kBP9e02Mxwrfw13SsaLPZbuu56CTvIGU9cPUpHuiUDVKnTLxYzU9BcuWsd_pXXpsbXRZVYja0y8VjeUoFj50YS_V0QX1sw-1-uex0GcWGjlTsRUotp7iIj-gdHsrmUSiHQqMM9HyuGU-Vk77kDFlc2G06IUF04W28NWo7rA-uc0R0YvxQ2dTTs_Y7ntYRnYT7n5c5Oz2Qulf-oYZs8-zxg4kULxcx63MTxkxz41/4db/s09CmJUrTjCPa9lTjto8uA/h1/h001.umz6rc-x42rhCQDxVDiBxJENBpxfm6mNu18ZfjtVUJ0) | The Collective PAC is strategically focused on creating a truly equitable democracy where our nation’s local, state and federal governments have diverse and talented elected leadership and Black people are fully represented in positions of power to create the policies necessary to progress our communities forward. Since our inception in 2016, we have helped 500 candidates win general elections at the local, state and federal levels. We strive to support the next wave of progressive and unapologetic Black candidates who will usher in a sea of change in our political system while helping America fulfill its promise of democracy by creating a government that will truly be for the people and of the people.

name: Jay Jones
subject: Fighting for Martin Luther King Jr.’s legacy
body:
jayjones.com [jayjones.com]
Folks – today is both Martin Luther King Jr. Day and the inauguration of convicted felon Donald Trump as our 47th President of the United States.
As a civil rights leader, Dr. Martin Luther King Jr. was truly the definition of what it means to be a patriot. He once said, “Let us move in these powerful days – these days of challenge – to make America what it ought to be.”
Those words could not be more true right now, friends. With Donald Trump ready to create chaos and demonize Americans who are not like him, we need to show ourselves and everyone else that every American deserves to have the chance to accomplish anything they put their minds to.
Over the next four years, we will face many challenges. But we will always face these challenges together. Because the hard work is just beginning.
Because the fact of the matter is this: we will continue to choose hope over fear, freedom over violence, and unity over division. And we must continue Dr. King’s fight.
Each and every one of you gives me hope for a brighter future. So let’s roll up our sleeves and do the hard, meaningful work to bring equality and justice for all.
Thank you,
Jay

Jay Jones for Virginia
1490-5A Quarterpath Road, #195
Williamsburg, VA 23185
United States

clean_body: jayjones.com [jayjones.com] Folks – today is both Martin Luther King Jr. Day and the inauguration of convicted felon Donald Trump as our 47th President of the United States. As a civil rights leader, Dr. Martin Luther King Jr. was truly the definition of what it means to be a patriot. He once said, “Let us move in these powerful days – these days of challenge – to make America what it ought to be.” Those words could not be more true right now, friends. With Donald Trump ready to create chaos and demonize Americans who are not like him, we need to show ourselves and everyone else that every American deserves to have the chance to accomplish anything they put their minds to. Over the next four years, we will face many challenges. But we will always face these challenges together. Because the hard work is just beginning. Because the fact of the matter is this: we will continue to choose hope over fear, freedom over violence, and unity over division. And we must continue Dr. King’s fight. Each and every one of you gives me hope for a brighter future. So let’s roll up our sleeves and do the hard, meaningful work to bring equality and justice for all. Thank you, Jay Jay Jones for Virginia 1490-5A Quarterpath Road, #195 Williamsburg, VA 23185 United States If you believe you received this message in error or wish to no longer receive email from us, please unsubscribe: .

name: Emily Randall
subject: On this dark day, I can tell my story
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Emily Randall for Congress
body:
If you’re feeling like all is lost and we have no chance of stopping Trump’s agenda, let me remind you of my story:

| Peter, I haven’t been looking forward to January 20, 2025. President Trump, the man who paved the way for the Supreme Court to overturn _Roe v. Wade_ and strip millions of Americans of their fundamental rights, will take the Oath of Office this afternoon. If you’re feeling discouraged, I don’t blame you. But if you’re feeling like all is lost and we have no chance of stopping Trump’s agenda, let me remind you of my story: When Trump first won, I was working for Planned Parenthood, and I saw the threat he posed to our reproductive freedoms. Fundraising and protesting weren’t enough. I decided on Election Night 2016 that I would run for office so I could be in a position to change the policies and protect my neighbors. It wasn’t easy. I was told over and over that “a queer Latina who worked for Planned Parenthood,” could not win in my competitive State Senate district. But not only did I win — I helped pass nearly 200 new laws, was elected Deputy Majority Leader by my colleagues, and won a race to become the country’s first queer Latina in Congress. I’m proof that the future isn’t set. The Trump agenda isn’t inevitable. And threats we face next, we’ll face them together. **My two goals are to protect our neighbors from the worst of the MAGA policies and to do everything I can to take back the House as soon as possible. That’s why Team Emily is starting to fundraise and organize NOW, so we’re in the best possible position to support Democratic candidates in 2026.[_Can you chip in $10, $25, or any amount to fuel this clean_body: | = Team, Eight years ago, Donald Trump stood on the steps of the U.S. Capitol and swore an oath to lead our country. What followed was a presidency defined by chaos, corruption, and cruelty—and it emboldened Ohio Republicans to embrace reckless, self-serving politics. Today, Trump is being inaugurated again. His enablers, including VP J.D. Vance, Mike DeWine, Bernie Moreno, and Frank LaRose are salivating at the chance to put the same extreme, unpopular agenda into action in Ohio. They want to ban abortion nationwide, undermine our democracy, and rig the system so they can hold on to power no matter what. = We’ll be the thorn in the side of the Trump administration & extreme politicians everywhere. We’ll expose the truth about the GOP’s corruption, hold extremists like J.D. Vance accountable, and show Ohioans that our party is the one fighting for working families. The hard truth is the GOP machine is fueled by millions in special-interest cash. = If you've saved your payment information with ActBlue Express, your donation will be processed immediately: = = = = = = Let’s get to work, Liz Walters, ODP Chair = | Ohio Democratic Party PO Box 130 Columbus, Ohio 43216 | Paid for by the Ohio Democratic Party and not authorized by any candidate or candidate's committee. | Click here to from the Ohio Democratic Party's email list.

body:
Texas Organizing Project (TOP) PAC’s aim is to further push the state toward a progressive future that will deliver substantive change and shift more power into the hands of Black and Latino Texans. # [#]
Peter,

Today, Donald Trump is officially President of the United States. Again.

It’s no secret that he stands in direct opposition to the work we do here at TOP PAC — the work we do to elect progressives up and down the ballot and fight for a stronger, more progressive, and more inclusive Texas.

From inciting a deadly riot at our nation’s Capitol to taking credit for the fall of Roe v. Wade , it’s clear that Trump is a threat to everything we hold dear.

It’s going to be a long four years, but we’ll be here fighting for human rights, democracy, and freedom every step of the way.

If you’d like to join the fight with us, you can donate any amount today. Together, we can build a Texas and a Union that works for ALL of us regardless of race, income, gender, preferred language, or ZIP Code. [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/top-em-jan25]

If you've stored your payment information with ActBlue Express, your donation will be processed immediately.

name: Ai-jen Poo | Care in Action
subject: We aren’t ceding any ground
body:
Together, we will defend our communities.
[](https://click.everyaction.com/k/100684052/523562899/664783965?ms=012025_inaugday&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9UU00vVFNNV08vMS83NjczNSIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICI2YWM3NzM5OC00N2Q3LWVmMTEtODhkMC0wMDIyNDgyYTlkOTIiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=KO1IAd_VmHtw870HepixlFjXwfZlQ8o9rQ6AhyNIHGA=&emci=a319c141-13d5-ef11-88d0-0022482a9d92&emdi=6ac77398-47d7-ef11-88d0-0022482a9d92&ceid=864787)
Hi Peter,
Today is Inauguration Day, and our vision is clear. Across the country, our members and communities are suiting up for this next fight.
**We will rise to this moment.** We will organize, we will work together, and our movement will only grow.
We know that our communities — care workers, immigrants, and women of color — are some of the most vulnerable and unprotected.
Threats of mass deportations, cuts to essential care programs, restrictions on women’s rights and abortion access — it’s hard to keep up with all of these plans, but we will not be distracted or exhausted.
**We won’t step back or give up:** We’re going to keep up the push to protect and expand rights for care workers, immigrant women, and women of color. [**_Will you join Care in Action as we re-commit to the work of defending our communities?_**](https://click.everyaction.com/k/100684064/523562929/19321869?ms=012025_inaugday&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9UU00vVFNNV08vMS83NjczNSIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICI2YWM3NzM5OC00N2Q3LWVmMTEtODhkMC0wMDIyNDgyYTlkOTIiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=KO1IAd_VmHtw870HepixlFjXwfZlQ8o9rQ6AhyNIHGA=&emci=a319c141-13d5-ef11-88d0-0022482a9d92&emdi=6ac77398-47d7-ef11-88d0-0022482a9d92&ceid=864787)_ _
[Defend our Communities](https://click.everyaction.com/k/100684079/523562949/19321869?ms=012025_inaugday&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9UU00vVFNNV08vMS83NjczNSIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICI2YWM3NzM5OC00N2Q3LWVmMTEtODhkMC0wMDIyNDgyYTlkOTIiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=KO1IAd_VmHtw870HepixlFjXwfZlQ8o9rQ6AhyNIHGA=&emci=a319c141-13d5-ef11-88d0-0022482a9d92&emdi=6ac77398-47d7-ef11-88d0-0022482a9d92&ceid=864787)
**We are here to stay.** Long before today, our movement – led by care workers, Black women, women of color, and immigrant women – organized for generations to champion a vision of care and to protect each other.
**As we reflect on the legacy of Martin Luther King, Jr. today,** we draw inspiration from him, from the broader Civil Rights Movement, and from the women of color throughout history who have led fights for freedom.
_Our collective power is enormous._
**We have shown up for our communities before.** In 2017, we came out in force to stand up for women, immigrants, and working people – from flooding airports to protest the Muslim Ban to rallying across the country to demand that Families Belong Together.
_Right now, we need you to join us in our vital effort to protect our communities and take care of each other._[**_Say you’re in this fight to protect our communities during the Trump administration, and take the next step in continuing the work to build a world where care workers, immigrant women, and women of color can thrive >> _**](https://click.everyaction.com/k/100684094/523562978/19321869?ms=012025_inaugday&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9UU00vVFNNV08vMS83NjczNSIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICI2YWM3NzM5OC00N2Q3LWVmMTEtODhkMC0wMDIyNDgyYTlkOTIiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=KO1IAd_VmHtw870HepixlFjXwfZlQ8o9rQ6AhyNIHGA=&emci=a319c141-13d5-ef11-88d0-0022482a9d92&emdi=6ac77398-47d7-ef11-88d0-0022482a9d92&ceid=864787)_ _
****************[Defend our Communities](https://click.everyaction.com/k/100684101/523562997/19321869?ms=012025_inaugday&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9UU00vVFNNV08vMS83NjczNSIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICI2YWM3NzM5OC00N2Q3LWVmMTEtODhkMC0wMDIyNDgyYTlkOTIiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=KO1IAd_VmHtw870HepixlFjXwfZlQ8o9rQ6AhyNIHGA=&emci=a319c141-13d5-ef11-88d0-0022482a9d92&emdi=6ac77398-47d7-ef11-88d0-0022482a9d92&ceid=864787)****************
Together, we are strong.
In solidarity,
Ai-jen Poo
Care in Action Senior Advisor
[](https://click.everyaction.com/k/100684110/523563010/-1554439957?ms=012025_inaugday&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9UU00vVFNNV08vMS83NjczNSIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICI2YWM3NzM5OC00N2Q3LWVmMTEtODhkMC0wMDIyNDgyYTlkOTIiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=KO1IAd_VmHtw870HepixlFjXwfZlQ8o9rQ6AhyNIHGA=&emci=a319c141-13d5-ef11-88d0-0022482a9d92&emdi=6ac77398-47d7-ef11-88d0-0022482a9d92&ceid=864787) [](https://click.everyaction.com/k/100684117/523563025/326371895?ms=012025_inaugday&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9UU00vVFNNV08vMS83NjczNSIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICI2YWM3NzM5OC00N2Q3LWVmMTEtODhkMC0wMDIyNDgyYTlkOTIiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=KO1IAd_VmHtw870HepixlFjXwfZlQ8o9rQ6AhyNIHGA=&emci=a319c141-13d5-ef11-88d0-0022482a9d92&emdi=6ac77398-47d7-ef11-88d0-0022482a9d92&ceid=864787) [](https://click.everyaction.com/k/100684123/523563061/955979663?ms=012025_inaugday&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9UU00vVFNNV08vMS83NjczNSIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICI2YWM3NzM5OC00N2Q3LWVmMTEtODhkMC0wMDIyNDgyYTlkOTIiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=KO1IAd_VmHtw870HepixlFjXwfZlQ8o9rQ6AhyNIHGA=&emci=a319c141-13d5-ef11-88d0-0022482a9d92&emdi=6ac77398-47d7-ef11-88d0-0022482a9d92&ceid=864787)
This email was sent by [Care in Action](https://click.everyaction.com/k/100684133/523563102/664783965?ms=012025_inaugday&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9UU00vVFNNV08vMS83NjczNSIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICI2YWM3NzM5OC00N2Q3LWVmMTEtODhkMC0wMDIyNDgyYTlkOTIiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=KO1IAd_VmHtw870HepixlFjXwfZlQ8o9rQ6AhyNIHGA=&emci=a319c141-13d5-ef11-88d0-0022482a9d92&emdi=6ac77398-47d7-ef11-88d0-0022482a9d92&ceid=864787). Care In Action was founded by progressive women of color. Care in Action is the policy and advocacy home for women who care, working on behalf of more than 2 million domestic workers and care workers in America. Care in Action is a 501(c)(4) nonprofit organization. Donations to Care in Action are not tax-deductible.

clean_body: Together, we will defend our communities. Hi Peter, Today is Inauguration Day, and our vision is clear. Across the country, our members and communities are suiting up for this next fight. We will rise to this moment. We will organize, we will work together, and our movement will only grow. We know that our communities — care workers, immigrants, and women of color — are some of the most vulnerable and unprotected. Threats of mass deportations, cuts to essential care programs, restrictions on women’s rights and abortion access — it’s hard to keep up with all of these plans, but we will not be distracted or exhausted. We won’t step back or give up: We’re going to keep up the push to protect and expand rights for care workers, immigrant women, and women of color. We are here to stay. Long before today, our movement – led by care workers, Black women, women of color, and immigrant women – organized for generations to champion a vision of care and to protect each other. As we reflect on the legacy of Martin Luther King, Jr. today, we draw inspiration from him, from the broader Civil Rights Movement, and from the women of color throughout history who have led fights for freedom. Our collective power is enormous. We have shown up for our communities before. In 2017, we came out in force to stand up for women, immigrants, and working people – from flooding airports to protest the Muslim Ban to rallying across the country to demand that Families Belong Together. Right now, we need you to join us in our vital effort to protect our communities and take care of each other. **************************** Together, we are strong. In solidarity, Ai-jen Poo Care in Action Senior Advisor

subject: Emerge women are bending arc of history

body:
At Emerge, we carry forward Dr. King’s legacy by empowering women to lead and fight for justice in every community.

| [](https://click.everyaction.com/k/100684615/523564138/-245045952?refcode=BLU_EM_EMRG_2025-01-20_1&amount=25&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9BVi9BVkVNUi8xLzgxMzIxIiwNCiAgIkRpc3RyaWJ1dGlvblVuaXF1ZUlkIjogIjY0ODBkYmM0LTQ3ZDctZWYxMS04OGQwLTAwMjI0ODJhOWQ5MiIsDQogICJFbWFpbEFkZHJlc3MiOiAiZHB3aWxsaXM2N0BnbWFpbC5jb20iDQp9&hmac=FCtTdKcgp_DKdGEqc20G8lf5BPay9yx-Zq-FytkLa18=)

| Peter,

Today, we honor Reverend Dr. Martin Luther King, Jr., a leader who transformed our nation with his vision, courage, and unwavering commitment to justice. His work reminds us that change doesn’t happen on its own—it requires action, leadership, and a collective commitment to building a better future.
---
[](https://click.everyaction.com/k/100684625/523564149/-245045952?refcode=BLU_EM_EMRG_2025-01-20_1&amount=25&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9BVi9BVkVNUi8xLzgxMzIxIiwNCiAgIkRpc3RyaWJ1dGlvblVuaXF1ZUlkIjogIjY0ODBkYmM0LTQ3ZDctZWYxMS04OGQwLTAwMjI0ODJhOWQ5MiIsDQogICJFbWFpbEFkZHJlc3MiOiAiZHB3aWxsaXM2N0BnbWFpbC5jb20iDQp9&hmac=FCtTdKcgp_DKdGEqc20G8lf5BPay9yx-Zq-FytkLa18=)
At Emerge, we carry forward Dr. King’s legacy by empowering women to lead and fight for justice in every community. Women have historically been underrepresented in government, and their voices are crucial to ensuring a fair and inclusive society. That’s why we work tirelessly to prepare women, especially those from underrepresented communities, to step into leadership roles.

Emerge is recruiting and training women who are part of the fabric of their communities and represent the New American Majority — Black, Brown, Indigenous women, women of color, as well as young, LGBTQ+, and unmarried women. We believe a government that truly reflects its people must include diverse voices and perspectives.

Even today, discrimination and inequality remain deeply rooted in our institutions. Dr. King’s work reminds us that confronting these challenges takes courage and persistence. The women we train are stepping up to lead the way, working to shape policies that protect civil rights, expand opportunity, and advance justice for everyone.

As we reflect on Dr. King’s enduring impact, we also recognize the challenges ahead. Inspired by his legacy, we stand ready to fight against any efforts that threaten democracy or equality. Together, we are lifting up women’s voices, ensuring that their leadership helps bend the arc of history toward justice.

Thank you for being part of this movement and for believing in the power of women to lead and create a better future for all.

In solidarity,
Team Emerge

* * *

name: Jeanne Shaheen
subject: A personal note
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Shaheen for Senate Contributions or gifts to Shaheen for Senate are not tax deductible
body:
I'm as determined as I've ever been
| | |
---
Friend, head of Donald Trump's inauguration this afternoon, I've been reflecting on the coming months and years of his administration, and I wanted to send a personal note to you.
There is zero question that we have some incredibly tough fights ahead of us – fights for our freedoms and potentially fights for our very democracy. But I promise you that I will fearlessly take these fights head-on.
No matter what happens in the next four years, I will never stop working as hard as I possibly can to create a better future for you and your family. That means bolstering our national security and boosting our economy. It means standing up for our veterans. It means giving small businesses the support they need to thrive. And it means defending our most fundamental rights – including our reproductive freedom.
I can't sit here and tell you that getting these things done with Donald Trump in the White House will be easy. It won't be. But I'm as determined as I've ever been to hold him and his allies accountable, to stop extremism from defining our future and to make a _real_ difference in the lives of our families.
In fact, I'm already getting started. During the Senate confirmation hearings for Trump's cabinet nominees, I haven't been afraid to fiercely question them about their qualifications, hold them accountable for their past behavior and emphasize the need to _actually_ work together across party lines for the good of our country and the American people. You can count on me to continue being a check on this administration at every turn.
While the next four years are filled with uncertainty and anxiety, this much is clear: **I will never back down in the fight to safeguard our future from Donald Trump's agenda – and that includes winning back the Senate for Democrats in 2026. But when it comes to winning the tough races we'll need to win to flip the majority blue, early investments from folks like you – right now, today – are pivotal.**
**[So if you're able, I hope you'll pitch in any amount you can give today to fuel our shared fight to continue making progress for the American people, to hold the Trump administration accountable and to flip the Senate blue.](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/100685473/523566906/-739535254?amounts=5,25,50,100,250&refcode=jtk3051-fr-nd&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQNDQvMS84ODk1OSIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICJmMDJkODdmZC00OGQ3LWVmMTEtODhkMC0wMDIyNDgyYTlkOTIiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=VdpRExqmEXyORH1-e1H1i_7xlkfQlsW9yhAvLbKZ4XE=)**

name: Justice Democrats
subject: Our country’s descent into oligarchy
disclaimer_text: PAID FOR BY JUSTICE DEMOCRATS
body:
[1]Justice Democrats

Peter,

Donald Trump's inauguration today is a coronation of our country's descent
into oligarchy: billionaires and corporations spending hundreds of
millions of dollars lining the pockets of another billionaire—now
President—to usher in a presidency governed for and by the wealthy elite.

Billionaire Elon Musk funded Trump's campaign and now they are on track to
raise up to $250 million from Wall Street, Crypto, Big Tech, Big Pharma,
and others to fund his Inaugural Committee, which has no oversight or
regulation.

Crypto corporation Ripple is giving Trump $5 million, and stock trading
app Robinhood is giving $2 million, as did Uber and its CEO. Amazon's Jeff
Bezos, Meta's Mark Zuckerberg, Apple's Tim Cook, OpenAI's Sam Altman,
Coinbase, Kraken, PhRMA, and Goldman Sachs have all given $1 million each.

They're buying influence.

And they can expect a massive return on their investment. Crypto is
already seeing one with Trump promising an executive order handout to the
Wall Street-backed Big Tech corporations on Day 1. Banks and developers
are already winning out as Trump and Republicans put conditions on aid to
desperate Americans who have lost their homes and need immediate disaster
relief in California.

This administration will be a boon for the already wealthy few and will be
crushing to everyday people struggling to get by. Bipartisanship with the
Trump administration looks like doing the bidding of billionaires and
abandoning the needs of working-class communities.

Donald Trump's Day 1 plans include mass deportations, blank check permits
for fossil fuel corporations, lining the pockets of Crypto CEOs, and
cutting funding for public schools.

Democrats should be forcefully fighting back for the working class, but
they choose not to. The Democratic Party must reckon with its leadership
and consultant class’ coziness with these same Big Tech and Wall Street
CEOs, which has helped pave the way for Republicans to manipulate the
narrative.

Joe Biden’s warning against oligarchy was welcome, but it’s too little,
too late. We must show the American people what an alternative to
oligarchy looks like. This is a moment to usher in a new era in the
Democratic Party that is governed by and for the working class, not
billionaires and corporations.

Democrats must empower working-class people to lead the ranks within their
own party and embrace a vision that grabs the attention and imagination of
the American people – with an agenda that matches the scale of the crises
they’re facing.

Ending the stranglehold of billionaires over this party and government
once and for all must be a first step for that vision to be taken
seriously. With an incoming administration so blatantly for the
billionaire class, Democrats should be able to expose that corruption.

But Democrats can’t do that if they keep taking millions from the same
millionaires as Republicans—or different ones for that matter.

The Democratic Party must show voters what standing up to the wealthy on
behalf of the working class looks like — not on singular policies but
across the Party's entire agenda. Stop daring Republicans to vote against
right-wing immigration bills that betray our values, and instead dare them
to vote against raising the minimum wage, raising taxes on billionaires,
or passing Medicare for All. Dare Republicans to be economic populists,
not billionaire fascists.

Everyday people want to see a Party that they believe will fight for them
against the people who are keeping their costs high and their paychecks
low. Only by rejecting corporate influence, will working-class people
finally have the promise of a party that actually serves them.

If Democrats refuse to do so, then we will be ready to challenge them in
the 2026 primary elections next year. Justice Democrats is already
searching for working-class progressives to run in open seats or against
out-of-touch corporatists, but we need more resources to find and support
as many candidates as possible.

[ https://secure.actblue.com/donate/jd-em-20250120?refcode=em20250120-36345&amounts=10%2C27%2C50%2C100%2C250%2C500%2C1000&amount=5&refcode2=36345_1203867__8TqZ_&akid=36345%2E1203867%2E_8TqZ_ ]Please pitch in $5 or more to our Candidate Recruitment
Fund to help us raise $100,000 by February 1 so we can hire another staff
member to focus on this critical work.

[ https://secure.actblue.com/donate/jd-em-20250120?refcode=em20250120-36345&amounts=10%2C27%2C50%2C100%2C250%2C500%2C1000&amount=5&refcode2=36345_1203867__8TqZ_&akid=36345%2E1203867%2E_8TqZ_ ]Contribute $5

These next four years will be hard, but we will not be silent. As we
resist Trump once more, let’s give it our all to elect the working-class
leaders we deserve who will truly represent the people and change our
Party and country for the better.

In solidarity,

Justice Democrats

Contribute to Justice Democrats: https://secure.actblue.com/donate/justicedemocrats?refcode=em20250120-36345-footer&t=1002&refcode2=36345_1203867__8TqZ_&akid=36345%2E1203867%2E_8TqZ_

This email was sent to dpwillis67@gmail.com. Email is the most important way for us to reach you about opportunities to get involved. If you need to remove yourself from our email list, click here to unsubscribe: https://go.justicedemocrats.com/unsubscribe/unsubscribe/?t=1003&akid=36345%2E1203867%2E_8TqZ_

Lancaster PA 17603

Peter,

**On November 5th, the American People gave Donald Trump a resounding victory, and now he is moments away from being sworn in as the 47th President of the United States.**

For months, Conservatives poured over plays, opponents, and strategies. **Now, it 's game time.** _The American people are counting on us._

[**President Trump needs our support as he leads our nation back to greatness.**](http://links.tubervilleforalabama.com/u/click?_t=b8b4efbe79f64b579dfa7d8da685a7a9&_m=49b20f095c034f499c41dce04ed8f235&_e=hKEe_2CompnqN4J_kK5yWAt2WTo0goFs50VX58-5wsqLEl99QeL_eijtB2LnPi2ajMNw-JixdTU7ONoY2qFgCnVm1L6eydw6rmWBiOtzGF82T4ygt_MZnu4eHdhVtzc7AAz2dAI0aWaH87zvLUdsxS8dqrYEgimRDa4wRnY86s4Y6DSEgu_oarlCxNm0xYC6Q_Gr72CVD_nfGKNyRm3nx9hTjiKJ4gtcyEMrRt6cdnRzgTw9kG4mql_IhnCg4qjDxl1LQb_0OE_tew0cMRz7cTRQz53tPgIdFjLdZBzYtLs6uK19ZySXqU548_JQPOCF_2uLuqp8eBre7ihSGveFhw%3D%3D)

The past four years have been a mixture of witch hunts, hoaxes, and putting Americans last. It's time to put the will of the American people first.

[**Together, we can Make America Great Again, but Peter, we need your help.**](http://links.tubervilleforalabama.com/u/click?_t=b8b4efbe79f64b579dfa7d8da685a7a9&_m=49b20f095c034f499c41dce04ed8f235&_e=hKEe_2CompnqN4J_kK5yWAt2WTo0goFs50VX58-5wsqLEl99QeL_eijtB2LnPi2ajMNw-JixdTU7ONoY2qFgCnVm1L6eydw6rmWBiOtzGF82T4ygt_MZnu4eHdhVtzc7AAz2dAI0aWaH87zvLUdsxS8dqrYEgimRDa4wRnY86s4Y6DSEgu_oarlCxNm0xYC6JXQQbUxbC1DH3mrq4m7ccMPofiuA3nvWxzwxHCMH7iZukQc14KGsw6dVqt3wToxoavsjN_ux30RCevp-lhGX00PsP1hrVZuz10sCc8SVXBwBlbuASpgJhQZotyD_b_qQrTrilVsXCgHkDEcqpyWBwA%3D%3D)

| [ **DONATE $25 TO HELP MAKE AMERICA GREAT AGAIN!** ](http://links.tubervilleforalabama.com/u/click?_t=b8b4efbe79f64b579dfa7d8da685a7a9&_m=49b20f095c034f499c41dce04ed8f235&_e=hKEe_2CompnqN4J_kK5yWAt2WTo0goFs50VX58-5wsqLEl99QeL_eijtB2LnPi2ajMNw-JixdTU7ONoY2qFgCnVm1L6eydw6rmWBiOtzGF82T4ygt_MZnu4eHdhVtzc7AAz2dAI0aWaH87zvLUdsxS8dqrYEgimRDa4wRnY86s4Y6DSEgu_oarlCxNm0xYC6wCxiGiyQ748NrGcBBwnne3P0ytGrZiFqQ6xho9pKKtfNckDb1_5A9IqUJzBROGhYHowc9mpwYzs-dsLqjqzWb2jWnylkpsICiuavJ7vUwzHFw9wmScZd1Tpbvg9xCDd7ZBALzO9zadLM1_BjroO3gA%3D%3D)
---

The Democrats consistently outraise Republicans. We cannot afford to be on the back foot in 2025, especially since the Radical Left is more determined than ever to stop the MAGA Movement after going 0-3 with the White House, Senate, and House.

We must stand united behind President Trump and the America First Movement. [**Will you join my team and help us keep fighting the Radical Left Mob as we work to Make America Great Again?**](http://links.tubervilleforalabama.com/u/click?_t=b8b4efbe79f64b579dfa7d8da685a7a9&_m=49b20f095c034f499c41dce04ed8f235&_e=hKEe_2CompnqN4J_kK5yWAt2WTo0goFs50VX58-5wsqLEl99QeL_eijtB2LnPi2ajMNw-JixdTU7ONoY2qFgCnVm1L6eydw6rmWBiOtzGF82T4ygt_MZnu4eHdhVtzc7AAz2dAI0aWaH87zvLUdsxS8dqrYEgimRDa4wRnY86s4Y6DSEgu_oarlCxNm0xYC6PpGIKYWXWt_14hMDySW7vgGmCTBaUN-_5YTkNTJCO0v2aFlKqVevdn1bF7OxTgKuP1nnriMoiVF0UoYrG788cFcImSTh7L7RC2azHnTBygIQ17Xpblt8iF7ykAX3b7KgD243hEzX08E0pspM5oNgmg%3D%3D)

Thank you,
_Coach Tuberville_

[](http://links.tubervilleforalabama.com/u/click?_t=b8b4efbe79f64b579dfa7d8da685a7a9&_m=49b20f095c034f499c41dce04ed8f235&_e=hKEe_2CompnqN4J_kK5yWAt2WTo0goFs50VX58-5wsqLEl99QeL_eijtB2LnPi2ajMNw-JixdTU7ONoY2qFgCnVm1L6eydw6rmWBiOtzGF82T4ygt_MZnu4eHdhVtzc7AAz2dAI0aWaH87zvLUdsxS8dqrYEgimRDa4wRnY86s4Y6DSEgu_oarlCxNm0xYC6C_c0rP4ERmDxHZ2q14RtZ2MKTUf8Qbzho6WpXVXsyLHvwghdlPcj_g69WTeYo1W4GlmJc4mxbuYXQ0T1uJC7B5IbiEUr1fTJY0JNMWehTiKYNlAuiQW9-lUbQsPotNHW__EL9IIilXERrB2P2zPnVA%3D%3D)

Meet Coach Tuberville:

**Coach Tuberville was born in Camden, Arkansas, the third of three children of Olive and Charles Tuberville. College Football 's 2004 Coach of the Year and two-time SEC Coach of the Year, Tommy Tuberville is most often recognized for his success on the field. But it is the legacy of his father, a highly decorated WWII veteran and Purple Heart recipient, that motivates Coach Tuberville to give back to the brave men and women of the United States Armed Forces.

In 1989 Coach Tuberville met his future wife, Suzanne Fette, at the Sugar Bowl in New Orleans. They were married in 1991 and have two boys, Tucker and Troy, who continue to make their dad proud.

In his career as a College Football Coach, Tommy started a long and successful career at the University of Miami, starting as a graduate assistant and rising to the Hurricanes' defensive coordinator. He led the Canes to three AP national championships and coached future Hall of Famers Ray Lewis and Warren Sapp, as well as Dwayne "The Rock" Johnson.

In 1998, Coach Tuberville was announced as the next head coach for the Auburn Tigers. Tuberville's time at Auburn was a success by any measure, as the Tigers captured eight consecutive bowl appearances, including five New Year's Day bowl appearances, five SEC Western Division titles, one SEC Championship, and a 13-0 season in 2004, after which Coach Tuberville was once again named the AP's SEC Coach of the Year. For his efforts during the perfect 2004 season, Tuberville would be named Paul "Bear" Bryant Coach of the Year, Associated Press Coach of the Year, Walter Camp Coach of the Year, Sporting News Coach of the Year, and AFCA Coach of the Year, among others.

_In 2020, Coach Tuberville became Senator Tuberville, winning his election to represent all Alabamians in the United States Senate with the full support of President Trump. Since then, he's supported President Trump's America-First policies while fighting off the Radical Left. In his first term as Senator, Tommy was one of eight Republicans to vote against certifying the 2020 election, has been a strong pro-life advocate, and called out Democrats for being pro-crime.

Now, Coach Tuberville is running for re-election so he can continue representing Alabamians and Americans in the United States Senate. The Radical Left has him circled as a prime target to defeat in the 2026 Midterm Elections. Can he count on your support? _

** [**HELP COACH TUBERVILLE**](http://links.tubervilleforalabama.com/u/click?_t=b8b4efbe79f64b579dfa7d8da685a7a9&_m=49b20f095c034f499c41dce04ed8f235&_e=hKEe_2CompnqN4J_kK5yWAt2WTo0goFs50VX58-5wsqLEl99QeL_eijtB2LnPi2ajMNw-JixdTU7ONoY2qFgCnVm1L6eydw6rmWBiOtzGF82T4ygt_MZnu4eHdhVtzc7AAz2dAI0aWaH87zvLUdsxS8dqrYEgimRDa4wRnY86s4Y6DSEgu_oarlCxNm0xYC6jkJWwoqaaPdAUFOjSogdHfBePV-HJPzmxQKxwTF2vcMCXv5XMuKAkjLPoza8FP-dbU0BXS2wSTuIVcxLp023EJoTTgTPCV171oM1BRG5N__H7S56fqxUi_0noI50d-yxWhF2Rz8vrhbLOhg0oe4E7g%3D%3D)

**" Please do not think we think you are not important, but..."**
Two years ago, during my campaign for US Senate, I spent MLK Day in a mostly black town (92%) called Howardville. It's in Missouri's far southeast "[Bootheel](https://substack.com/redirect/2e79c3f9-8b5a-4e61-a123-0d6d2b087aa3?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)" region, the same area as Hayti Heights,[ another all black Missouri town whose struggles with clean water and sewage I wrote about a few weeks ago](https://substack.com/redirect/adf5f8c8-08b0-495a-826e-dc8d78204c52?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg).
In the spirit of Dr. King, my friend and Howardville resident, Vanessa Frazier, had gathered a group of other local residents together to figure out how they could protect their access to voting, which had recently taken a serious blow.
| [](https://substack.com/redirect/0f07ecb3-5341-4e01-af0c-09de1e419134?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)|
---|---|---
_In Howardville, Missouri, with Vanessa Frazier (center, black hat)_
Lucas's Substack is a reader-supported publication. To receive new posts and support my work, consider becoming a free or paid subscriber.
[Upgrade to paid](https://substack.com/redirect/2/eyJlIjoiaHR0cHM6Ly9sdWNhc2t1bmNlLnN1YnN0YWNrLmNvbS9zdWJzY3JpYmU_.OZti7Rpy_nG6zYc82-ov8FEOe1MwRqUcHPLkmzjEQ9w?&utm_medium=email&utm_source=subscribe-widget-preamble&utm_content=155256827)
You see, Vanessa, and everyone else who lived in Howardville, had received letters telling them that their polling places had been shut down.
The letters read: "Please do not think we think you are not important, but with only 263 registered voters in the precinct, **it is just not fiscally responsible to set up a polling place** ..."
A third of polling places were closed in New Madrid County, a county that stretches across 700 square miles.
| [](https://substack.com/redirect/8cda6186-ad6c-41ec-9865-3c3a2b2ad3ca?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)|
---|---|---
_Vanessa 's letter with some of her speaking notes_
It was another reminder that Dr. King's fight for the democratic _and_ economic empowerment of everyday people is far from over. With zero public input, Howardville residents were told they'd need to travel miles and miles just to vote -- as would hundreds of their other friends in nearby towns. At the meeting, many residents said that they or their neighbors don't drive and that the voting alternative for them was a joke: an option to vote by mail--_with a notary_ --which most people there said they had no access to and which added another layer of burden to voting.
People from all around the area showed up at Vanessa's Howardville MLK Day event to talk about how they were blindsided by this. Republican and Democrat. Black and white.

But mostly black. And overwhelmingly poor.
If you've ever seen the movie Braveheart, the leadership in Missouri followed the same strategy as the English king at the Battle of Falkirk, firing indiscriminately into an infantry melee to take out as many people as possible, regardless of their side:
[](https://substack.com/redirect/49fabd21-4572-4bcd-b07d-46666621e276?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)
_" Archers"
"I beg your pardon, Sire, but won't we hit our own troops?"
"Yes, but we'll hit theirs as well. We have reserves. Attack."_
The reasoning: the English king had more total troops than the Scots, so an equal depletion in numbers favored him because it increased the ratio in his favor. And besides, they were just poor commoners, so who cares?
Welcome to the strategy behind modern voter suppression.
A theme in Howardville that MLK day, a day we should have been celebrating equal rights and the legacy of Dr. King, was that the deck is still stacked against the Howardville community. Just like it's always been.
Here they were, in the 2020s, battling local leadership that thinks they are irrelevant, to try to retain access to _voting_. Howardville residents talked about growing up attending local segregated schools nearly _fifteen years_ after the landmark 1954 Brown vs. Board of Education ruling, because the local leadership of that era felt the same way. One of those old school buildings, now falling apart, is actually on the national register of historic places for being such a late segregated school.
| [](https://substack.com/redirect/2eb7f24f-0939-44f7-acd4-5e108734e285?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)|
---|---|---
_Howardville HS Historic Registry Sign_
The right is obsessed with DEI these days. Because it seems unfair. And it feels unfair.
You know what else feels unfair? Being born black in America.
Because Howardville and Hayti Heights aren't exceptions or outliers.
A lot of the time we think of racism as big acts or overt actions. And while these exist, perhaps the deepest impact of race in America is that it works a lot like a casino. The odds of you having a good outcome in any given situation are always worse if you are a minority, particularly if you are black. And these small disadvantages compound over time.
Healthcare is a good example. Black infants are[ more than twice as likely to die](https://substack.com/redirect/f7de3f99-31d4-4742-bcf6-4174cb10a0ba?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) as white infants. Black moms are[ more than twice as likely](https://substack.com/redirect/2e5c0003-6ced-4ac5-a73b-b288bbc7a932?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) to die as a result of childbirth than anyone else. They're also[ more than twice as likely to die](https://substack.com/redirect/8e5b4def-99a5-4fd8-ba4b-acd79a8dbf34?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) from breast cancer. Your doctor is[ less likely to respond](https://substack.com/redirect/43cec9c7-326a-4ae3-8723-f38543298428?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) to you. Your provider is[ less likely to listen to you](https://substack.com/redirect/27604f62-48d7-42b5-accc-266e26909534?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) or prescribe needed medication. And even[ less likely to believe](https://substack.com/redirect/3f833b36-d7f3-43e7-b0a3-7aad86add23d?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) what you tell them.
And forget about hiring. Just[ having a black name makes you significantly less likely](https://substack.com/redirect/c7b89111-dc07-4e7c-bbb0-cec04fabd7d7?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) to get a call on a job application. Even crazier, white men _with_ criminal records are[ more likely to get a job application call-back](https://substack.com/redirect/072b7d74-acb7-431d-a247-44d3476fce32?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) than black men _without_ criminal records. Does that sound fair?
That doesn't mean there aren't financially well-off black people. A lot of black Americans have worked very hard and used a lot of talent to overcome the odds. Many of them supported me on the campaign trail. Developers, business leaders, engineers, doctors, you name it. Giants in their fields. And a common theme in all of our discussions was that they just wanted the next generation to have fair odds.

Because they aren't examples that the system is equal. They have beaten the odds, just like some people who walk into a casino actually walk back out richer. It doesn't change the fact that, overall, the house wins.

Traffic stops are a common example.
When I was a young man I got pulled over quite a few times, for running stop signs, for speeding, and other things. Those tickets would have broken me if I had gotten them all, but despite being pulled over 10 times in a two year span, I only got one ticket.
In a multiyear study of more than 60 million traffic stops across 20 states, [Stanford University's Open Policing Project](https://substack.com/redirect/baad44e1-8be9-49e8-b9d1-0b02e937583b?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) found that black drivers are not only more likely to be stopped than white drivers, but that black and Hispanic motorists are also more likely to be ticketed and have their cars searched for less cause than whites. There have even been reports of local authorities [freely discussing](https://substack.com/redirect/93dfb32b-0c1f-4d9c-9cce-23082c55811d?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) and [making light of violence](https://substack.com/redirect/a36766df-cbeb-46bb-b736-c057d5101949?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) against black people, and actually[ advising recruits](https://substack.com/redirect/98877f1b-826e-4faf-8e56-964303f55ba6?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) to shoot young marijuana users if they're black.
On April 4, 2015, an unarmed black man named Walter Scott was shot in the back five times and killed in North Charleston by a police officer. Scott, who had been stopped for a burnt out tail-light but had spent time in jail for not paying child support, was fleeing from the officer when he was shot. I remember getting in an argument at the time with someone who said that a person gets what they deserve if they don't listen to the police.

I strongly disagreed. You see, when I was eighteen years old, my roommates and I lived on the fourth floor and watched a little too much David Letterman. He used to toss random things off of his roof and we started doing the same thing out of our window when no one was nearby. Bouncy balls, a broken chair, a watermelon... It was dumb. One day we went a little too far by stuffing a cereal box with toilet paper, lighting it on fire, and dropping it out into the snow. Someone called the cops and we were arrested.

The judge gave us each a "suspended imposition of sentence"- which meant that if we stayed out of trouble, the convictions would disappear.
Unfortunately, I was mischievous, and several months later, while exploring a subterranean steam tunnel, a police officer discovered me and yelled at me to stop. Not wanting to get caught trespassing, because it put a conviction on my record, I ran. I know, I'm not painting the most flattering picture of my young adulthood...

But I ran. And I got away. And it never crossed my mind that running from the police could have led to my death like it did for Walter Scott.
And yet, for my best friend growing up and high school cross country teammate, Benyam, he has to keep that in the back of his mind that _even just around his neighborhood_ could lead to his death. A couple of years ago, after a string of black men were shot while exercising, Benyam[ published his running-while-black checklist in the Washington Post](https://substack.com/redirect/89e60288-5241-48a2-8b70-02e4da8efb9f?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg):
_Is my beard too thick?_
_Is it too dark outside?_
_Am I dressed like a runner? God forbid I wear mesh b-ball shorts and a loose T-shirt in the suburbs of Dallas._
_Then, while on the run, I have to consider:_
_Is my rap music too loud? Can they hear it?_
_Does my cap, worn backward, send the wrong message?_
_Do I open up my stride now or wait until I get out of the neighborhood and into the open roads so I don 't appear to be running away from something or someone?_
_How many times have I looked over my shoulder on this block? Does that look suspicious?_
_Then there is scenario planning while running -- if this, then that:_
_-If you see a young white woman on same path, cross street immediately and try not to make eye contact twice._
_-If there is a white older woman/man on same path, wave/nod/smile, cross street casually._
_-If you 're running behind them and they don't hear you, cough loudly/clear throat, cross street._
_-If the sidewalk is narrow, always give way to walkers or bikers and step into the grass or street, even though you 've shattered your ankle twice, once on grass and once stepping into the street from the sidewalk._
_-Turn down volume on music when passing by neighbors. Need to be able to respond if they say something -- don't want to come off profiled as angry black man._
_-If a raised pickup truck approaches, immediately take to the sidewalk and get a good look at driver. High alert. Ready to dive into someone 's yard in case he swerves into me._
| [](https://substack.com/redirect/8a9f4445-4b15-4b98-97a3-4cb2c90a511e?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)|
---|---|---
_Benyam at my Marine Corps Officer Candidate School graduation, Quantico, Virginia_
I have never experienced what it's like to be black in this country. I only know what it was like to grow up white in a black area of Jefferson City, Missouri, and it's been eye opening for me.

| [](https://substack.com/redirect/a8a15110-7a36-45f4-b99d-3d9a66baed19?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)|
---|---|---
| [](https://substack.com/redirect/c8e06c36-e8fb-4ed2-9dd4-27310d4bec45?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)|
---|---|---
_On our back porch with the Mays ' kids and in front of our old house with Ms. Presberry, who moved to Jefferson City from Caruthersville in the Missouri bootheel_
And I don't just mean that city maintenance was neglected and that the neighborhood is now falling apart.
I mean the things that people don't say out loud or write about or pretend don't exist.
Things like people looking down on where I lived. Other kid's parents hesitating for them to come over. White kids calling my black neighbor kids the n-word consistently throughout my school years. High school classmates telling me that I lived in "porch monkey" land.
I remember being a 20-year-old Yale student back home over break and spending time on the other side of town with the fancy folks. Hanging with adults in high-up positions who oversaw hiring for their organizations and hearing them casually drop the n-word when referring to black people.
It's no surprise to me that people like that would interview white convicts for job interviews instead of black men without criminal records.
This isn't to say that everyone I interacted with held those views. The vast majority of people in my hometown were decent and cared about one another. But plenty were not, and that stacked the deck.

Being black now isn't really any different than it was then. The deck is still stacked, and it really isn't fair.

That's why this MLK Day, I'm hoping you will join me in celebrating some of the great black women leaders I met during my campaign who are fighting every day to get their communities the respect and voice they deserve.

Vanessa down in Howardville, who fought for her neighbors to have the right to vote. Mayor Catrina in Hayti Heights, who saved her city's water and sewage systems. Shalonda Webb, St. Louis County Councilwoman and the hardest working public servant I've ever met. And all the others across this great state who work hard every day, against the odds, to make life just a little bit better for everyone around them, no matter what color they are.
I hope they try even bigger things some day and even if the odds are against them, as they always have been, I'll be there for them and I hope you will be, too.
Lucas
