clean_body: ...it is just not fiscally responsible to set up a polling place." | | ---|---|--- | | | Forwarded this email? ? for more ? ...it is just not fiscally responsible to set up a polling place." | | | Jan 20 | | | | | | | | | | | | " Please do not think we think you are not important, but..." Two years ago, during my campaign for US Senate, I spent MLK Day in a mostly black town (92%) called Howardville. It's in Missouri's far southeast "" region, the same area as Hayti Heights,. In the spirit of Dr. King, my friend and Howardville resident, Vanessa Frazier, had gathered a group of other local residents together to figure out how they could protect their access to voting, which had recently taken a serious blow. | | ---|---|--- In Howardville, Missouri, with Vanessa Frazier (center, black hat) Lucas's Substack is a reader-supported publication. To receive new posts and support my work, consider becoming a free or paid subscriber. You see, Vanessa, and everyone else who lived in Howardville, had received letters telling them that their polling places had been shut down. The letters read: "Please do not think we think you are not important, but with only 263 registered voters in the precinct, it is just not fiscally responsible to set up a polling place ..." A third of polling places were closed in New Madrid County, a county that stretches across 700 square miles. | | ---|---|--- Vanessa 's letter with some of her speaking notes It was another reminder that Dr. King's fight for the democratic and economic empowerment of everyday people is far from over. With zero public input, Howardville residents were told they'd need to travel miles and miles just to vote -- as would hundreds of their other friends in nearby towns. At the meeting, many residents said that they or their neighbors don't drive and that the voting alternative for them was a joke: an option to vote by mail--with a notary --which most people there said they had no access to and which added another layer of burden to voting. People from all around the area showed up at Vanessa's Howardville MLK Day event to talk about how they were blindsided by this. Republican and Democrat. Black and white. But mostly black. And overwhelmingly poor. If you've ever seen the movie Braveheart, the leadership in Missouri followed the same strategy as the English king at the Battle of Falkirk, firing indiscriminately into an infantry melee to take out as many people as possible, regardless of their side: " Archers" "I beg your pardon, Sire, but won't we hit our own troops?" "Yes, but we'll hit theirs as well. We have reserves. Attack." The reasoning: the English king had more total troops than the Scots, so an equal depletion in numbers favored him because it increased the ratio in his favor. And besides, they were just poor commoners, so who cares? Welcome to the strategy behind modern voter suppression. A theme in Howardville that MLK day, a day we should have been celebrating equal rights and the legacy of Dr. King, was that the deck is still stacked against the Howardville community. Just like it's always been. Here they were, in the 2020s, battling local leadership that thinks they are irrelevant, to try to retain access to voting. Howardville residents talked about growing up attending local segregated schools nearly fifteen years after the landmark 1954 Brown vs. Board of Education ruling, because the local leadership of that era felt the same way. One of those old school buildings, now falling apart, is actually on the national register of historic places for being such a late segregated school. | | ---|---|--- Howardville HS Historic Registry Sign The right is obsessed with DEI these days. Because it seems unfair. And it feels unfair. You know what else feels unfair? Being born black in America. Because Howardville and Hayti Heights aren't exceptions or outliers. A lot of the time we think of racism as big acts or overt actions. And while these exist, perhaps the deepest impact of race in America is that it works a lot like a casino. The odds of you having a good outcome in any given situation are always worse if you are a minority, particularly if you are black. And these small disadvantages compound over time. Healthcare is a good example. Black infants are as white infants. Black moms are to die as a result of childbirth than anyone else. They're also from breast cancer. Your doctor is to you. Your provider is or prescribe needed medication. And even what you tell them. And forget about hiring. Just to get a call on a job application. Even crazier, white men with criminal records are than black men without criminal records. Does that sound fair? That doesn't mean there aren't financially well-off black people. A lot of black Americans have worked very hard and used a lot of talent to overcome the odds. Many of them supported me on the campaign trail. Developers, business leaders, engineers, doctors, you name it. Giants in their fields. And a common theme in all of our discussions was that they just wanted the next generation to have fair odds. Because they aren't examples that the system is equal. They have beaten the odds, just like some people who walk into a casino actually walk back out richer. It doesn't change the fact that, overall, the house wins. Traffic stops are a common example. When I was a young man I got pulled over quite a few times, for running stop signs, for speeding, and other things. Those tickets would have broken me if I had gotten them all, but despite being pulled over 10 times in a two year span, I only got one ticket. In a multiyear study of more than 60 million traffic stops across 20 states, found that black drivers are not only more likely to be stopped than white drivers, but that black and Hispanic motorists are also more likely to be ticketed and have their cars searched for less cause than whites. There have even been reports of local authorities and against black people, and actually to shoot young marijuana users if they're black. On April 4, 2015, an unarmed black man named Walter Scott was shot in the back five times and killed in North Charleston by a police officer. Scott, who had been stopped for a burnt out tail-light but had spent time in jail for not paying child support, was fleeing from the officer when he was shot. I remember getting in an argument at the time with someone who said that a person gets what they deserve if they don't listen to the police. I strongly disagreed. You see, when I was eighteen years old, my roommates and I lived on the fourth floor and watched a little too much David Letterman. He used to toss random things off of his roof and we started doing the same thing out of our window when no one was nearby. Bouncy balls, a broken chair, a watermelon... It was dumb. One day we went a little too far by stuffing a cereal box with toilet paper, lighting it on fire, and dropping it out into the snow. Someone called the cops and we were arrested. The judge gave us each a "suspended imposition of sentence"- which meant that if we stayed out of trouble, the convictions would disappear. Unfortunately, I was mischievous, and several months later, while exploring a subterranean steam tunnel, a police officer discovered me and yelled at me to stop. Not wanting to get caught trespassing, because it put a conviction on my record, I ran. I know, I'm not painting the most flattering picture of my young adulthood... But I ran. And I got away. And it never crossed my mind that running from the police could have led to my death like it did for Walter Scott. And yet, for my best friend growing up and high school cross country teammate, Benyam, he has to keep that in the back of his mind that even just around his neighborhood could lead to his death. A couple of years ago, after a string of black men were shot while exercising, Benyam: Is my beard too thick? Is it too dark outside? Am I dressed like a runner? God forbid I wear mesh b-ball shorts and a loose T-shirt in the suburbs of Dallas. Then, while on the run, I have to consider: Is my rap music too loud? Can they hear it? Does my cap, worn backward, send the wrong message? Do I open up my stride now or wait until I get out of the neighborhood and into the open roads so I don 't appear to be running away from something or someone? How many times have I looked over my shoulder on this block? Does that look suspicious? Then there is scenario planning while running -- if this, then that: -If you see a young white woman on same path, cross street immediately and try not to make eye contact twice. -If there is a white older woman/man on same path, wave/nod/smile, cross street casually. -If you 're running behind them and they don't hear you, cough loudly/clear throat, cross street. -If the sidewalk is narrow, always give way to walkers or bikers and step into the grass or street, even though you 've shattered your ankle twice, once on grass and once stepping into the street from the sidewalk. -Turn down volume on music when passing by neighbors. Need to be able to respond if they say something -- don't want to come off profiled as angry black man. -If a raised pickup truck approaches, immediately take to the sidewalk and get a good look at driver. High alert. Ready to dive into someone 's yard in case he swerves into me. | | ---|---|--- Benyam at my Marine Corps Officer Candidate School graduation, Quantico, Virginia I have never experienced what it's like to be black in this country. I only know what it was like to grow up white in a black area of Jefferson City, Missouri, and it's been eye opening for me. | | ---|---|--- | | ---|---|--- On our back porch with the Mays ' kids and in front of our old house with Ms. Presberry, who moved to Jefferson City from Caruthersville in the Missouri bootheel And I don't just mean that city maintenance was neglected and that the neighborhood is now falling apart. I mean the things that people don't say out loud or write about or pretend don't exist. Things like people looking down on where I lived. Other kid's parents hesitating for them to come over. White kids calling my black neighbor kids the n-word consistently throughout my school years. High school classmates telling me that I lived in "porch monkey" land. I remember being a 20-year-old Yale student back home over break and spending time on the other side of town with the fancy folks. Hanging with adults in high-up positions who oversaw hiring for their organizations and hearing them casually drop the n-word when referring to black people. It's no surprise to me that people like that would interview white convicts for job interviews instead of black men without criminal records. This isn't to say that everyone I interacted with held those views. The vast majority of people in my hometown were decent and cared about one another. But plenty were not, and that stacked the deck. Being black now isn't really any different than it was then. The deck is still stacked, and it really isn't fair. That's why this MLK Day, I'm hoping you will join me in celebrating some of the great black women leaders I met during my campaign who are fighting every day to get their communities the respect and voice they deserve. Vanessa down in Howardville, who fought for her neighbors to have the right to vote. Mayor Catrina in Hayti Heights, who saved her city's water and sewage systems. Shalonda Webb, St. Louis County Councilwoman and the hardest working public servant I've ever met. And all the others across this great state who work hard every day, against the odds, to make life just a little bit better for everyone around them, no matter what color they are. I hope they try even bigger things some day and even if the odds are against them, as they always have been, I'll be there for them and I hope you will be, too. body:
Let’s commit ourselves to unite together to create a brighter, more equitable future.

You received this message from Lisa Blunt Rochester because you've previously taken action and supported Democrats like Lisa. If you would like to get less email, **[click here.](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/100686698/523570381/1079316600?source=LOC_EM_NOFR_2025.01.20_MLKInauguration_B1_F1_S1_C1_30OP_N-T1&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMTIvMS85Mjg3NiIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICJhODhmN2VjYy00YmQ3LWVmMTEtODhkMC0wMDIyNDgyYTlkOTIiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=qfjq5h90-eIS6NoRGF1GysMypV3OJAFtlM5scJQFR4Q=&emci=ce5e2b53-7ccf-ef11-88d0-0022482a9d92&emdi=a88f7ecc-4bd7-ef11-88d0-0022482a9d92&ceid=11913588)** To unsubscribe, **[click here.](https://secure.ngpvan.com/p/_6DeEby01kOvxTWj8qzW6A2?unsubscribedata=MPiLc621i0e5nluwZiJkiM3Y9GyJEJQrS5h%2FiTje0P0bc7GKHAuHEULTH1s4CoXflkldCaZsCJ2gQH6SvgNs9%2FxbiEc8FoerAXevqx1CkUTKFKg22p1qCebo3MyNENO8WIfadOaa6VKSFL8kDTurDSGLa94CIxd109Wj%2B81jr%2BofO8%2BcUaQewjMW9YViGSggm5k3%2FTrhDqA5wDVoAcQTcJtv7XIDbnFbaxvOv8QhReU1EDJwfwLdBi%2BzooJkVwegS9jv379STsvo4u%2FUYwThB%2B7%2FixRQbHMaQaBRCN6ZZo5ZRSb6WqUoDvTxMmQYLy%2FxQu169411gILveTRvn9wTmOJDywY%2Bz9eHOgK4ld1yWSYxDTFpTe3RshmGsxhzAJ02Yduxqmk2mETGLgL3lMOJFGzX%2FgPKsD01f8A0FDF8IwdoysfoLcD3qtiLo15fG3HI)**

Folks, on this Dr. Martin Luther King Jr. Day, we remind ourselves about his mission of service, justice, and equality. Dr. King was not just a champion for civil rights, but an inspiration to all of us who fight to make America and the world a better place. His voice inspired a movement and his words stick with us as guiding principles in life. Today, we will watch Donald Trump – someone who divided us and singled out Americans for who they are and what they believe – be inaugurated as the President of the United States once again. And for many people, they are nervous and the future is uncertain. One of my favorite quotes of Dr. King's is, **"The time is always right to do what is right."** Even today, we all should take the time to think about what our next steps will be in our ongoing mission to strive towards justice and equality for all. In honor of Dr. King's service he gave to our country, I'm encouraging each of you to give back to your community in any way possible and to do what is right. Whether it's volunteering at a food bank or helping a neighbor with a chore, I've always found that good deeds are free but repay you in the best ways possible. In the weeks, months and years to come, let’s commit ourselves to unite together to create a brighter, more equitable future for all Americans. Thank you for reading, Lisa Blunt Rochester
* * *
| [](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/100686700/523570383/631174349?refcode=LOC_LBR_EM_FR_2024_Email-Footer&source=LOC_EM_NOFR_2025.01.20_MLKInauguration_B1_F1_S1_C1_30OP_N-T1&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMTIvMS85Mjg3NiIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICJhODhmN2VjYy00YmQ3LWVmMTEtODhkMC0wMDIyNDgyYTlkOTIiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=qfjq5h90-eIS6NoRGF1GysMypV3OJAFtlM5scJQFR4Q=) I officially became the first woman and person of color to **_ever_** represent Delaware in the Senate. But even though our campaign is over, the work has just begun.

clean_body: Let’s commit ourselves to unite together to create a brighter, more equitable future. You received this message from Lisa Blunt Rochester because you've previously taken action and supported Democrats like Lisa. If you would like to get less email, To unsubscribe, | | = Folks, on this Dr. Martin Luther King Jr. Day, we remind ourselves about his mission of service, justice, and equality. Dr. King was not just a champion for civil rights, but an inspiration to all of us who fight to make America and the world a better place. His voice inspired a movement and his words stick with us as guiding principles in life. Today, we will watch Donald Trump – someone who divided us and singled out Americans for who they are and what they believe – be inaugurated as the President of the United States once again. And for many people, they are nervous and the future is uncertain. One of my favorite quotes of Dr. King's is, "The time is always right to do what is right." Even today, we all should take the time to think about what our next steps will be in our ongoing mission to strive towards justice and equality for all. In honor of Dr. King's service he gave to our country, I'm encouraging each of you to give back to your community in any way possible and to do what is right. Whether it's volunteering at a food bank or helping a neighbor with a chore, I've always found that good deeds are free but repay you in the best ways possible. In the weeks, months and years to come, let’s commit ourselves to unite together to create a brighter, more equitable future for all Americans. Thank you for reading, Lisa Blunt Rochester | = I officially became the first woman and person of color to ever represent Delaware in the Senate. But even though our campaign is over, the work has just begun. Will you chip in any amount today to help Bright Hope take center stage in the Senate? | = = Lisa Blunt Rochester for Senate PO BOX 9767 Wilmington, DE 19809 United States | mailto:info@lisabluntrochester.com Paid for by Lisa Blunt Rochester for Senate Emails like these are an important aspect of our strategy to keep in touch with our supporters. name: Indiana Democratic Party
subject: A day of contrast
disclaimer_text: Paid for by the Indiana Democratic Party, www
body:
|
---
Peter, On this Inauguration Day, we must not forget the life and legacy of Dr. Martin Luther King Jr., who our country honors on this day. As we enter into the next four years, King’s life and legacy stands in stark contrast to the division and negativity within our politics today. We should honor Dr. King’s life by fighting everyday for an America where people are judged by the content of their character, not by the color of their skin or where they were born. **[If you’re ready to fight back right here in Indiana — we need your help. Consider a donation today to our rebuild fund to support our party and push back on the division in Washington.](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/100686756/523570624/1764064610?refcode=250120contrast&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMDMvMS82NzYzNSIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICI0MDZiNThlYy00YmQ3LWVmMTEtODhkMC0wMDIyNDgyYTlkOTIiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogIkRQV0lMTElTNjdAR01BSUwuQ09NIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=ouhh566LfBJfvzgQMPzNAaCKCh9O7i6so7uqgTyaKsY=)** Today begins a new era in our country, and together we can be part of the team fighting for working families, not corporate handouts here in Indiana. **[Success in 2026 and 2028 counts on us coming together now. name: JAG
subject: In the Victimhood Olympics, Everyone Loses
body:
When I was born in India (where my parents were working in the Peace Corps), there was a horrific and tragic practice of “doctors” mutilating children.

|
---
| | [](http://e.americanbriefing.com/rd/9z2zdsvde0qdvae07l961jsstoi9hftn1me5mndti80_rp22sh2s8i6epj7c5g61or7c1h2bs) Friend,

When I was born in India (where my parents were working in the Peace Corps), there was a horrific and tragic practice of “doctors” mutilating children. Sometimes, hands or legs were chopped off; sometimes, eyes gouged out.

“Why???” you gasp in horror. Sadism? Some demented selfishness? To the contrary. The answer is altruism: these barbaric butchers were trying to make the children more pitiful (and more profitable) beggars, playing on the sympathies for such victims as to merit a few more rupees cast their way.

You shake your head: such a thing couldn’t happen here in modern America, you reassure yourself. Here, young people strive to be rewarded for their merit, not their misfortunes, right? For their achievements, not their ailments. For their talents, not their tales of woe.

If you still believe that you’re living in the wrong century. And you need to wake up.

Go to any university campus or public school. There, you’ll find DEI activist teachers doing the work of the child butchers – but instead of physically mutilating the young, they mutilate their minds by inculcating them with narratives of victimhood.

Their subjects aren’t waving deformed limbs but waving banners of oppression. And unlike the butchered beggars of India, rather than begging, they demand. Reparations. Special privileges. Affirmative action. Safe spaces.

But the real aim of this modern mutilation – for both the perpetrators and its victims – is power.

Ayn Rand pointed out the similarities between these ancient barbaric practices and the way in which modern education deforms the mind and crushes the spirit. She did it by paraphrasing her favorite author, Victor Hugo:
_“And what did they make of these children? Monsters.
Why monsters? To rule.”_
Justice met Need, and the natural offshoot was Victimhood.

Victimhood’s unholy union with Envy is what’s driving many of the problems we face today.

The victim mentality turns people’s grief into grievances, telling them ever so seductively, _“The reason you don’t have what you want or need is because you’re a victim of..._

_A rigged system
Systemic racism
Sexism and misogyny_
... the list goes on and on.

This victim mentality spreads like a Socially Transmitted Disease, eating away at that which is best within us: optimism, reason, self-esteem, benevolence, and – most importantly – gratitude.

By pitting black and white, men and women, rich and poor, against each other in a never-ending victim-oppressor dance, Victimhood replaces one’s identity as a strong, capable individual with the label _“Victim.”_ It breeds a sense of entitlement that breaks the human spirit.

At The Atlas Society, we’re committed to inoculating young minds against this corrosive mentality by using cutting-edge media to communicate Ayn Rand’s ideals of reason, achievement, personal responsibility, and liberty to the next generation.

[_Today, I’m asking you to join me in this pursuit. When you donate $35, $50, $100, or more, you are playing an integral role in saving young people around the world from the grip of Victimhood. Click to donate and receive an exclusive FREE download of our graphic novel of _Anthem_ with your gift of $35 or more! >>_](http://e.americanbriefing.com/rd/9z2zgeb7ns067rtjah7p2itncud43q5ucsp00palsf8_rp22sh2s8i6epj7c5g61or7c1h2bs)
[](http://e.americanbriefing.com/rd/9z2z831hfh695k7f2kur7g590fb4rr0ffs9mvm0vn20_rp22sh2s8i6epj7c5g61or7c1h2bs)
As the CEO of The Atlas Society, I know that _it’s not easy to achieve your dreams_. I worked hard to get where I am today, and I am _grateful_ for the men and women who helped me be _victorious_ in achieving my goals.

I’m particularly grateful to Ayn Rand, whose philosophy gave me the weapons to fight back against envy, victimhood, and resentment. I’m committed to sharing these resources with the rising generation of young people in ways that are engaging, accessible, and inspiring.

Whether it’s through our action-packed animated book trailers, our thought-provoking Draw My Life videos, or our captivating graphic novels, The Atlas Society is using art to engage young minds with the message of a free society where everyone has the opportunity to be victorious.

Ayn Rand herself called art _“the indispensable medium for the communication of a moral ideal.”_ You can help us replace the victim mentality with Ayn Rand’s vision of self-determination and achievement by donating today.
[Donate to Fight the Victim Mentality >>](http://e.americanbriefing.com/rd/9z2z047civvn20onn6qd4u4ik347gfk3756btm1qrv8_rp22sh2s8i6epj7c5g61or7c1h2bs)
By introducing young people to the ideas of Ayn Rand, we are providing them with an opportunity to build a victim-proof mindset that prizes TRUTH above all else.

Victimhood tells young people, **_“You’ll never be a _victor_ , but you can _always_ be a victim.”_**

**Ayn Rand offers a radically different message that empowers them to reach their fullest potential** by embracing reason, achievement, benevolence, and ethical self-interest as the foundation for liberty, happiness, and a flourishing society.

Ed Crane, Founder of the CATO Institute, deemed Ayn Rand the _“all-time greatest recruiter for the liberty movement”_ because her novels provide a captivating alternative to the siren song of collective victimhood.

[_Donate $35 or more to download an exclusive copy of our graphic novel adaptation of _Anthem_ and see for yourself how we’re challenging the victim mentality through the works of Ayn Rand. Click now to make your tax-deductible investment in a freer, more empowering future. >>_](http://e.americanbriefing.com/rd/9z2zusa284piq51dp6lhuo61kurd86l3ef07am4pec0_rp22sh2s8i6epj7c5g61or7c1h2bs)
[Donate $10](http://e.americanbriefing.com/rd/9z2z4iccq59aaa03f4sck906ob21jnit7osi233pr18_rp22sh2s8i6epj7c5g61or7c1h2bs)

[Donate $35](http://e.americanbriefing.com/rd/9z2zluvl9aso7kihlmq4nji7fqresa083aakk24kdmo_rp22sh2s8i6epj7c5g61or7c1h2bs)

[Donate $50](http://e.americanbriefing.com/rd/9z2zbmuglap3lq18n68233ks0mvt95d50l29uabmhsg_rp22sh2s8i6epj7c5g61or7c1h2bs)

[Donate $100](http://e.americanbriefing.com/rd/9z2zd231m83csqki48pq7gbqiahlqspajab5q5dloig_rp22sh2s8i6epj7c5g61or7c1h2bs)

[Donate $250](http://e.americanbriefing.com/rd/9z2zcgbapjad4m24ccfmub5g1s35imctuaj8pp7llr0_rp22sh2s8i6epj7c5g61or7c1h2bs)

[Donate $500](http://e.americanbriefing.com/rd/9z2zpcphgp4qb6ashfoachdvju9psp73os4ggjosdh8_rp22sh2s8i6epj7c5g61or7c1h2bs)

[Donate $1,000](http://e.americanbriefing.com/rd/9z2z851rpdibskavrviam4k7vhtt5i6pboi76toipv0_rp22sh2s8i6epj7c5g61or7c1h2bs)

[Donate Any Other Amount](http://e.americanbriefing.com/rd/9z2zltesphelehct1vg9hcaqe92iag0fvitvdg4bu6g_rp22sh2s8i6epj7c5g61or7c1h2bs)
Ayn Rand’s novels stand as a testament to the human spirit and a celebration of the individual’s power to break through the fog of Victimhood, Envy, and Resentment and instead realize a future marked by achievement, pride, and gratitude.

Along with my colleagues at The Atlas Society, I’m on a mission to reach 1 BILLION young people with Ayn Rand’s work. _I hope you’ll join me!_

Thank you in advance for your support,

Jennifer Anju Grossman (JAG)
CEO
The Atlas Society

_The Atlas Society is a 501(c)(3) Not For Profit Organization. All donations are tax-deductible._
---
| |
---
---

American Briefing
---
_PO Box 2221 Fort Collins, CO 80522_
_[**unsubscribe**](http://e.americanbriefing.com/rd/9z2zrphm5mb6q2nqfp2l7gvee5ou86b42ebahveg1m8_rp22sh2s8i6epj7c5g61or7c1h238h5e8he485he5o75pt7kuo72ttle5pq8ddl69qbcd1jm8p79d9j6iprect66uqrf9bjmpqn4djm69qb4d9nkvijbo9getqb3cdkmppmeo0n6mq39cdjkorrbcp4mni6cp70cpjm9oh5u)_ | _[manage data preferences](http://e.americanbriefing.com/rd/9z2z1lslsn0l5kdsl1gl1t01pc6fn5ppvh2uii72d48_rp22sh2s8i6epj7c5g61or7c1h2bs)_

name: Byron Donalds
subject: I’m not easily scared
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Byron Donalds for Congress
body:
But there’s one thing that keeps me up at night

| | | | [](https://post.spmailtechnol.com/f/a/GhsJNuR6Cqe4ioJTY9Rjnw~~/AARclAA~/RgRpcRLgP0RVaHR0cHM6Ly9zZWN1cmUud2lucmVkLmNvbS9ieXJvbmRvbmFsZHMvZG9uLXByc3AtZW1haWwtYmlvLXYyLXBvcC8_dXRtX3RjPVJPTi0yMDI1MDEyMFcDc3BjQgpnfxt7jmfpxCQdUhRkcHdpbGxpczY3QGdtYWlsLmNvbVgEAAAAAA~~)

[](https://post.spmailtechnol.com/f/a/GhsJNuR6Cqe4ioJTY9Rjnw~~/AARclAA~/RgRpcRLgP0RVaHR0cHM6Ly9zZWN1cmUud2lucmVkLmNvbS9ieXJvbmRvbmFsZHMvZG9uLXByc3AtZW1haWwtYmlvLXYyLXBvcC8_dXRtX3RjPVJPTi0yMDI1MDEyMFcDc3BjQgpnfxt7jmfpxCQdUhRkcHdpbGxpczY3QGdtYWlsLmNvbVgEAAAAAA~~)
|
---
This is President Trump’s strongest supporter in the House, Byron Donalds.
I grew up in Brooklyn, New York, raised by a single mother who sacrificed everything for me and my siblings.
I’ve had _**thick skin**_ from a young age - so if it’s a radical-left news anchor trying to gaslight me for being an unapologetic Black MAGA Patriot, or House Democrats going low with pathetic slurs, I’ll never take my foot off the gas until the fight is won.
I take that to heart even today as I continue the fight for the MAGA Movement, _**and I do it ALL for you**_.
Look, I’m not easily frightened, **but Democrats retaking the House Majority keeps me up at night.**
They’ve already begun building a war chest to sweep-up vulnerable Seats that they lost by a fraction of a percent, and MAGA Congressmembers are the **FIRST** on their target list.
They know if we lose our Majority - President Trump’s America First Agenda WILL come to a screeching halt.
I’m fighting for the _MAGA House Majority_ every single day, but the only way I can continue my efforts is through the support of folks just like you.
With the attacks from the radical-left, Fake News, and Democrats ratcheting up, I’ve never needed your support more.
_**[So I’m humbly asking, can you chip in today to help me keep Congress Red?](https://post.spmailtechnol.com/f/a/GhsJNuR6Cqe4ioJTY9Rjnw~~/AARclAA~/RgRpcRLgP0RVaHR0cHM6Ly9zZWN1cmUud2lucmVkLmNvbS9ieXJvbmRvbmFsZHMvZG9uLXByc3AtZW1haWwtYmlvLXYyLXBvcC8_dXRtX3RjPVJPTi0yMDI1MDEyMFcDc3BjQgpnfxt7jmfpxCQdUhRkcHdpbGxpczY3QGdtYWlsLmNvbVgEAAAAAA~~)**_ _****__****_

|
---

Peter, today was supposed to be a great day. If things had gone differently, we could have watched Kamala Harris be sworn in as our nation’s first Black woman president — on Dr. Martin Luther King Jr. Day. Instead, we’re about to watch racist felon Donald Trump be inaugurated as extremist-in-chief for a second time — and frankly, we’re **_terrified_** about what the future holds. One thing’s for sure: **Our fight to elect proven Democratic leaders up and down the ballot in Georgia has never been more important than it is right now.** Our statewide electeds will be the first line of defense against whatever Trump throws our way in the next four years — and it’s up to all of us to prepare for the inevitable. [We need your urgent support to show Trump that we’re not backing down here in the Peach State. “We are confronted with the fierce urgency of now” - Martin Luther King, Jr. | | | [](https://click.actionnetwork.org/ss/c/u001.X6XUC7u8ppFLNtMO_55SZlNseb7gEYIOhm_EWnhzEOZKmTtnV3k2h4V2x_O3H7rdW2az5rgktBwzxQ8Ml9Wgkmv85qW-UsBonL4KRa0bcNqdICU6T3NBDdNIZG7w7wEWY8ZY4ecrIzOV4FgKjIere7SswuTlhu3hWut4OZJakpw9JbNrX3f7msVY0UOorDI3PquIk2yEMxHunEjtw1KoM5tTo6YTlsHcSFG6yCIG-xGRlxTMOyFxAvNAN3GcBFXoS5s7jHJIbBnOj0QwndG1aKCwd7W60qWOreNmpUvKiArRaQwXFWNsfvo-coJipsrs6StPilUooY_kbcswUdbeC7uLzqly7Z3UzKSopfEAPDc/4db/3DzBYfsuQ4GFaaZMSaWUlQ/h0/h001.2CZLURg8kOYARy7nmSJPGIEkEBHA0WXe-0t03PXGils)
---
Friend, Today, we are honoring the radical legacy of Dr. Martin Luther King, Jr. As we mark the 30th anniversary of MLK Day, a new presidential administration will be sworn in after spreading anti-Black hate and threatening to further take away our hard-fought rights. We must continue carrying forward the legacy of the civil rights movement to strengthen our rights and demand racial and economic justice for all. Dr. Martin Luther King, Jr. had a vision for a “Beloved Community,” a world where no one goes hungry, people have what they need to survive and thrive, and love and connection triumphs over hate, fear, and bigotry. Although that world feels far away, we are moving toward it each day in our communities, including when we join with our neighbors in grassroots groups pushing for change. In my community, the group Oakland Forward is growing a strong and supportive network of activists and advocates who are shaping public policy like paid family and medical leave while confronting the ongoing challenges of systemic racism, economic inequality, and social injustice. **Community-centered and led by and for Black and brown residents, Oakland Forward is building long-term power for a more just society like MLK's "Beloved Community" while serving marginalized people who are in need now.** [](https://click.actionnetwork.org/ss/c/u001.iIBo0N5fSFzEFL40Tn-vtZzzfTY2NadQ7R1AXSz5TewzfNxSliP9deNsn4P04ZlY7F1Oq4WWhoKaRveNa5hGnNsl6nHO1VPxCxTkQRBnQYu9LIGt5XD2qoAM0ZQgiq6hKCReLtEtK_LysAl1QmtEkWgdVoIuV4uJD0RQQJI2CdQ_Nip-HdAZuv-7waCODVnUYab-KsRraJIgy_-UGY6XU-t4M7ypYXHFqRqsLdHnU-jm_DhpEY3mJC2Q8iQjRhARRXvrz-Hn7-wKpNMsL87-5vVoUBWbufAsm8JMSmVXaQM/4db/3DzBYfsuQ4GFaaZMSaWUlQ/h1/h001.X5tkNJ0GQwICZ6CQUAUYcllGhxXlPfh8mDpDAFmNaRM) [**Will you donate this MLK Day to support Oakland Forward’s ongoing work for systemic change and equity for all?**](https://click.actionnetwork.org/ss/c/u001.iIBo0N5fSFzEFL40Tn-vtZzzfTY2NadQ7R1AXSz5TewzfNxSliP9deNsn4P04ZlY7F1Oq4WWhoKaRveNa5hGnNsl6nHO1VPxCxTkQRBnQYvDwQrl0XNOLtT-rczplZhvfO3gVtKNc_MtkRd14mBNYNBxyUZknjaHsDVf95vTBmFjCKPHQqXlQPIla62QRCTMuzFjMyNlMew8g82JPm48Jy_nEhjvsAIzT69UBMPkOvg14vc9tkopWXtJ7XC_hrGIfNG9RwwCBzPj0ItOYBaTA8yCZXm4Nn0kwjPBAlxpJS8/4db/3DzBYfsuQ4GFaaZMSaWUlQ/h2/h001.HKM9xp4Aui9MlCEKfIGnlMHdqNRh-sE4XqRb63EYxig) | [DONATE NOW](https://click.actionnetwork.org/ss/c/u001.iIBo0N5fSFzEFL40Tn-vtZzzfTY2NadQ7R1AXSz5TewzfNxSliP9deNsn4P04ZlY7F1Oq4WWhoKaRveNa5hGnNsl6nHO1VPxCxTkQRBnQYs2_M7QXqDHKOf6XNzh-iKPSkO1rrTDPpxFDzhlHTk6bbBh5bjKoZId-YL-7v7MwOWL0OuEGuBZpz6_ix2l5ZbPuQ7yGbLuMiuTCgpELLNtsZ9l6QSoHZsF8_6fZx-7qgUxVtmmxz7yICGehIirDbk8LEAitCv1ckrxP3L4x3A-MJjO39yWtd6FN53BXcCBG-U/4db/3DzBYfsuQ4GFaaZMSaWUlQ/h3/h001.Toa2uq5kFpg0rIjRAcYjzLDUNfLa_xfx4cMVcYRbMhI)
---
Thank you. As Dr. King wrote: _“Injustice anywhere is a threat to justice everywhere. We are caught in an inescapable network of mutuality, tied in a single garment of destiny. Whatever affects one directly, affects all indirectly.”_
As we move into a phase of emboldened hatred, it’s critical we stand together until we are all free. Please know you have an ally and a partner in me. Together, let’s carry on the legacy of MLK and the everyday people who made up the civil rights movement—people who were dreaming big in hostile times and who made our political institutions listen.
In love and solidarity,
Rashida
\---------- Forwarded message ---------
From: **Rashida Tlaib******
Date: Sun, Jan 19, 2025
Subject: Honoring MLK’s legacy for racial and economic justice
To: dpwillis67@gmail.com
Friend,
Tomorrow marks the 30th anniversary of Martin Luther King, Jr. Day. One way we can honor Dr. King is by supporting the Black-led groups carrying on his legacy for liberation and making change happen in our communities.
As the civil rights movement showed us, people in Congress and the White House didn’t just wake up one day and decide to pass transformative policies like the _Civil Rights Act_. Transformative change doesn’t come from the government, it comes from everyday people pushing for our rights in many ways at once: through movements in the streets, the courts, campuses, and more.
**I want to lift up Oakland Forward, a grassroots organization in my district that’s serving and empowering Black and brown communities and working for a more equitable society where people can live free from discrimination and oppression, akin to Dr. Martin Luther King, Jr.’s vision of a Beloved Community.**
[](https://click.actionnetwork.org/ss/c/u001.iIBo0N5fSFzEFL40Tn-vtZzzfTY2NadQ7R1AXSz5TewzfNxSliP9deNsn4P04ZlY7F1Oq4WWhoKaRveNa5hGnNsl6nHO1VPxCxTkQRBnQYuaF2USb8odRm7PnxOJTlU8tMde5HLtS7XYwYOuegJBKLJENAGdNhPDvcInaUmedze-7nB4ztuQwvOo32qD_bM9m5We3CJTGwexa8JGbL5meauPMsyvgUtqSAs_TMJy1TgWT36qPE1oKQw0E9Dhd8cqFS2eqqy9YTqot1lRNZIn21YRPLoIF7NCFquK8wYvrLA/4db/3DzBYfsuQ4GFaaZMSaWUlQ/h4/h001.jo7kQFb1cJSBRvFmxvnjpYL46b61jxxizb6IVVvBn0c)
Dr. King recognized that racism and poverty are interconnected. Carrying on that legacy, Oakland Forward’s priorities include securing economic justice and pushing for meaningful policy change such as paid family and medical leave. As we enter a new political term with an openly hostile president that doesn't believe in the vision of Dr. King, let's come together in support of our community-based organizations like Oakland Forward. Their work to challenge systemic injustice and racism is more important than ever.
[**For MLK Day, will you chip in to the grassroots nonprofit Oakland Forward, which is carrying on MLK’s legacy for racial justice and a more equitable society?**](https://click.actionnetwork.org/ss/c/u001.iIBo0N5fSFzEFL40Tn-vtZzzfTY2NadQ7R1AXSz5TewzfNxSliP9deNsn4P04ZlY7F1Oq4WWhoKaRveNa5hGnNsl6nHO1VPxCxTkQRBnQYt7CJo3zPiwkNMt3h4HgQSz_3NHRaGg12leYVg1BzWebNQHXF0_M2SesfA0TtYRKKJZG1YPr7kbPTCTYeT6GheOGEkuZyTM0f-XWtoqYu7jKCDLO9H2ciaBJN_iB0d86hhvoAqSg5vpL8XdZCeDDcHPYHJPGJvPEEhiEZtYxTthGfKrH5nO8ZBTy-2kaDHCdqY/4db/3DzBYfsuQ4GFaaZMSaWUlQ/h5/h001.hvzDOSPyOAz4Vv-QQ7TweMNiWANoOU1H8o_MBGzaURk)
[CHIP IN](https://click.actionnetwork.org/ss/c/u001.iIBo0N5fSFzEFL40Tn-vtZzzfTY2NadQ7R1AXSz5TewzfNxSliP9deNsn4P04ZlY7F1Oq4WWhoKaRveNa5hGnNsl6nHO1VPxCxTkQRBnQYvmka4FmSMbSv2zlqD0WMgUeH4_y1_OF5R-wwJQ8fPO3QRWBwF2j4kkKkSifnYeY2qIdqsHDtGtESXXHQvOzxwhuQ5DuX807xt67qYACyuEHE6jv4xrO3znyHD8vr5vrjs5tfUZgpA1q43LYISb2GRLWuyKNmLolyz8q8rX7Wbcfr6ieG1feG71frXashOMkjU/4db/3DzBYfsuQ4GFaaZMSaWUlQ/h6/h001.TybO4qJw5vNAaXo2T1m7tQLhubffg9ReT6oGuim_y_g)
---
_“We are confronted with the fierce urgency of now,”_ said MLK in April 1967. _“Now let us begin. Now let us rededicate ourselves to the long and bitter, but beautiful, struggle for a new world.”_
This Martin Luther King, Jr. Day, we are still struggling for that world. Please know that you always have an ally in me in this ongoing work. Together, we will continue the struggle our movement ancestors led.
It’s an honor to continue to serve you and all the residents of my district in my fourth term in Congress. I’m committing to partnering with my constituents in the fight for equity and justice for all, including guaranteed civil rights for everyone.
Onward together in solidarity,
Rashida

Peter,

_**[I guess I shouldn't count my chickens before they hatch.](http://links.conservativeum.com/u/click?_t=26c2e4aa9bbe40418f61e26972138f21&_m=d03e37e390584f23881242a021ed29f2&_e=FDsxJ_8-W-zIeoaY-NdJ6mxe6HGwV2heDbHbYS1cpoCP20s-mtL0upZZEcUZHxOrDRqQUBZBvLzQctwP8gLXvldHHSJcFu_VGwmOIfTHaYeQlbtT23qa-3bQg87uY4eHVYx_0AUI6x7hc30_U4ddDUfwaCU3JtNke6Oo6kcEfXeWLoHnyQ6VY00Co0vQQZkMnyO0TgkFUE_xS43MVqnOMsEe4VXfbtdOuhnLg7XtyHVAbl3tsx9MpIMp_6IRYYeJJJ7YbGtxGq164Uw9H3L9lPW_lUjNyT9n6JLywaXQ3bqxH8ZB8YTQdwTfqhfOua4Y2wxie0-xbhpwhqzyeRXYmmciUjmBH2qb65d_L1w_ofBAswlOBBRGjVSo6os_61qwboSzbCR_Vkw3mWhBBnseB2O_gHhArxgVELzna9gNl13l4bZztiCKQU850NSeFDtvArigbQ2uUSdkvfAwzcai4nI5SPa5qqiEGOkBt5x6CbIRDW6W7tvL_EG4aV4e7zLR "I guess I shouldn't count my chickens before they hatch.")**_

I thought support from MAGA Patriots was at an all-time high, _but I guess I was wrong. _

**So, I'm choosing to lay all my cards on the table one final time and hope for a different outcome.**

This is Marsha Blackburn - I'm a top ally of Donald Trump and Republican Senator from the great state of Tennessee. I'm sure you think messages like this are a dime a dozen, but I'm pleading with you to hear me out right now.

**Just days from now, President Trump will take the oath of office and be officially sworn in as our 47th President of the United States.**

But here's the deal: _things have really taken a turn since Election Day._

Following our major victory, I thought Republican enthusiasm would be at an all-time high, but that couldn't be further from the truth!

The fact of the matter is that fundraising has _PLUMMETED!_

I hate to put you on the spot like this, but without your help, I'm worried we may come up short of our critical Inauguration Day fundraising goal. This would be a MAJOR setback for our new Republican TRIFECTA and would be a detrimental way to start President Trump's second term!

That is unless we turn things around this very instant. name: Rebecca Weber
subject: Democrats and RINOs CAN’T tank this nomination!
body:
Senate Democrats are going to try anything and everything to coerce and bully any Republican who shows weakness into tanking this critical appointment.

| |
---
| | Today I need your help to DEMAND PETE HEGSETH CONFIRMATION!
---
| |
---
| |
---
[](https://e.updateforamerica.com/rd/9z2zci3b7lmojkpr0f01juoklv2qe98ceq1c457pl0o_rp22sh2s8i6epj7c5g69pb0clh2bs)
---
| | Fellow Patriot, The Senate confirmation hearing for Pete Hegseth just concluded and the real work begins **now** to get him confirmed!Senate Democrats are going to try anything and everything to coerce and bully any Republican who shows weakness into tanking this critical appointment. We refuse to let that happen. _**[_**That's why I need your help to DEMAND PETE HEGSETH CONFIRMATION!**_](https://e.updateforamerica.com/rd/9z2z5qjssof27o3s3qtsuhenl1ebubbtacsi9u1hnso_rp22sh2s8i6epj7c5g69pb0clh2bs)**_ Pete Hegseth is a decorated veteran, a proven leader, and a loyal defender of the America First agenda. He has what it takes to stand up to the deep state, protect our troops, and restore the integrity of our military—something sorely lacking under the Biden administration. America and her military are still reeling from the failed leadership of **Defense Secretary Lloyd Austin** , the architect of the disastrous **_Afghanistan withdrawal._** Under Austin’s watch, America endured one of the most humiliating moments in modern history:
---
| |
* **13 brave service members killed in Kabul.**
* **Thousands of Americans and allies left behind in enemy territory.**
* **Billions of dollars’ worth of U.S. military equipment handed over to the Taliban.**

---
My friend, **our military deserves better.** President Trump understands that. That’s why he’s nominated Pete Hegseth—a battle-hardened warrior who knows what it means to serve his country and will never bow to woke ideologues or globalist interests.But we know what’s coming: Democrats will unite to block his confirmation, and it will only take a handful of weak Republican Senators to sink this critical appointment.If we don’t stand up and demand his confirmation, then weak Republicans could help the Democrats tank this nomination.We can’t let that happen. We won’t let RINOs undermine President Trump again! _**[_**If you agree that Pete Hegseth MUST BE CONFIRMED as Secretary of Defense, then please click here to fill out your CONFIRM PETE HEGSETH Response Form right away!**_](https://e.updateforamerica.com/rd/9z2z5o4nafbkuv3lgfc1rrdeine3b3nft2ictf95hvg_rp22sh2s8i6epj7c5g69pb0clh2bs)**_ And my friend, this isn’t just about Pete Hegseth. This is about ensuring President Trump’s America First agenda is implemented without sabotage from the radical Left or weak Republicans.Donald Trump won in a landslide, mandate election. The American people have given HIM the power to choose his cabinet. To deny his picks is to deny the will of **_We the People_**. If we stand down now, we’ll send a message that RINOs and Democrats can undermine the will of the people and block President Trump’s vision for a stronger America. That can’t happen!AMAC Action is mounting a massive pressure campaign – **but we need every conservative voice backing us up.**
---
[ ** CONFIRM PETE HEGSETH** ](https://e.updateforamerica.com/rd/9z2zqf323vkmnd6rh7e85ssrfsgj3vag85ill1hskg0_rp22sh2s8i6epj7c5g69pb0clh2bs)
---
Friend, my name is Rebecca Weber – CEO of AMAC. You may have heard of AMAC Action before – we’re the #1 most influential advocacy group in the country when it comes to standing for the values of conservative seniors like you. If you’ve ever heard of AMAC Action, you may know that unlike the left-leaning AARP, we’ve been fighting AGAINST “Obamacare”, AGAINST “Medicare for All,” and AGAINST all of these radical schemes to “transform” America and our elections. That’s why we have 75 chapters and 328 delegates fighting to preserve and protect our Constitution and our traditional values and heritage. AMAC Action and its volunteer advocates pound on the doors of every member of the U.S. House of Representatives and the U.S. Senate to fight for you. And with conservative control in Washington with only _razor-thin margins_ , our advocacy is more critical than ever before. We cannot take our foot off the gas for one second. It’s simple: when we speak, Washington listens. When you speak, We listen. And we’ve got a To-Do list a mile long to pave the way for the incoming administration. But my friend, all we do, we do with the support of patriots like you.And mounting a huge pressure campaign to confirm Pete Hegseth will take time, money, and resources. **[That’s why I’m asking you to join AMAC Action with your very best CONFIRM PETE HEGSETH emergency contribution today.](https://e.updateforamerica.com/rd/9z2zo3evj8059b4fsf904ope289nojgv5qm05qtl42g_rp22sh2s8i6epj7c5g69pb0clh2bs)** Let’s send a clear message:
---
* **The American people will not be denied.**
* **Weak-kneed Republicans will not stand in the way of the America First movement.**
* **And Pete Hegseth will be confirmed as Secretary of Defense.**

---
No one is in a better position to advocate for change than AMAC Action – and with you by our side, we’re an unstoppable force.**_But we have got to move quickly. We have just weeks – maybe days –_**** _ to get out ahead of the radical Left and the RINOs – _****_So please don’t delay!_****** Let’s make it clear that the American people will not be denied. Let’s show them that Election Day was just the beginning.
---
| | Thank you, friend!
---
For Freedom,
---
**Rebecca Weber****AMAC Action**
---

name: Sean Feucht
subject: Worshiping In The U.S. Capitol Rotunda
body:
| | | [](https://links.iterable.com/u/click?_t=dcac8242330e4c15a55d3b915d5debd3&_m=7fc78db6372b4ebcbaa8947cebab6194&_e=M37Y0H7sI2p0ZK6UPKJ2NB4GGLpUqRfNZMTeuFKXhkNqEk0XhlxEhqXsHe5p5bKGQMGOzG_kHp7Ln_bdwDtQxOiNtcSMhoX-9O8MzGdSRI5o_808Jo7MSsvCqoQ1gJovKbqxdJffzE_Cbvb04Kqr4pAMf3gUHrUT8htQ-NLzGBd-sNxRltNQsEZV_pVwbFZo75MCkmO_6ZtKPJpxh3dp2PjfOduwzWcMvOgUp-SlhonITNKsjn1KBjWy9WB7Iqiwlgai1ycO81Y8QQgjSyX34nSXUtv2vLmC5KT6_e_wsfaHTAkeMkpovs34WM4ZUzGbwfl56DaLfiiAK3lAQohdZQ%3D%3D)
---
Dear Friend,
---
As President Trump is set to be Inaugurated in the Rotunda of the United States Capitol, we remember how God opened the door to this hallowed space and allowed Let Us Worship to fill the very same air with praise and worship. We've been given special access to this space, and we took full advantage. It allowed us to pray over a space that would be used for this monumental event.
---
[](https://links.iterable.com/u/click?_t=dcac8242330e4c15a55d3b915d5debd3&_m=7fc78db6372b4ebcbaa8947cebab6194&_e=M37Y0H7sI2p0ZK6UPKJ2NB4GGLpUqRfNZMTeuFKXhkNqEk0XhlxEhqXsHe5p5bKGQMGOzG_kHp7Ln_bdwDtQxOiNtcSMhoX-9O8MzGdSRI5o_808Jo7MSsvCqoQ1gJovKbqxdJffzE_Cbvb04Kqr4pAMf3gUHrUT8htQ-NLzGBdq7JDSZxQUCNKAMhW73DaJ2cuqGizf29pFCfmCunTdL6bm3l1u_01OXD1eR_fXxu5q1zvIyL8lIwiEHpyXUlp5y--aX7cseF4wYEa25pFmzN5KnKzX8YOD-76TolPcqNnOnNTqIPn3q9NU7Jx3FGU3M_GUmhOOzwghgdOlPKdiMw%3D%3D)
---
It's just another example of His faithfulness, and I'm reminded of Daniel 2: "Then Daniel praised the God of heaven and said: “Praise be to the name of God for ever and ever;
wisdom and power are his.
He changes times and seasons;
he deposes kings and raises up others.
He gives wisdom to the wise
and knowledge to the discerning.
He reveals deep and hidden things;
he knows what lies in darkness,
and light dwells with him."
---
Till all hear,
---
---
Sean
---
[**SUPPORT LET US WORSHIP >>>**](https://links.iterable.com/u/click?_t=dcac8242330e4c15a55d3b915d5debd3&_m=7fc78db6372b4ebcbaa8947cebab6194&_e=M37Y0H7sI2p0ZK6UPKJ2NB4GGLpUqRfNZMTeuFKXhkNqEk0XhlxEhqXsHe5p5bKGQMGOzG_kHp7Ln_bdwDtQxOiNtcSMhoX-9O8MzGdSRI5o_808Jo7MSsvCqoQ1gJovKbqxdJffzE_Cbvb04Kqr4pAMf3gUHrUT8htQ-NLzGBe4HH7EB5MOWqqD6nXW4i5YBC4va9yyeu3BMcyfvmw2M7B465JcWixDG9L76LMtmSLSROd7_qpvSIGuf_bTu-iAwRlYcI4Cav0i1D9FivwMQ3uq6bPwYvD8a8Vpy1kP2pdMynH4ih0fOXND1OL4MLLdXjxVCWdwaIWbMJsD7WjPIA%3D%3D)

**» Read this morning's (free) edition **[here](https://substack.com/redirect/c6ef9522-5a2e-439d-814e-1c347926c8d3?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg).

>> SUBSCRIBE HERE: [ATXpulsepremium.com](https://substack.com/redirect/68dd6dc8-2d8d-4065-9c98-381f2c75652b?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg). ($5/mo or $50/yr or $199 lifetime)
* * *
* **TOP NEWS**

"**Texans brace for Arctic blast as ERCOT issues another Weather Watch,** " KVUE's**John Diaz** -- "With [another Arctic blast coming to Texas](https://substack.com/redirect/2a94d34c-e6d8-4d8d-8382-af4348df7e79?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg), the Electric Reliability Council of Texas (ERCOT) has issued a Weather Watch due to [freezing temperatures in the forecast](https://substack.com/redirect/2a94d34c-e6d8-4d8d-8382-af4348df7e79?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg).

Extreme cold in the forecast for most of Texas means higher electrical demand and the possibility of lower reserves.

The watch is in effect beginning Monday, Jan 20, through Thursday, Jan. 23. However, ERCOT said it expects grid conditions to be normal during the Weather Watch and no energy emergencies are expected.
[BECOME A PAID SUBSCRIBER: $7/mo](https://substack.com/redirect/900ad053-687b-4c2a-8b54-d00622f4184e?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)
Weather Watches are issued to help Texans stay informed about grid conditions in case of emergencies.

ERCOT stated that it will closely monitor conditions and utilize all available tools to manage the grid effectively. According to [ERCOT's 6-Day Forecast](https://substack.com/redirect/55ee48e3-9648-41ea-8367-d42b64ec07c5?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg), energy demand is expected to peak on Monday and Tuesday mornings, when wintry precipitation could be possible for some parts of Texas.
[BECOME A PAID SUBSCRIBER: $70/yr](https://substack.com/redirect/900ad053-687b-4c2a-8b54-d00622f4184e?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)
Texans can stay updated on grid conditions by signing up for notifications through the [Texas Advisory and Notification System (TXANS)](https://substack.com/redirect/4625b6df-18f9-4e49-bcfb-a3f78adb4638?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg). Real-time and extended [grid condition updates are available here](https://substack.com/redirect/06e124dc-9eb8-4b1f-9a27-4c8ed191ef1b?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg). Additionally, Texans can subscribe to ERCOT emergency alerts or download the ERCOT app for timely updates.

In December, [ERCOT warned that extreme cold events would be more likely](https://substack.com/redirect/0485d2f1-1dc8-45a6-98ee-78b90d52b7c0?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) than usual this winter, similar to those in 2021. However, ERCOT CEO Pablo Vegas said in November that he [doesn't expect calls for conservation or rolling blackouts](https://substack.com/redirect/9ffbb892-5a0b-4853-9490-8b437eac00c9?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg).

While this Arctic blast is expected to bring the coldest temperatures of the winter so far, Vegas said there have been a lot of [changes to the grid since the deadly winter storm](https://substack.com/redirect/9ffbb892-5a0b-4853-9490-8b437eac00c9?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)." [KVUE](https://substack.com/redirect/c6516bdb-1005-4639-9966-450600f5e4e7?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)
**FREE SUBSCRIPTIONS HAVE ENDED** _: As of July 11, 2022, we have moved to a paywall for free subscribers. Free subscribers will only receive the top story in their morning email. Free subscribers will need to become paid subscribers to receive full story summaries_
[ Upgrade to paid](https://substack.com/redirect/2/eyJlIjoiaHR0cHM6Ly9tdXN0cmVhZHR4LnN1YnN0YWNrLmNvbS9zdWJzY3JpYmU_.Tg7WU-HKkeAdPBb_CYpx7FTmUSwgnVVsPZw8zTFZibc?&utm_medium=email&utm_source=subscribe-widget-preamble&utm_content=155262570)
* **TOP NEWS**

HOU CHRON: "**Houston freeze will cause significant travel disruptions, leaders say** " [Houston Chronicle](https://substack.com/redirect/1b436212-f50e-4c54-92df-5ecb4d89c41b?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) ($)
DMN: "**Dallas weather: North Texas may get snow flurries, no accumulation** " [DMN](https://substack.com/redirect/a1bdbc54-6dd9-4e3f-83d0-6bac04c81981?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) ($)
SAEN: "**Winter storm hits South Texas tonight. Here's when roads get icy** " [SAEN](https://substack.com/redirect/0ef36099-c80b-447c-ba0f-b349c7466cce?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) ($)
AP: "**Appeals court rules against DACA, but leaves protections for migrants in place** " [AP](https://substack.com/redirect/4050cd93-1b4e-4f40-9b90-46d298f4f6f6?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)
SAEN: "**Two Mexican nationals sentenced in AR-15 smuggling operation** " [SAEN](https://substack.com/redirect/9f24a995-149e-4450-a4ca-d3dfd1a7ca7c?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) ($)

Extreme cold in the forecast for most of Texas means higher electrical demand and the possibility of lower reserves. The watch is in effect beginning Monday, Jan 20, through Thursday, Jan. 23. However, ERCOT said it expects grid conditions to be normal during the Weather Watch and no energy emergencies are expected. Weather Watches are issued to help Texans stay informed about grid conditions in case of emergencies. ERCOT stated that it will closely monitor conditions and utilize all available tools to manage the grid effectively. According to , energy demand is expected to peak on Monday and Tuesday mornings, when wintry precipitation could be possible for some parts of Texas. subject: whose America?

body:
| [ ](http://clicksp.frostforcongress.com/f/a/Z_RtIy0jITX6Abo10uBn9A~~/AAPPAAA~/RgRpcQX4P0TSaHR0cHM6Ly9zZWN1cmUuYWN0Ymx1ZS5jb20vZG9uYXRlL21hZi1lbS1wZ2U_cmVmY29kZT0yMDI1MDEyMF9lbV9mcl9iMV9pbmF1Z3VyYXRpb24tZGF5X2VuJmFtb3VudD0yNyZjZG1jPTJydGZYQzR5eUVWUFR0YTduNGVwa0JIVndHRSZyZWZjb2RlMj0ycnRmWEM0eXlFVlBUdGE3bjRlcGtCSFZ3R0UmcmVmY29kZWNkbWM9MnJ0ZlhDNHl5RVZQVHRhN240ZXBrQkhWd0dFVwNzcGNCCmeJ-ICOZyHmeeZSFGRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tWAQAAABp)
---
**Peter, words can’t express how _wrong_ it is that Donald Trump is being inaugurated on Martin Luther King, Jr. Day.** Their world views couldn’t have less to do with each other – which means Trump’s view couldn’t have less to do with the America we should be striving for today. I saw that America last year, when I was mobilizing young voters across the country. I saw it in Gen Z, in my constituents, and in all Americans: a country of _ideals_ , built on Dr. King’s dream and the dreams of people who fought for progress before us. That’s how I know that our country isn't one of hate or fear mongering, even if that’s what Donald Trump wants us to believe. **Our country is one of hope. But hope needs sustaining. It needs to be fought for. And Donald Trump is determined to make that fight damn expensive.** So if you’re with me to fight against the odds, I need you to show it right now. Let’s put our money where our mouth is, and raise the undeniable proof that we’re motivated by more than hate or fear. [**_Peter, I’m committed to fighting for my generation and MLK’s ideals every day – under every administration. But we have to fuel this fight together, dollar by dollar. clean_body: | Peter, words can’t express how wrong it is that Donald Trump is being inaugurated on Martin Luther King, Jr. Day. Their world views couldn’t have less to do with each other – which means Trump’s view couldn’t have less to do with the America we should be striving for today. I saw that America last year, when I was mobilizing young voters across the country. I saw it in Gen Z, in my constituents, and in all Americans: a country of ideals , built on Dr. King’s dream and the dreams of people who fought for progress before us. That’s how I know that our country isn't one of hate or fear mongering, even if that’s what Donald Trump wants us to believe. Our country is one of hope. But hope needs sustaining. It needs to be fought for. And Donald Trump is determined to make that fight damn expensive. So if you’re with me to fight against the odds, I need you to show it right now. Let’s put our money where our mouth is, and raise the undeniable proof that we’re motivated by more than hate or fear. If you've saved your payment information with ActBlue Express, your donation will go through immediately: | | | | | ~~ | ~~ | ~ Let’s win this fight, Maxwell Frost | Maxwell Alejandro Frost is an organizer representing Florida's 10th Congressional District. | Peter,

Donald Trump's return to the White House is a stark reminder of the challenges ahead and the work we must do to protect and build upon the grassroots infrastructure we’ve built together.

Republicans hold slim majorities in the House and Senate, and they’re prepared to use every loophole they can find to ram through their extreme agenda and gut _Social Security_ and _Medicare_ to pay for giant tax cuts for their billionaire and CEO buddies.

Holding Trump accountable over these next 4 years will require early and strategic investments in key races across the country. **Your support is critical as we work to build progressive power in battleground races, flip the House and Senate, and fight for hard-working Americans!**

body:
| Peter, the time is now 12:00 PM EST, and Donald Trump is officially the President of the United States. **It’s time to buck up and get to work now, Peter.**

The next 4 years will be unpredictable for our country, for our democracy, and our planet. **But we’re _not_ powerless to fight back.**

Republicans have a strenuous hold on the House. And with Special Elections around the corner to fill the vacated seats of a number of Trump appointees, we could narrow that lead even further. **But I need your help, Peter!**

If we want to undo Donald Trump’s power, we have to start building back right now. That means winning the messaging war, earning back the trust of the working class, and flipping the perception amongst voters that Democrats won’t fight for them.

[**My team and I are already underway on that fight, but such an uphill climb takes resources, and buy-in, from top supporters like you. Your support, of even just $1, is one small part of our work to undo Donald Trump’s power. "**Texans brace for Arctic blast as ERCOT issues another Weather Watch,** " KVUE's**John Diaz** -- "With [another Arctic blast coming to Texas](https://substack.com/redirect/707c79ee-fd40-4a21-a38e-c20a4c49d5cc?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg), the Electric Reliability Council of Texas (ERCOT) has issued a Weather Watch due to [freezing temperatures in the forecast](https://substack.com/redirect/707c79ee-fd40-4a21-a38e-c20a4c49d5cc?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg).

Extreme cold in the forecast for most of Texas means higher electrical demand and the possibility of lower reserves.

The watch is in effect beginning Monday, Jan 20, through Thursday, Jan. 23. However, ERCOT said it expects grid conditions to be normal during the Weather Watch and no energy emergencies are expected.
[BECOME A PAID SUBSCRIBER: $7/mo](https://substack.com/redirect/d93856fd-e631-489e-a5b5-c53480fabb92?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)
Weather Watches are issued to help Texans stay informed about grid conditions in case of emergencies.

ERCOT stated that it will closely monitor conditions and utilize all available tools to manage the grid effectively. According to [ERCOT's 6-Day Forecast](https://substack.com/redirect/7df41246-a30c-4060-8919-e383e10b0c88?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg), energy demand is expected to peak on Monday and Tuesday mornings, when wintry precipitation could be possible for some parts of Texas.
[BECOME A PAID SUBSCRIBER: $70/yr](https://substack.com/redirect/d93856fd-e631-489e-a5b5-c53480fabb92?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)
Texans can stay updated on grid conditions by signing up for notifications through the [Texas Advisory and Notification System (TXANS)](https://substack.com/redirect/f94845b1-36fc-4030-ae60-cd45f73b6aef?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg). Real-time and extended [grid condition updates are available here](https://substack.com/redirect/2c941a82-482f-49f9-b472-62acc93b6860?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg). Additionally, Texans can subscribe to ERCOT emergency alerts or download the ERCOT app for timely updates.

In December, [ERCOT warned that extreme cold events would be more likely](https://substack.com/redirect/5c9fe603-3004-4e7b-9f7e-4f4f177e4e75?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) than usual this winter, similar to those in 2021. However, ERCOT CEO Pablo Vegas said in November that he [doesn't expect calls for conservation or rolling blackouts](https://substack.com/redirect/1fc9b226-aa03-4263-8f9a-f2bd5ca7723c?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg).

While this Arctic blast is expected to bring the coldest temperatures of the winter so far, Vegas said there have been a lot of [changes to the grid since the deadly winter storm](https://substack.com/redirect/1fc9b226-aa03-4263-8f9a-f2bd5ca7723c?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)." [KVUE](https://substack.com/redirect/a9b29316-0227-4e75-909a-23c00c8825f1?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)

name: AMAC Action
subject: Democrats and RINOs CAN’Ttank this nomination!
body:
Senate Democrats are going to try anything and everything to coerce and bully any Republican who shows weakness into tanking this critical appointment.

| |
---
| | Today I need your help to DEMAND PETE HEGSETH CONFIRMATION!
---
| |
---
| |
---
[](http://e.goppresidential.com/rd/9z2zu4d0rrdnbl8dvu9melpdjvflab27sum2pnoablo_rp22sh2s8i6epj7c5i60ob2c3h2bs)
---
| | Fellow Patriot, The Senate confirmation hearing for Pete Hegseth just concluded and the real work begins **now** to get him confirmed!Senate Democrats are going to try anything and everything to coerce and bully any Republican who shows weakness into tanking this critical appointment. We refuse to let that happen. _**[_**That's why I need your help to DEMAND PETE HEGSETH CONFIRMATION!**_](http://e.goppresidential.com/rd/9z2z3gjnqngk1loa7kstg8qdik1m68cgkodegjpoilg_rp22sh2s8i6epj7c5i60ob2c3h2bs)**_ Pete Hegseth is a decorated veteran, a proven leader, and a loyal defender of the America First agenda. He has what it takes to stand up to the deep state, protect our troops, and restore the integrity of our military—something sorely lacking under the Biden administration. America and her military are still reeling from the failed leadership of **Defense Secretary Lloyd Austin** , the architect of the disastrous **_Afghanistan withdrawal._** Under Austin’s watch, America endured one of the most humiliating moments in modern history:
---
| |
* **13 brave service members killed in Kabul.**
* **Thousands of Americans and allies left behind in enemy territory.**
* **Billions of dollars’ worth of U.S. military equipment handed over to the Taliban.**

---
My friend, **our military deserves better.** President Trump understands that. That’s why he’s nominated Pete Hegseth—a battle-hardened warrior who knows what it means to serve his country and will never bow to woke ideologues or globalist interests.But we know what’s coming: Democrats will unite to block his confirmation, and it will only take a handful of weak Republican Senators to sink this critical appointment.If we don’t stand up and demand his confirmation, then weak Republicans could help the Democrats tank this nomination.We can’t let that happen. We won’t let RINOs undermine President Trump again! _**[_**If you agree that Pete Hegseth MUST BE CONFIRMED as Secretary of Defense, then please click here to fill out your CONFIRM PETE HEGSETH Response Form right away!**_](http://e.goppresidential.com/rd/9z2z8hkdfca6qiq8bvdga5u0of2af5np38chf35ne9g_rp22sh2s8i6epj7c5i60ob2c3h2bs)**_ And my friend, this isn’t just about Pete Hegseth. This is about ensuring President Trump’s America First agenda is implemented without sabotage from the radical Left or weak Republicans.Donald Trump won in a landslide, mandate election. The American people have given HIM the power to choose his cabinet. To deny his picks is to deny the will of **_We the People_**. If we stand down now, we’ll send a message that RINOs and Democrats can undermine the will of the people and block President Trump’s vision for a stronger America. That can’t happen!AMAC Action is mounting a massive pressure campaign – **but we need every conservative voice backing us up.**
---
[ ** CONFIRM PETE HEGSETH** ](http://e.goppresidential.com/rd/9z2z4cua7fip7hpamu72d9v7irbffbn2ar359e10t6g_rp22sh2s8i6epj7c5i60ob2c3h2bs)
---
Friend, my name is Rebecca Weber – CEO of AMAC. You may have heard of AMAC Action before – we’re the #1 most influential advocacy group in the country when it comes to standing for the values of conservative seniors like you. If you’ve ever heard of AMAC Action, you may know that unlike the left-leaning AARP, we’ve been fighting AGAINST “Obamacare”, AGAINST “Medicare for All,” and AGAINST all of these radical schemes to “transform” America and our elections. That’s why we have 75 chapters and 328 delegates fighting to preserve and protect our Constitution and our traditional values and heritage. AMAC Action and its volunteer advocates pound on the doors of every member of the U.S. House of Representatives and the U.S. Senate to fight for you. And with conservative control in Washington with only _razor-thin margins_ , our advocacy is more critical than ever before. We cannot take our foot off the gas for one second. It’s simple: when we speak, Washington listens. When you speak, We listen. And we’ve got a To-Do list a mile long to pave the way for the incoming administration. But my friend, all we do, we do with the support of patriots like you.And mounting a huge pressure campaign to confirm Pete Hegseth will take time, money, and resources. **[That’s why I’m asking you to join AMAC Action with your very best CONFIRM PETE HEGSETH emergency contribution today.](http://e.goppresidential.com/rd/9z2zr5h536b0ngug8ae6rrs4i409r6frk5b16b697q8_rp22sh2s8i6epj7c5i60ob2c3h2bs)** Let’s send a clear message:
---
* **The American people will not be denied.**
* **Weak-kneed Republicans will not stand in the way of the America First movement.**
* **And Pete Hegseth will be confirmed as Secretary of Defense.**

---
No one is in a better position to advocate for change than AMAC Action – and with you by our side, we’re an unstoppable force.**_But we have got to move quickly. We have just weeks – maybe days –_**** _ to get out ahead of the radical Left and the RINOs – _****_So please don’t delay!_****** Let’s make it clear that the American people will not be denied. Let’s show them that Election Day was just the beginning.
---
| | Thank you, friend!
---
For Freedom,
---
**Rebecca Weber****AMAC Action**

clean_body: Senate Democrats are going to try anything and everything to coerce and bully any Republican who shows weakness into tanking this critical appointment. | | | | Today I need your help to DEMAND PETE HEGSETH CONFIRMATION! | | | | | | Fellow Patriot, The Senate confirmation hearing for Pete Hegseth just concluded and the real work begins now to get him confirmed!Senate Democrats are going to try anything and everything to coerce and bully any Republican who shows weakness into tanking this critical appointment. We refuse to let that happen. Pete Hegseth is a decorated veteran, a proven leader, and a loyal defender of the America First agenda. He has what it takes to stand up to the deep state, protect our troops, and restore the integrity of our military—something sorely lacking under the Biden administration. America and her military are still reeling from the failed leadership of Defense Secretary Lloyd Austin , the architect of the disastrous Afghanistan withdrawal. Under Austin’s watch, America endured one of the most humiliating moments in modern history: | | 13 brave service members killed in Kabul. Thousands of Americans and allies left behind in enemy territory. Billions of dollars’ worth of U.S. military equipment handed over to the Taliban. My friend, our military deserves better. President Trump understands that. That’s why he’s nominated Pete Hegseth—a battle-hardened warrior who knows what it means to serve his country and will never bow to woke ideologues or globalist interests.But we know what’s coming: Democrats will unite to block his confirmation, and it will only take a handful of weak Republican Senators to sink this critical appointment.If we don’t stand up and demand his confirmation, then weak Republicans could help the Democrats tank this nomination.We can’t let that happen. We won’t let RINOs undermine President Trump again! And my friend, this isn’t just about Pete Hegseth. This is about ensuring President Trump’s America First agenda is implemented without sabotage from the radical Left or weak Republicans.Donald Trump won in a landslide, mandate election. The American people have given HIM the power to choose his cabinet. To deny his picks is to deny the will of We the People. If we stand down now, we’ll send a message that RINOs and Democrats can undermine the will of the people and block President Trump’s vision for a stronger America. That can’t happen!AMAC Action is mounting a massive pressure campaign – but we need every conservative voice backing us up. Friend, my name is Rebecca Weber – CEO of AMAC. You may have heard of AMAC Action before – we’re the #1 most influential advocacy group in the country when it comes to standing for the values of conservative seniors like you. If you’ve ever heard of AMAC Action, you may know that unlike the left-leaning AARP, we’ve been fighting AGAINST “Obamacare”, AGAINST “Medicare for All,” and AGAINST all of these radical schemes to “transform” America and our elections. That’s why we have 75 chapters and 328 delegates fighting to preserve and protect our Constitution and our traditional values and heritage. AMAC Action and its volunteer advocates pound on the doors of every member of the U.S. House of Representatives and the U.S. Senate to fight for you. And with conservative control in Washington with only razor-thin margins , our advocacy is more critical than ever before. We cannot take our foot off the gas for one second. It’s simple: when we speak, Washington listens. When you speak, We listen. And we’ve got a To-Do list a mile long to pave the way for the incoming administration. But my friend, all we do, we do with the support of patriots like you.And mounting a huge pressure campaign to confirm Pete Hegseth will take time, money, and resources. Let’s send a clear message: The American people will not be denied. Weak-kneed Republicans will not stand in the way of the America First movement. And Pete Hegseth will be confirmed as Secretary of Defense. No one is in a better position to advocate for change than AMAC Action – and with you by our side, we’re an unstoppable force.But we have got to move quickly. We have just weeks – maybe days – to get out ahead of the radical Left and the RINOs – So please don’t delay!** Let’s make it clear that the American people will not be denied. Let’s show them that Election Day was just the beginning. | | Thank you, friend! For Freedom, Rebecca Weber**AMAC Action | | | | | AMAC Action is a 501(c)4 committed to working directly with patriots like you to preserve the values that have made America the greatest nation. The "Action" branch of AMAC, our community-based chapters work tirelessly educating AMAC members and like-minded conservative members of the community and driving legislative results for our country. name: JAG
subject: In the Victimhood Olympics, Everyone Loses
body:
When I was born in India (where my parents were working in the Peace Corps), there was a horrific and tragic practice of “doctors” mutilating children.

|
---
| | [](https://e.americandefensenews.com/rd/9z2z5befp0jo3e105iu31hqm0b654ilh0vjb0svg7t8_rp22sh2s8i6epj7c5i60ob4c3h2bs) Friend,

When I was born in India (where my parents were working in the Peace Corps), there was a horrific and tragic practice of “doctors” mutilating children. Sometimes, hands or legs were chopped off; sometimes, eyes gouged out.

“Why???” you gasp in horror. Sadism? Some demented selfishness? To the contrary. The answer is altruism: these barbaric butchers were trying to make the children more pitiful (and more profitable) beggars, playing on the sympathies for such victims as to merit a few more rupees cast their way.

You shake your head: such a thing couldn’t happen here in modern America, you reassure yourself. Here, young people strive to be rewarded for their merit, not their misfortunes, right? For their achievements, not their ailments. For their talents, not their tales of woe.

If you still believe that you’re living in the wrong century. And you need to wake up.

Go to any university campus or public school. There, you’ll find DEI activist teachers doing the work of the child butchers – but instead of physically mutilating the young, they mutilate their minds by inculcating them with narratives of victimhood.

Their subjects aren’t waving deformed limbs but waving banners of oppression. And unlike the butchered beggars of India, rather than begging, they demand. Reparations. Special privileges. Affirmative action. Safe spaces.

But the real aim of this modern mutilation – for both the perpetrators and its victims – is power.

Ayn Rand pointed out the similarities between these ancient barbaric practices and the way in which modern education deforms the mind and crushes the spirit. She did it by paraphrasing her favorite author, Victor Hugo:
_“And what did they make of these children? Monsters.
Why monsters? To rule.”_
Justice met Need, and the natural offshoot was Victimhood.

Victimhood’s unholy union with Envy is what’s driving many of the problems we face today.

The victim mentality turns people’s grief into grievances, telling them ever so seductively, _“The reason you don’t have what you want or need is because you’re a victim of..._

_A rigged system
Systemic racism
Sexism and misogyny_
... the list goes on and on.

This victim mentality spreads like a Socially Transmitted Disease, eating away at that which is best within us: optimism, reason, self-esteem, benevolence, and – most importantly – gratitude.

By pitting black and white, men and women, rich and poor, against each other in a never-ending victim-oppressor dance, Victimhood replaces one’s identity as a strong, capable individual with the label _“Victim.”_ It breeds a sense of entitlement that breaks the human spirit.

At The Atlas Society, we’re committed to inoculating young minds against this corrosive mentality by using cutting-edge media to communicate Ayn Rand’s ideals of reason, achievement, personal responsibility, and liberty to the next generation.

[_Today, I’m asking you to join me in this pursuit. When you donate $35, $50, $100, or more, you are playing an integral role in saving young people around the world from the grip of Victimhood. Click to donate and receive an exclusive FREE download of our graphic novel of _Anthem_ with your gift of $35 or more! >>_](https://e.americandefensenews.com/rd/9z2z9bvrd4gnbcj1r414a95tc2s5kevh9bv6v30si0o_rp22sh2s8i6epj7c5i60ob4c3h2bs)
[](https://e.americandefensenews.com/rd/9z2z2g4dfjhotscqifkvolhkvm77u6trp6kn768u9vo_rp22sh2s8i6epj7c5i60ob4c3h2bs)
As the CEO of The Atlas Society, I know that _it’s not easy to achieve your dreams_. I worked hard to get where I am today, and I am _grateful_ for the men and women who helped me be _victorious_ in achieving my goals.

I’m particularly grateful to Ayn Rand, whose philosophy gave me the weapons to fight back against envy, victimhood, and resentment. I’m committed to sharing these resources with the rising generation of young people in ways that are engaging, accessible, and inspiring.

Whether it’s through our action-packed animated book trailers, our thought-provoking Draw My Life videos, or our captivating graphic novels, The Atlas Society is using art to engage young minds with the message of a free society where everyone has the opportunity to be victorious.

Ayn Rand herself called art _“the indispensable medium for the communication of a moral ideal.”_ You can help us replace the victim mentality with Ayn Rand’s vision of self-determination and achievement by donating today.
[Donate to Fight the Victim Mentality >>](https://e.americandefensenews.com/rd/9z2znceknbn53enhahet6qi51u21v7v8pg7mmsue1p8_rp22sh2s8i6epj7c5i60ob4c3h2bs)
By introducing young people to the ideas of Ayn Rand, we are providing them with an opportunity to build a victim-proof mindset that prizes TRUTH above all else.

Victimhood tells young people, **_“You’ll never be a _victor_ , but you can _always_ be a victim.”_**

**Ayn Rand offers a radically different message that empowers them to reach their fullest potential** by embracing reason, achievement, benevolence, and ethical self-interest as the foundation for liberty, happiness, and a flourishing society.

Ed Crane, Founder of the CATO Institute, deemed Ayn Rand the _“all-time greatest recruiter for the liberty movement”_ because her novels provide a captivating alternative to the siren song of collective victimhood.

[_Donate $35 or more to download an exclusive copy of our graphic novel adaptation of _Anthem_ and see for yourself how we’re challenging the victim mentality through the works of Ayn Rand. Click now to make your tax-deductible investment in a freer, more empowering future. >>_](https://e.americandefensenews.com/rd/9z2zfklk24bfcm3rqumvirhov9o3r0ibtjsp89cvq10_rp22sh2s8i6epj7c5i60ob4c3h2bs)
[Donate $10](https://e.americandefensenews.com/rd/9z2z9p6p1d1ko86ijspse6sl2gc6gpqpm00uqsq9200_rp22sh2s8i6epj7c5i60ob4c3h2bs)

[Donate $35](https://e.americandefensenews.com/rd/9z2zrd2dfc33451n5jih6pkpcp8gplasisvpcsld988_rp22sh2s8i6epj7c5i60ob4c3h2bs)

[Donate $50](https://e.americandefensenews.com/rd/9z2z90gp9mfr5taq65qmb3pj49q84rpmf4qt454gth8_rp22sh2s8i6epj7c5i60ob4c3h2bs)

[Donate $100](https://e.americandefensenews.com/rd/9z2znveafgfr5llr8md4fqqg0k0jolm1n963h331kmo_rp22sh2s8i6epj7c5i60ob4c3h2bs)

[Donate $250](https://e.americandefensenews.com/rd/9z2z1aua46ovm39a5nmeoj2iito179pnp7k7cmudgcg_rp22sh2s8i6epj7c5i60ob4c3h2bs)

[Donate $500](https://e.americandefensenews.com/rd/9z2zrvl8f8ob5temg47idtpnjpq2iabc1t7ve17tul8_rp22sh2s8i6epj7c5i60ob4c3h2bs)

[Donate $1,000](https://e.americandefensenews.com/rd/9z2z965e8d6ovo48rrgpnuj7qkoi9ufifg48np41oq0_rp22sh2s8i6epj7c5i60ob4c3h2bs)

[Donate Any Other Amount](https://e.americandefensenews.com/rd/9z2z9cj748r14ocen8gpo1heooq74mtns1d1081uvlo_rp22sh2s8i6epj7c5i60ob4c3h2bs)
Ayn Rand’s novels stand as a testament to the human spirit and a celebration of the individual’s power to break through the fog of Victimhood, Envy, and Resentment and instead realize a future marked by achievement, pride, and gratitude.

Along with my colleagues at The Atlas Society, I’m on a mission to reach 1 BILLION young people with Ayn Rand’s work. _I hope you’ll join me!_

Thank you in advance for your support,

Jennifer Anju Grossman (JAG)
CEO
The Atlas Society

_The Atlas Society is a 501(c)(3) Not For Profit Organization. All donations are tax-deductible._
