name: Sharice Davids
subject: No matter what lies ahead
disclaimer_text: PAID FOR BY SHARICE FOR CONGRESS
body:
I understand that, for many of you, today feels like a bleak day

[](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/100699234/523595742/-240735101?refcode=email-header&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMDIvMS82NTc1OCIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICJjOGNlYzQ2My01ZGQ3LWVmMTEtODhkMC0wMDIyNDgyYTlkOTIiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=O3-SToU9BPGkVTlKsGXTsZSxVSVgZp31AWkyQdasyzs=) I understand that, for many of you, today feels like a bleak day. Under the new presidential administration, I know that we will face unprecedented challenges in the years ahead when it comes to protecting the fundamental freedoms we hold dear. **But I want to emphasize that I am not backing down from my values — and I hope you won’t either.** I know that I have a unique voice as Kansas’ only Democratic representative in Washington. And I have a duty to use that voice in Congress to stand up to extremism, defend our rights, and advocate for common sense over petty politics. I’ve never believed that politics should only be about stopping your opponents and nothing more. It’s about bringing people together to effect change and building a better future for the next generation. While I hope the new administration is as committed to those values as I am, please know that I will be fighting for them every single day for as long as I have the privilege of serving as your Congresswoman. No matter what lies ahead for our state and our country, I remain committed to the work that the people of Kansas elected me to do: Bringing resources and results back to families and communities across the Third District. Finally, I wanted to say thank you one last time for your continued support. I am immensely grateful to have you on this team as we take on the challenges ahead of us. Together, we’ll be ready for whatever’s next. With gratitude, -Sharice Davids | | [Donate →](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/100699235/523595743/-2111986852?refcode=email_012025_Inauguration&amounts=5,25,50,100,250,500,1000&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMDIvMS82NTc1OCIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICJjOGNlYzQ2My01ZGQ3LWVmMTEtODhkMC0wMDIyNDgyYTlkOTIiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=O3-SToU9BPGkVTlKsGXTsZSxVSVgZp31AWkyQdasyzs=)

clean_body: I understand that, for many of you, today feels like a bleak day = I understand that, for many of you, today feels like a bleak day. Under the new presidential administration, I know that we will face unprecedented challenges in the years ahead when it comes to protecting the fundamental freedoms we hold dear. But I want to emphasize that I am not backing down from my values — and I hope you won’t either. I know that I have a unique voice as Kansas’ only Democratic representative in Washington. And I have a duty to use that voice in Congress to stand up to extremism, defend our rights, and advocate for common sense over petty politics. I’ve never believed that politics should only be about stopping your opponents and nothing more. It’s about bringing people together to effect change and building a better future for the next generation. While I hope the new administration is as committed to those values as I am, please know that I will be fighting for them every single day for as long as I have the privilege of serving as your Congresswoman. No matter what lies ahead for our state and our country, I remain committed to the work that the people of Kansas elected me to do: Bringing resources and results back to families and communities across the Third District. Finally, I wanted to say thank you one last time for your continued support. I am immensely grateful to have you on this team as we take on the challenges ahead of us. Together, we’ll be ready for whatever’s next. With gratitude, -Sharice Davids | | = | This email was sent to: dpwillis67@gmail.com. Email is a key way for us to stay in touch and make sure you get the latest updates from our campaign. name: Inaugural Alert - via House GOP
subject: In recognition of YOUR efforts to bring about this momentous occasion…
disclaimer_text: Paid for by the NRCC and not authorized by any candidate or candidate's committee
body:
We cannot emphasize how important this is

|
---
| | | | [](https://email.republicanmajority.net/c/eJyUkbGOnjAQhJ_GdEbrBYxdUJx0ovujKE1Ky78x3Ea2QcZwd28fOdJVaZJ2NfPNrObnwzwsBUrbNxv9lKPJpym3Sdk5_rlf_PTBu8Kzd2QQcOAgOILpmmWSGkclGj-JsRt76MUAzdukYV0kKKfkoFAhaq0cSNDaucE917WhqXJAIAjVKzG22Nt1EfDUyvXeyifrIfvjegZyNkX7a89UPtvkSxOmt1KOk3UvDGeG8-ndlX37Tin7pXV7ZDjX5gxnSvbarmwD3_aw-MQ3e_tQbd1sYzn2kwrtiXWvHUOZKyhT2lj3WvLlGcqrRONsPCxtVfXV2Xx_4T8eGlohpFBgihi0BDXwYawg_2EKLf-u_5Oyp-JTqdE3z5F_WRnKjyvdf92bPC3HO4VApxxZD1u0FOr3TfmfAe8JfwcAAP__9d-bkQ "Golden Gavel")

Friend,

Today marks a momentous occasion: the swearing-in of our Republican trifecta in Washington. **As one of our most dedicated supporters, we're honored to present you with the exclusive inaugural Golden Gavel Award,** commemorating your tireless efforts to bring about this historic moment.

This distinction is reserved for individuals who have made a significant impact in shaping the future of our nation. **Your unwavering commitment to our cause has been instrumental in driving meaningful change, and _we're grateful for your partnership._**

---

name: Sarah Elfreth
subject: Reading Dr. King’s words with my students
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Elfreth for Maryland
body:
Today reminds us of Dr. King's dream of an America where equality, justice, and opportunity prevailed for all.

| [](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/100700007/523597433/-2051496126?nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMTIvMS85MTI0OSIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICI2ODFmODc4OC01ZmQ3LWVmMTEtODhkMC0wMDIyNDgyYTlkOTIiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=TfCT3oujUQnFACp82wgrJH-58zPTWYx92DcPOsnYHeU=&emci=c3090c7f-5fd7-ef11-88d0-0022482a9d92&emdi=681f8788-5fd7-ef11-88d0-0022482a9d92&ceid=30237190)
---
Every year, I honor the legacy of Dr. Martin Luther King, Jr. with my students at Towson University. Together, we read and reflect on his profound Letter from Birmingham Jail. Watching as students absorb Dr. King’s words and weave his lessons into their everyday lives inspires me.

Today reminds us of Dr. King's dream of an America where equality, justice, and opportunity prevailed for all. His fight for civil rights reshaped our nation, and his courage continues to guide us as we work toward a better future for us all.

As I begin serving as your representative in Congress, I carry Dr. King’s values with me. I am committed to fighting for racial justice, protecting voting rights, and uplifting communities by creating opportunities for every citizen.

This day is also a call to action to honor Dr. King’s legacy in our lives every day. I wish you a happy and reflective Dr. Martin Luther King Jr. Day!

Together,

Sarah Elfreth

* * *
| | [](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/100700008/523597434/-2051496126?nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMTIvMS85MTI0OSIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICI2ODFmODc4OC01ZmQ3LWVmMTEtODhkMC0wMDIyNDgyYTlkOTIiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=TfCT3oujUQnFACp82wgrJH-58zPTWYx92DcPOsnYHeU=&emci=c3090c7f-5fd7-ef11-88d0-0022482a9d92&emdi=681f8788-5fd7-ef11-88d0-0022482a9d92&ceid=30237190)

name: John Kasich
subject: We can make a difference together
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Kasich for America, not authorized by any
body:
Sharing with you my thoughts about Inauguration day| | | [](http://links.johnkasich.com/u/click?_t=6a3b28686d0a4d77958e12349305a4fe&_m=51dec9b74e744aeb93eb7532de69d10a&_e=9H72dpc-WLJSypaqxplgAZosC3ypZNprrDvBKsDjHz_cNLrQYnXCBEvIfMNGDjs1CvtYl3TUvXJegLcT6nBaFCgMKeXq1rZl2EebiZ77A2yJ4OOdXQR_zBvYUX43pRJaDbGODJ8NNMx8WQU72AEB5mVpAOnu-fFPE0gxY1RRZwJPh_zJRd00XrzKBp94J3fWfuLHUJRFhZhoU17NWZH8cw%3D%3D)
---
Peter, I wanted to take a moment to share a few updates with you and reflect on the significance of Inauguration Day.

Today is a sacred day in America as we come together to celebrate a peaceful transfer of power from one administration to the next. As Americans we should unite around this ongoing American experiment and hope for our shared success. Also today, we honor Dr. Martin Luther King Jr., and we are reminded that the power to create change lies within each of us. Dr. King knew that waiting on politicians to act was not enough - real change had to start in our homes, schools, churches, and communities. He brought people together, across racial and economic divides, to drive progress from the ground up. In today’s divided times, we must follow his example and take action where we live. Instead of waiting for Washington, let’s honor Dr. King’s legacy by stepping up and working together in our communities to make a difference. The solution is us. I’m grateful for your continued support as we work toward that goal.
---
|
---
In case you missed it, I joined NBC's Chuck Todd on his podcast recently to talk about the state of our nation. You can listen to the conversation on [iTunes](http://links.johnkasich.com/u/click?_t=6a3b28686d0a4d77958e12349305a4fe&_m=51dec9b74e744aeb93eb7532de69d10a&_e=9H72dpc-WLJSypaqxplgATmPuoPIRMtZ2d1Zy7wdUZhxT7172IOB6Vp3RA_TulixmUyPpEloNjgGA32GeGvO5NNazprbg98z0-u1V-6Opdc2Hs3RYfMVQ3C2hZF_1XiamqKZdmbhrrwdZzKq0aCQXwYGSG3q9EQ4dna0GyLwUSRAUhO1cloncTWkF2DZPsJNyZ4-g6MYDrgt1WfzMX2xPTnkM6QbeOEbvC9RfWkvn-8vekbHDw2pNuml1wPueoPAv8yL6j0FKlAKGgEQFHcv_WUWsn3cCJJWnT4uBQ356E5qmabL9foNfP3NJR_Gva5sxaGyCLpcqanSMpIdKsnSag%3D%3D), Spotify or wherever you get your podcasts.
---
[](http://links.johnkasich.com/u/click?_t=6a3b28686d0a4d77958e12349305a4fe&_m=51dec9b74e744aeb93eb7532de69d10a&_e=9H72dpc-WLJSypaqxplgATmPuoPIRMtZ2d1Zy7wdUZhxT7172IOB6Vp3RA_TulixmUyPpEloNjgGA32GeGvO5NNazprbg98z0-u1V-6Opdc2Hs3RYfMVQ3C2hZF_1XiamqKZdmbhrrwdZzKq0aCQXwYGSG3q9EQ4dna0GyLwUSTkiFk025pbmPdwnVRmhi1xD6f_jEFFLpOKuGO9cfp5O3gMEFaiiRrW5JeSYSt_K1i9Tujfc9tWk2HV61-wM8qUMBFB9yy0tcHNiBRF4gRJYu9f8Dr-jY9zcAswgfq0b6UQ2ULh20xh1i2MTg6LjCaMzBpyLvKbUmiLPItZ3BaS-w%3D%3D)
---
|
---
Recently, our nation came uncomfortably close to a government shutdown—another example of how our leaders are struggling to address the budget responsibly and tackle key issues facing our country. I shared my thoughts on this [in a column for The Hill](http://links.johnkasich.com/u/click?_t=6a3b28686d0a4d77958e12349305a4fe&_m=51dec9b74e744aeb93eb7532de69d10a&_e=9H72dpc-WLJSypaqxplgAdzrkdWv7ksAuSNsDUXjIjizCzS2VCV4Sd3NJnGYVUbQJnibCyLu2qy2ro5v4OfAfrOrhrWJ2IMccNgwCTUn1kEOjoyLpiM9t6pn9TEIZQZ-EQ_OOdmtVwhxMvsqe3NmiunPZFQk2t1yvfrAUqd_olFx-7xUdxpt4pmHieiKrrwD4tqKgXqTz4VBiJMSDrVy8T7GfY0QFeAGcxN81q4tsjfruGvtoIDOMADpPSNNlifUv_-NHqYeKGd3kYrQ1HVBVodHjYkjZaBn0Ywc7Suhopo%3D):
---
[](http://links.johnkasich.com/u/click?_t=6a3b28686d0a4d77958e12349305a4fe&_m=51dec9b74e744aeb93eb7532de69d10a&_e=9H72dpc-WLJSypaqxplgAdzrkdWv7ksAuSNsDUXjIjizCzS2VCV4Sd3NJnGYVUbQJnibCyLu2qy2ro5v4OfAfrOrhrWJ2IMccNgwCTUn1kEOjoyLpiM9t6pn9TEIZQZ-EQ_OOdmtVwhxMvsqe3Nmitln4R-lNQCXa6GYrdq9BxSHNfTWCGmB53YCjC1nyGYI6limjv6_amcm6DengwxeEiE0FeDmFhywCkvsFtOzquKuW5cGEKxKsU2I9-5PqrLceDVhBlDtr_fI1K6ZZjI6AQCHhbwGXI-pfbUCqTbCNIc%3D)
---
|

clean_body: Sharing with you my thoughts about Inauguration day| | | Peter, I wanted to take a moment to share a few updates with you and reflect on the significance of Inauguration Day. Today is a sacred day in America as we come together to celebrate a peaceful transfer of power from one administration to the next. As Americans we should unite around this ongoing American experiment and hope for our shared success. Also today, we honor Dr. Martin Luther King Jr., and we are reminded that the power to create change lies within each of us. Dr. King knew that waiting on politicians to act was not enough - real change had to start in our homes, schools, churches, and communities. He brought people together, across racial and economic divides, to drive progress from the ground up. In today’s divided times, we must follow his example and take action where we live. Instead of waiting for Washington, let’s honor Dr. King’s legacy by stepping up and working together in our communities to make a difference. The solution is us. I’m grateful for your continued support as we work toward that goal. | In case you missed it, I joined NBC's Chuck Todd on his podcast recently to talk about the state of our nation. You can listen to the conversation on , Spotify or wherever you get your podcasts. | Recently, our nation came uncomfortably close to a government shutdown—another example of how our leaders are struggling to address the budget responsibly and tackle key issues facing our country. I shared my thoughts on this : | Peter - I'm counting on your continued support to ensure that we can keep this important work moving forward. One of the realities is that we need to raise the funds to continue rallying support for this positive agenda. to help with this important cause and ensure that we can keep up the fight for a better America? I'm so appreciative of your ongoing support. Thank you. -John | | | Get the Latest Updates: | | | | ---|---|---|--- | | | | Paid for by Kasich for America, not authorized by any candidate or candidate's committee. www.JohnKasich.com

name: Katie Porter
subject: My advice for Democrats
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Truth to Power PAC
body:
# [](https://act.katieporter.com/go/9?t=1&akid=39395%2E3422442%2Ei0-lb7)
Today is a sad day for democracy and our country. But we can’t just look backwards or wallow about our losses.
While I’m not in office anymore, I served in Congress for two years under the Trump Administration. Using things like congressional hearings, I managed to get some of what we wanted, like free COVID testing at the start of the pandemic. Here are a few pieces of advice I’d give to elected Democrats as they navigate his second term.
1. **Stand up to the bullying and BS:** Just the other day, Trump threatened to hold California disaster relief funds hostage unless Congress passes a big bill with all of his pet projects—like tax cuts for billionaires. California fire victims should not be pawns in his games.
2. **Stay focused on the real people being harmed by Trump’s policies:** It’s easy for DC politicians to live and work in a DC bubble. But Members should stay focused on the actual effect of Trump’s policies. In a Congressional hearing during Trump’s last administration, I called out his consumer enforcement chief for not being able to define or calculate (or even estimate!) the cost of a payday loan. And I called out Ben Carson, his Housing and Urban Development Secretary, for confusing an _REO_ (a property owned by a bank after foreclosure), with an _OREO_ , a chocolate sandwich cookie. These moments weren’t about roasting people to go viral; they were about making sure we had people who knew enough about their job to be actually able to do the work. Sadly, in Trump’s last administration—and it seems his new one too—this was not the norm.
3. **We can work together without compromising our values:** Congress is short on good ideas. If someone–no matter the side of the aisle–has a good idea, we should act on it. If Republicans are serious about effectively rooting out waste and fraud in places like our defense budget, let’s do it.
4. **Speak truth to power:** It’s the right thing to do! When Trump does something abhorrent, we’ve got to call it out, even when it feels exhausting. I won re-election in my swing district even as I called out Trump and pushed for bold change, because voters knew I called out BS and fought for them.

Thanks for reading and for being a part of our movement.
Katie Porter

clean_body: Today is a sad day for democracy and our country. But we can’t just look backwards or wallow about our losses. While I’m not in office anymore, I served in Congress for two years under the Trump Administration. Using things like congressional hearings, I managed to get some of what we wanted, like free COVID testing at the start of the pandemic. Here are a few pieces of advice I’d give to elected Democrats as they navigate his second term. 1. Stand up to the bullying and BS: Just the other day, Trump threatened to hold California disaster relief funds hostage unless Congress passes a big bill with all of his pet projects—like tax cuts for billionaires. California fire victims should not be pawns in his games. 2. Stay focused on the real people being harmed by Trump’s policies: It’s easy for DC politicians to live and work in a DC bubble. But Members should stay focused on the actual effect of Trump’s policies. In a Congressional hearing during Trump’s last administration, I called out his consumer enforcement chief for not being able to define or calculate (or even estimate!) the cost of a payday loan. And I called out Ben Carson, his Housing and Urban Development Secretary, for confusing an REO (a property owned by a bank after foreclosure), with an OREO , a chocolate sandwich cookie. These moments weren’t about roasting people to go viral; they were about making sure we had people who knew enough about their job to be actually able to do the work. Sadly, in Trump’s last administration—and it seems his new one too—this was not the norm. 3. We can work together without compromising our values: Congress is short on good ideas. If someone–no matter the side of the aisle–has a good idea, we should act on it. If Republicans are serious about effectively rooting out waste and fraud in places like our defense budget, let’s do it. 4. Speak truth to power: It’s the right thing to do! When Trump does something abhorrent, we’ve got to call it out, even when it feels exhausting. I won re-election in my swing district even as I called out Trump and pushed for bold change, because voters knew I called out BS and fought for them. Thanks for reading and for being a part of our movement. Katie Porter Katie Porter started Truth to Power to support Democrats who reject corporate PAC money and are running to protect the environment, restore the nationwide right to an abortion, and create an economy that works for working people. Paid for by Truth to Power PAC

body:
The longer Trump holds office, the longer the list will grow. So we have to do everything we can to ensure Trump’s agenda doesn’t trickle down to the states – we need to act immediately. | | |
---
| |
---|---
**We’ll keep this short, but as a grassroots Democrat living in your state, you need to know what Donald Trump’s top priorities are as president:** Creating tax policies that **favor his right-wing billionaire allies and leave working people with a larger burden**
Changing public education to **enforce ultra-conservative values under the guise of "parental rights"**
Appointing federal judges that will undermine our constitutional protections and **weaken civil rights for vulnerable communities**
Dismantling the Affordable Care Act, **leaving _millions_ without affordable health care coverage **

name: Brad Finstad
subject: A Truly Historic Moment is Upon Us
body:
| | | |
---
Today, we celebrated a moment unlike any other in United States history, the return of Donald Trump to the White House!
This is more than an inauguration; it’s a once-in-a-lifetime event that marks the beginning of a new chapter in our nation’s story.
Only one other president in history, Grover Cleveland, has ever served nonconsecutive terms.
Donald Trump’s return is a testament to the enduring strength of the America First movement and the unwavering support of millions who believe in restoring our Republic.
This unique moment is about more than just one man; it’s about the bold policies, the vision, and the energy that transformed our nation during Trump’s first term. Now, we have the chance to finish what we started, to build on that foundation, and to bring renewed hope to Americans everywhere. The excitement is strong, and the future is bright. Together, we’ve made history, so let’s celebrate this incredible milestone and prepare for what’s to come under another Trump presidency!
---
---
Brad FinstadMinnesota's 1st Congressional District
---
| |
---
|
---
|

name: Matt, GOP
subject: Trump’s second term is here!
disclaimer_text: PAID FOR BY ROYCE WHITE FOR SENATE
body:
We crushed the Radical Left. Now it's time to set the agenda and lead America to greatness.
|
Peter,

**🇺🇲 President Donald J. Trump is back where he belongs - in the White House.** But don't be fooled. The radical left isn't going away quietly.

The woke mob and their cowardly allies in the industrial media complex are scrambling to rewrite the narrative. The RINOs and neocons who betrayed America First Patriots are trying to sneak back into relevance. **They didn 't learn a thing from their failures, but _WE DID._ **

This movement is about you. It's about the American people who stood firm in the face of relentless attacks and refused to give up this country.

_Now it 's time to show the world that our best days are ahead._

**We need to hear from you.** [**What are you most looking forward to in President Trump 's second term?**](https://links.iterable.com/u/click?_t=ce1ce9e616484205a28a45cfaf96cecf&_m=f4ffaab5c853439cbb7cfaca5d8662fe&_e=pwipDzAQhpklNhFRss9qdFlBsz-SS_ooOMNb7VMboK7A3cU4rqqL9vCTxbYfdRvMiYCcAWc4UDOMs6NUl6wzi2hqNx6HsBubxBG3YXuFQzsLbYhQkEu3qjTthp4fj7OYXQHoQ-5qyPLh7VG-jJqcjBepKL7ne8q_BpAAyS0GpISnhinIN7mJ0LQMxItdKMzu8YljXSquxSQR1wltR2b-ypEE2atvg7ftygELIxQYxuEegKxiqWvOR6LWgKvQdixCadEJKwUQ8U4dbH3z_jvX-P6PtQAujPhksFpQZiV-D2m66zAcybYl-NDe6rmmBWiF4F011GO-mjimtpllocvhcQ%3D%3D)

---

Together, we will restore law and order, rid Washington of corrupt bureaucrats, and rebuild America. And the left is no match for the power of our movement.

[ **ANSWER BEFORE MIDNIGHT** ](https://links.iterable.com/u/click?_t=ce1ce9e616484205a28a45cfaf96cecf&_m=f4ffaab5c853439cbb7cfaca5d8662fe&_e=pwipDzAQhpklNhFRss9qdFlBsz-SS_ooOMNb7VMboK7A3cU4rqqL9vCTxbYfdRvMiYCcAWc4UDOMs6NUl6wzi2hqNx6HsBubxBG3YXuFQzsLbYhQkEu3qjTthp4fj7OYXQHoQ-5qyPLh7VG-jJqcjBepKL7ne8q_BpAAyS0GpISnhinIN7mJ0LQMxItdKMzuV3YL1p9jksCDY38F1COVNhshEQpuY6bh-A8S7aiKlSYk5HEMYMWx8cmLDgtBZX-TcbS1iXlPIsMXex86gI43Kuts6r1WK_lX1p3mNvS-ave17nEJmqOujBd3unZPtUJjuaD-jqT-Qd-f94h7JIfyPA%3D%3D)

Thank you,
Matt, GOP

name: Gay Valimont
subject: Fighting Corruption, Protecting Your Tax Dollars
disclaimer_text: Paid For By Gay Valimont Gay Valimont For Congress
body:
| | |
---
Good morning/afternoon, This is Gay Valimont, running for FL-1, and ashamed Floridian: **COVID-19 may be over, but there is still a plague within our institutions.** The recent indictment of former State Rep. Carolina Amesty for COVID-19 relief fraud is yet another reminder of how corruption erodes trust in our leaders. When elected officials misuse funds meant to help struggling families and businesses, **it’s not just theft—it’s a betrayal of the people they swore to serve. It is up to you and I to put an end to this.**
---
---
Hardworking families are still recovering from the economic impacts of the pandemic. Every dollar of COVID-19 relief was critical to keeping food on tables, roofs over heads, and small businesses afloat. Corruption like this is deeply personal to the communities that were left behind during the pandemic, and we need leaders that will not steal from them. I believe your tax dollars should go to improving lives, not lining the pockets of corrupt politicians. From greater transparency in relief programs to stronger oversight of government spending, I’ll fight to ensure every cent is used for its intended purpose: **helping you. But time is running out, special elections are April 1st.** **[Donating $8 to my campaign today can put us on track for April 1st. We must get corrupt officials out of office, and put those with YOUR best interest in. Let’s SAVE OUR GOVERNMENT.](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/100700461/523599234/181761264?nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQNTkvMS8xMDc3NDYiLA0KICAiRGlzdHJpYnV0aW9uVW5pcXVlSWQiOiAiNDk0NjhlYzAtNjBkNy1lZjExLTg4ZDAtMDAyMjQ4MmE5ZDkyIiwNCiAgIkVtYWlsQWRkcmVzcyI6ICJkcHdpbGxpczY3QGdtYWlsLmNvbSINCn0%3D&hmac=IS5cDxGk4vMGs41tEg1v50zgqi0UoODSKmTv6GwDJIk=)**

disclaimer_text: Paid for by Democratic Association of Secretaries of State, Treasurer Denise Merrill

body:
And thwart the MAGA agenda

Friend,

Today, America First fighter JD Vance will make history as the first millennial and first Marine Corps veteran to become Vice President of the United States.

We couldn't be more excited for President Trump and JD to Make America Great again, but there's just one problem: **[In 2026, there will be a hotly contested special election in Ohio to fill the Senate seat JD is vacating, and Democrats are sure to spend MILLIONS to try and buy the election.](https://links.conservativesgive.com/u/click?_t=959d0fa54cad47baa4213176314ae733&_m=7adf99479de34f94acd850e21be2854f&_e=V7tF33ME8m64J3g_z2FO1yrsN2bKZrb9qznYj3opHDmqZZqVN_gBdnfLMo2Le0cFqjPqlH98xaqjWW4op_2uboZ6zjhpgsCRTRW9nDmvFR31yR9rxfHe5BUEJ9Rv_53MC8m65YZzL6FG0KN9MN_zcVoXohi0Nbh__SVf1oP5vfQFPHCiFiRiFeUvavwpxZ6YyOh7WsRr3iWtIDyL-Zr1aWbUNDMY3yfqFHTtKCGxavGRAD2pEOOx8F2enWQj6LGc7giXdoWfGPTVS5sekGo0Tg4J3c1CEwltHZqFH9GVHdj9vP_rwJoezMlTuZHEX5r700xDyY1b3WNGd6-m6GAt8TUcOQo3_HDPQLw2Ku4EaaX8AtQVypAWCsvWGTouQkC39t-JN622NI0WGQax7kwOD3ogiKb69r3kY28G53dbKYdbQZTPH7Pt8CKzh4qgpKjq5DclvunNUnXR57ylwbBU-A%3D%3D)**
_Rumor has it, far-Left politician Sherod Brown might even run in the special election, which would be a NIGHTMARE for our Trump Senate Caucus._
President Trump and JD Vance are counting on a strong Majority in the Senate to enact their agenda over the next four years. But if we fail to defend Ohio, there's almost **[no chance](https://links.conservativesgive.com/u/click?_t=959d0fa54cad47baa4213176314ae733&_m=7adf99479de34f94acd850e21be2854f&_e=V7tF33ME8m64J3g_z2FO1yrsN2bKZrb9qznYj3opHDmqZZqVN_gBdnfLMo2Le0cFqjPqlH98xaqjWW4op_2uboZ6zjhpgsCRTRW9nDmvFR31yR9rxfHe5BUEJ9Rv_53MC8m65YZzL6FG0KN9MN_zcVoXohi0Nbh__SVf1oP5vfQFPHCiFiRiFeUvavwpxZ6YyOh7WsRr3iWtIDyL-Zr1aWbUNDMY3yfqFHTtKCGxavGRAD2pEOOx8F2enWQj6LGc7giXdoWfGPTVS5sekGo0Tg4J3c1CEwltHZqFH9GVHdj9vP_rwJoezMlTuZHEX5r7-efP_ELhRW1FKvhgeCMEYmiA5XLzN8NLnBr18z8q6Uv6SXSkmNcPe3Lvwfsk6K4aBX28kdnKH7AKUapK9G6Ud4DyvhMLD75XHcQfXfisbxPHT-zPocrVaypATKqHYZ27BYvGlks_cQLoZG6mxyZp_A%3D%3D)** of passing crucial legislation to save our country.

clean_body: And thwart the MAGA agenda Friend, Today, America First fighter JD Vance will make history as the first millennial and first Marine Corps veteran to become Vice President of the United States. We couldn't be more excited for President Trump and JD to Make America Great again, but there's just one problem: Rumor has it, far-Left politician Sherod Brown might even run in the special election, which would be a NIGHTMARE for our Trump Senate Caucus. President Trump and JD Vance are counting on a strong Majority in the Senate to enact their agenda over the next four years. But if we fail to defend Ohio, there's almost of passing crucial legislation to save our country. body:
We're helping to build a warchest of funds for the two Democrats who run for these open Florida seats - we need your urgent help!
Chip in NOW so we can flip these red seats blue >>> [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/rtp-floridaspecials?refcode=111624_2]
Peter, we’ve just received breaking news out of Florida:
Governor Ron DeSantis has officially called on the Secretary of State to schedule TWO special elections on a RAPIDLY accelerated timeline.
To put it simply: Democrats now have a limited time to prepare to win TWO MORE HOUSE SEATS being vacated by Trump cabinet appointments
These special elections will determine the future of FL-01 , vacated by Matt Gaetz (reportedly stepping up as Trump’s pick for Attorney General), and FL-06 , soon to be left by Mike Waltz (rumored to become Trump’s National Security Advisor).
Flipping these suits blue IS possible - but only if we start preparing NOW. Will you help us build up a warchest of funding to rush to the Democrats who run for these seats? [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/rtp-floridaspecials?refcode=111624_2]
If you've saved your payment information with ActBlue Express, your donation to The Real Texas PAC will go through immediately: DONATE $5 [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/rtp-floridaspecials?express_lane=true&refcode=111624_2&amount=5]
DONATE $10 [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/rtp-floridaspecials?express_lane=true&refcode=111624_2&amount=10]
DONATE $50 [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/rtp-floridaspecials?express_lane=true&refcode=111624_2&amount=50]
DONATE $100 [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/rtp-floridaspecials?express_lane=true&refcode=111624_2&amount=100]
DONATE $250 [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/rtp-floridaspecials?express_lane=true&refcode=111624_2&amount=250]
Other Amount [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/rtp-floridaspecials?refcode=111624_2&amount=25]
Make no mistake: DeSantis and his allies are trying to fast-track these elections to catch Democrats off guard.
They know they need to secure these seats quickly to maintain their MAGA control of Congress.
We CANNOT let them get away with it!
Chip in NOW so we can have a stash of funds ready to go when Democrats put up a candidate for these seats >>> [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/rtp-floridaspecials?refcode=111624_2]
Thanks for taking action,
Team RTP

Married couples may together give a total of $10,000 per calendar year. Federal political action committees (PACs) can contribute up to $5,000 per calendar year. The first $5,000 contributed in a calendar year from federally permissible sources will be deposited in The Real Texas PAC's contribution account. subject: We can transform American politics.
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Early Vote Action PAC](https://go
body:
I’ve got a supporters’ only memo about the future for you

| | | | |
---
Friend – Thanks to the efforts by my group Early Vote Action, **_we delivered Pennsylvania for President Trump, and helped to secure the 2024 election._**
Now that the election is behind us, and we know just how much Early Vote Action and the great Americans who work with us are capable of, I’d say it’s time to think even bigger: **It’s time to start flipping blue states for the GOP.**

[THERE’S NO TIME TO WASTE - LET’S GET BACK TO WORK >>>](https://go.vbtrb.com/r/9QAT/?__vbtrk=MjcwNjcyOjEwMjk5MDQwNjpuZXdzbGV0dGVy&_uax=MjcwNjcyOjEwMjk5MDQwNg&ct)
Make no mistake: The Democrats aren’t just going to go away to lick their wounds after this humiliating defeat.
As I write this, they are already recruiting veteran political strategists, contacting billionaire special interest donors, and laying the groundwork to take back the Congress in 2026, and the White House in 2028.
**__**The simple fact of the matter is that, if we don’t start _**working**_ **_right now_** , they will have a huge advantage over us in the next election.

[THERE’S NO TIME TO WASTE - LET’S GET BACK TO WORK >>>](https://go.vbtrb.com/r/9QAT/?__vbtrk=MjcwNjcyOjEwMjk5MDQwNjpuZXdzbGV0dGVy&_uax=MjcwNjcyOjEwMjk5MDQwNg&ct)
**With your support, Early Vote Action can take the great work that we did in 2024 and use it as the bedrock to _fundamentally transform the American political landscape for decades to come_!**
Here’s what I believe we can accomplish with your generous support:

* We could **FLIP NJ RED** and create a clearer and easier path to victory for future Republicans than ever before.

* We could **SOLIDIFY PENNSYLVANIA AS A REPUBLICAN STRONGHOLD ,** and guarantee one of the seven swing states will never be a fight again.

* We could **MAINTAIN THE GOP HOUSE MAJORITY** , so future Republican Presidents don’t need to worry about their agenda being foiled by a Democrat House majority

[LET’S TRANSFORM THE AMERICAN POLITICAL LANDSCAPE](https://go.vbtrb.com/r/9QAT/?__vbtrk=MjcwNjcyOjEwMjk5MDQwNjpuZXdzbGV0dGVy&_uax=MjcwNjcyOjEwMjk5MDQwNg&ct)
EVA’s track record is proven...
And after the work we did to deliver Pennsylvania for President Trump, the sky is the limit...
**_But to get there, I desperately need your support._**
**__[Can I count on you](https://go.vbtrb.com/r/9QAT/?__vbtrk=MjcwNjcyOjEwMjk5MDQwNjpuZXdzbGV0dGVy&_uax=MjcwNjcyOjEwMjk5MDQwNg&ct)_?_**

Yours Sincerely,

Scott PreslerFounder, Early Vote Action

[](https://go.vbtrb.com/r/9QAT/?__vbtrk=MjcwNjcyOjEwMjk5MDQwNjpuZXdzbGV0dGVy&_uax=MjcwNjcyOjEwMjk5MDQwNg&ct)

name: Ellen R. Malcolm, EMILYs List
subject: Reasons for hope...
body:
|
---
[](https://click.everyaction.com/k/100700923/523600668/-1491281772?refcode=WUEFE128_20250120_ERMRS_PL&sourceid=1055601&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9UU00vVFNNRUwvMS84NzA2OSIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICI5ZGQ3MjljYi02MGQ3LWVmMTEtODhkMC0wMDIyNDgyYTlkOTIiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=rhXzsABqIvC7KsHrMaX7aqvvEu2nGWoetBWf-iYH5Z0=) Peter,

As we brace ourselves for the chaos Trump will bring back to the White House, here's a reminder:

The rage and sadness we felt after last year's presidential election is only part of the story. Finding opportunity in defeat is what has kept me doing this work for 40 years and counting.

Here's what I know for sure: We're not going to sit by while Trump tries to destroy our democracy. We will capitalize on our anger, we will fight back, and we will win.

**I want to tell you a story from the early days of EMILYs List that I've been thinking about a lot lately. It's about defeat, hope, and what comes next:**

In 1992, in the wake of Anita Hill's bravery during Clarence Thomas' Supreme Court hearings, women nationwide rose up. They were sick and tired of the harassment and discrimination they'd been forced to endure at work for decades. They demanded real political power. _**And they turned out in _historic numbers_ to elect Democratic women.**_

EMILYs List's membership grew from 3,000 to 24,000 that year. We raised $6.5 million for our candidates. And we elected four Democratic women to the U.S. Senate and 20 to the U.S. House. It was an extraordinary, record-shattering year. And I'll be honest, Peter — after all that success, we felt a little invincible. We thought we could accomplish _anything_.

And then came 1994.

Newt Gingrich rolled out the GOP's "Contract with America" legislative platform, seeking to nationalize the midterm elections. And it worked. Republicans picked up 54 U.S. House seats and 10 U.S. Senate seats, taking control of both chambers. We lost some of the women we had just worked _so hard_ to elect in 1992.

It was dubbed the "Republican Revolution." And in the aftermath, all the media wanted to talk about was how voters were sick of Democrats, how we had alienated the white men in our base, and how the American people no longer believed Democrats could deliver for them. It was a real moment of reckoning for our party. (Sound familiar?)

So here's what we did: We doubled down. We launched our "Boot Newt" campaign to combat the new speaker's extremist agenda, and we fought voter apathy by going straight to the press to let Americans know exactly what we stood for. We created the EMILYs List major donor program, which has gone on to raise hundreds of millions of dollars. We built a powerful political program that directly assists our women in launching and running their campaigns. And we launched Women Vote, our massively successful independent expenditure arm, which has helped elect hundreds of Democratic pro-choice women over the past 30 years.

**1992 is the year that put EMILYs List on the map. But if it weren't for the losses of 1994, EMILYs List may not have become the powerhouse institution it is today.**

The business of making real social change through electoral politics isn't easy, and it isn't linear. I call it "leaping and creeping" — we make big leaps forward, and then we face some tough losses, and then we creep forward again, win by win. As we celebrate 40 years of EMILYs List, I'm here to tell you, Peter: _We're always moving forward_. Even when we lose. Even when it feels like all is lost. _Over time, we are making real and lasting change_. That's the story of EMILYs List, it's the story of the Democratic Party, and it's the story of America.

There's no way around it: We experienced a devastating loss at the top of the ticket in November. The Trump administration poses an existential threat to our rights and our democracy. And while there are many reasons for fear, outrage, and disappointment, there are just as many reasons for hope. Together, we will rebuild and recover. The next leap is around the corner.

Thank you for being with EMILYs List, today and always.

Ellen Malcolm
Founder, EMILYs List

**[P.S.: If you're ready to help EMILYs List chart a new path forward, please donate today:](https://click.everyaction.com/k/100700924/523600669/-1491281772?refcode=WUEFE128_20250120_ERMRS_PL&sourceid=1055601&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9UU00vVFNNRUwvMS84NzA2OSIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICI5ZGQ3MjljYi02MGQ3LWVmMTEtODhkMC0wMDIyNDgyYTlkOTIiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=rhXzsABqIvC7KsHrMaX7aqvvEu2nGWoetBWf-iYH5Z0=)** | [DONATE](https://click.everyaction.com/k/100700925/523600670/-1491281772?refcode=WUEFE128_20250120_ERMRS_PL&sourceid=1055601&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9UU00vVFNNRUwvMS84NzA2OSIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICI5ZGQ3MjljYi02MGQ3LWVmMTEtODhkMC0wMDIyNDgyYTlkOTIiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=rhXzsABqIvC7KsHrMaX7aqvvEu2nGWoetBWf-iYH5Z0=)

name: Dr. Raul Ruiz
subject: History will look back on us
disclaimer_text: PAID FOR BY DR. RAUL RUIZ FOR CONGRESS COMMITTEE
body:
Donald Trump has officially been inaugurated as our 47th President.

While Washington remains gridlocked, Dr. Raul Ruiz is committed to serving and helping constituents with the problems they have with the federal government and cutting red tape. Today marks an incredibly consequential day in American history. Donald Trump has officially been inaugurated as our 47th President, and now heads to the White House to begin what he has promised to be four years of political division, cuts to critical governmental agencies and programs, and continued attacks on the values of progress and opportunity we collectively hold. From threatening to use the power of the federal government to target his political rivals to unprecedented gutting of services the American people rely on, Donald Trump is prepared to make use of his cabinet and Republican Congress chock full of MAGA loyalists to execute his far-right Project 2025 agenda. It goes without saying that the impact of this day will have consequences for generations to come. This is a moment that will be looked at not only for what got us here — but what we decide to do in the face of such adversity. I’m committed to defending our vision of equality and justice for all — not just a select few billionaires calling the shots in Washington. But I need to know that you’re with me in this fight. Donations of $10 from everyday Americans are what fuel our movement. name: Gene Karpinski, LCV
subject: [3X MATCH:] Today is Donald Trump’s inauguration. His attacks on the environment and democracy have already started.
body:
To view this mail in a browser, copy https://links.messages.lcv.org/servlet/MailView?ms=MTIwMjM4NjAS1&r=NjA3MDU0MTAzNTc3S0&j=MjE0MDI1MTE1MAS2&mt=1&rt=0 into your browser.
Peter — Today is Martin Luther King Jr. Day, a day celebrating a leader
who devoted his life to promoting and protecting civil rights for all people.
Yet today, with Donald Trump’s inauguration, that civil rights work is in
jeopardy. We need all hands on deck to protect our environment and our democracy for all. Access to clean air, clean water, and participation in the democratic process
should be a civil right afforded to all. But Trump is promising an onslaught of executive actions over the next few days to overturn environmental protections that keep our communities safe, throw open public lands and waters to drilling and mining operations, reverse progress to make voting more accessible to all citizens, and allow more climate- and health-harming pollutioninto the air we breathe and the water we drink. These harmful proposals will be
most immediately damaging to low-wealth communities and communities of color who
are already experiencing disproportionate harm from pollution, voter
suppression, and climate change. LCV’s Membership Drive is happening right now. This fundraising campaign
determines how far we can go to stop Trump’s actions, right now and for all of
2025. For a limited time, a group of donors has agreed to TRIPLE MATCH all gifts,
which means your support can go 3X as far to protect the environment and our
democracy. Meeting our Membership Drive goal is critical to LCV’s work for the whole year, and we haven’t heard from you yet. The best thing you can do right now to oppose Trump’s attacks on the environment is to renew your LCV membership for 2025 — right now, $5 is all it takes. https://links.messages.lcv. Here’s where our Membership Drive stands right now: LEAGUE OF CONSERVATION VOTERS
2025 MEMBERSHIP DRIVE

Goal: $50,000

Progress to Goal: 38%
Deadline: 11:59 pm, January 31, 2025
Match Status: TRIPLE MATCH ACTIVE https://links.messages.lcv.org/els/v2/ebDKc8k_

FOR IMMEDIATE REVIEW
Supporter Record: Peter Willis
DONATED TO JANUARY MEMBERSHIP DRIVE? NOT YET

Member Status: PENDING — Renew your membership now for just $5 >> https://links.messages.lcv.org/els/v2/w_ We’ve fought Trump’s dirty agenda before... and won real progress for people and
the planet. During his first term, our movement blocked harmful pipelines, saved
public lands, and pushed forward clean energy, EVEN with Trump in office. And we were able to protect our environment thanks to support from people like YOU,
Peter. To defend our progress and do even more in 2025, we really need to raise $50,000
before this triple match offer expires on January 31. If everyone reading this
right now were to pitch in, we’d blow through that goal in minutes. We can only stand up to Trump and protect environmental progress together.

clean_body: To view this mail in a browser, copy into your browser. Peter — Today is Martin Luther King Jr. Day, a day celebrating a leader who devoted his life to promoting and protecting civil rights for all people. Yet today, with Donald Trump’s inauguration, that civil rights work is in jeopardy. We need all hands on deck to protect our environment and our democracy for all. Access to clean air, clean water, and participation in the democratic process should be a civil right afforded to all. But Trump is promising an onslaught of executive actions over the next few days to overturn environmental protections that keep our communities safe, throw open public lands and waters to drilling and mining operations, reverse progress to make voting more accessible to all citizens, and allow more climate- and health-harming pollutioninto the air we breathe and the water we drink. These harmful proposals will be most immediately damaging to low-wealth communities and communities of color who are already experiencing disproportionate harm from pollution, voter suppression, and climate change. LCV’s Membership Drive is happening right now. This fundraising campaign determines how far we can go to stop Trump’s actions, right now and for all of 2025. For a limited time, a group of donors has agreed to TRIPLE MATCH all gifts, which means your support can go 3X as far to protect the environment and our democracy. Meeting our Membership Drive goal is critical to LCV’s work for the whole year, and we haven’t heard from you yet. The best thing you can do right now to oppose Trump’s attacks on the environment is to renew your LCV membership for 2025 — right now, $5 is all it takes. Here’s where our Membership Drive stands right now: LEAGUE OF CONSERVATION VOTERS 2025 MEMBERSHIP DRIVE Goal: $50,000 Progress to Goal: 38% Deadline: 11:59 pm, January 31, 2025 Match Status: TRIPLE MATCH ACTIVE FOR IMMEDIATE REVIEW Supporter Record: Peter Willis DONATED TO JANUARY MEMBERSHIP DRIVE? NOT YET Member Status: PENDING — Renew your membership now for just $5 >> We’ve fought Trump’s dirty agenda before... and won real progress for people and the planet. During his first term, our movement blocked harmful pipelines, saved public lands, and pushed forward clean energy, EVEN with Trump in office. And we were able to protect our environment thanks to support from people like YOU, Peter. To defend our progress and do even more in 2025, we really need to raise $50,000 before this triple match offer expires on January 31. If everyone reading this right now were to pitch in, we’d blow through that goal in minutes. We can only stand up to Trump and protect environmental progress together. Support from people like you this year will make the difference in whether we succeed or fail. Please, renew your LCV membership for 2025 — right now, $5 is all it takes. Thanks for standing with us, Peter. It’s because of people like you that we’re able to keep fighting to protect people, our rights, and our planet. Onward, Gene Karpinski President League of Conservation Voters This email was sent to dpwillis67@gmail.com. name: Tim Kaine
subject: My thoughts as President Trump’s second term officially begins:
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Kaine for Virginia
body:
https://secure.actblue.com/donate/em-cgp-2025 [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/em-cgp-2025]
Friends,
It’s official — President Trump has been inaugurated as the 47th President of the United States, and his second term has begun.
Let’s start here: The peaceful transfer of power remains one of the most powerful symbols of our great democracy.
Today, I wish President Trump and Vice President Vance the best as they take on this monumental duty. I hold a deep and sincere hope that they will be successful for all Americans.
I said in 2017 that my motto during the first Trump term would be: Advance everywhere we can, defend everything we must . As I continue to advocate for the interests of Virginia in the Senate, that remains my goal and mantra.
No matter what you’re feeling right now — fear, anger, hope, anxiety, despair, determination — know that there are still many strong and committed leaders in Washington who are determined to fight for you, and to fight for the most vulnerable among us.
Whatever challenges or successes lie ahead for our country, the fact is that we will face them together, as one people, ultimately united by our shared beliefs in the core values this country was founded on — democracy and freedom.
I am an optimist by nature, but that doesn’t mean I’m not also a realist. I fought hard to prevent today’s events, and like you, I remain disappointed that we didn’t achieve what we’d hoped in last year’s elections.
But the page has been turned, and I am honored to do my Constitutionally-mandated duty: To support and defend the Constitution of the United States against all enemies, foreign and domestic; to bear true faith and allegiance to the same; and to well and faithfully discharge the duties of the office to which I have been elected by the great people of Virginia.
Doing that duty will require fighting against this Administration, to be sure. It will also require working with it when we can, and finding compromises where we can.
As we begin this new chapter, I am so grateful to have this team by my side. Your support, your encouragement, your wisdom, and your friendship are invaluable.
I’ll leave you with this great quote from Martin Luther King, Jr. that I’m reflecting on today: “We must accept finite disappointment, but never lose infinite hope.” A fitting sentiment.
Onward,
Tim Kaine

name: Becca Balint
subject: Choosing courage over comfort
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Becca Balint for Vermont**
body:
Today, I sat with other members of Congress and watched as Donald Trump was sworn in as the 47th President of the United States. | [](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/100701431/523602518/-785282667?refcode=AU_RAB_CL_GEN_NOA_AC_20250120_PM1_V1_S1&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMTAvMS84MTM0MiIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICJlNWNmY2E2Ny02M2Q3LWVmMTEtODhkMC0wMDIyNDgyYTlkOTIiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=qJmsyWhpzUs6SJXSkUvIq75HP4HnG_0q50G-JNOJpnA=)
---
Today, I sat with other members of Congress and watched as Donald Trump was sworn in as the 47th President of the United States. I didn’t enjoy it. But I felt I needed to be there. My presence and that of my Democratic colleagues is a living embodiment of what Dr. Martin Luther King, Jr. said over 60 years ago: _“The ultimate measure of a man is not where he stands in moments of comfort and convenience,**but where he stands at times of challenge and controversy.** ”_ Donald Trump refused to attend President Biden’s inauguration. But I attended as a reminder that, in our democracy, we don’t only attend the peaceful transition of power when our candidate wins._I’m taking a stand for the rule of law and our Constitution._ Unlike all the election deniers who attended today, including the man who was sworn in, I have the peace, strength, and courage that comes from knowing I’m not beholden to anyone except the Vermonters I serve in Congress. I'm facing the next four years with clarity of purpose, a clear conscience, and a deep commitment to doing whatever I can to protect our democracy and continue the meaningful, consequential work on behalf of my constituents to make life better for them and for Americans across the nation. I’m choosing to move forward with courage not because I’m blind to the dangers we face, but because this moment requires calm, focus, and a sense of purpose. Thank you for being in my corner, Peter. Becca Balint [](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/100701432/523602519/724160491?refcode=AU_RAB_CL_GEN_NOA_AC_20250120_PM1_V1_S1&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMTAvMS84MTM0MiIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICJlNWNmY2E2Ny02M2Q3LWVmMTEtODhkMC0wMDIyNDgyYTlkOTIiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=qJmsyWhpzUs6SJXSkUvIq75HP4HnG_0q50G-JNOJpnA=) | [DONATE](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/100701433/523602520/724160491?refcode=AU_RAB_CL_GEN_NOA_AC_20250120_PM1_V1_S1&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMTAvMS84MTM0MiIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICJlNWNmY2E2Ny02M2Q3LWVmMTEtODhkMC0wMDIyNDgyYTlkOTIiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=qJmsyWhpzUs6SJXSkUvIq75HP4HnG_0q50G-JNOJpnA=)

My presence and that of my Democratic colleagues is a living embodiment of what Dr. Martin Luther King, Jr. said over 60 years ago: “The ultimate measure of a man is not where he stands in moments of comfort and convenience,but where he stands at times of challenge and controversy. ” Donald Trump refused to attend President Biden’s inauguration. Unlike all the election deniers who attended today, including the man who was sworn in, I have the peace, strength, and courage that comes from knowing I’m not beholden to anyone except the Vermonters I serve in Congress. I'm facing the next four years with clarity of purpose, a clear conscience, and a deep commitment to doing whatever I can to protect our democracy and continue the meaningful, consequential work on behalf of my constituents to make life better for them and for Americans across the nation. I’m choosing to move forward with courage not because I’m blind to the dangers we face, but because this moment requires calm, focus, and a sense of purpose. Thank you for being in my corner, Peter. Becca Balint = | = mailto:beccabalintvt@beccabalint.com | Paid for by Becca Balint for Vermont P.O. Box 291 Burlington, VT 05402

Folks, Donald Trump has officially taken office for the second time and is our nation’s 47th president. **There’s no question about it: Our democracy WILL be tested over the next four years.** Now in control of the presidency and Congress, the far-right has made their intentions clear. Voting rights are in danger – from restrictive court cases, gerrymandering through redistricting, and much more. At Every Eligible American, we remain steadfast in our mission to make sure everyone who is able to vote, can vote, up and down the ballot. While there’s no way to know what will happen over these next few years, we’re preparing now for the battles to come. This fight is personal for me. As a public school teacher, I’ve spent my career fighting for kids who need someone in their corner. Now, I’m fighting for all of us—to protect education, to defend democracy, and to build a future free from MAGA extremism.
If you’re with me, here’s what I need:

name: Hillsdale College via Reclaiming American Freedom
subject: How do you see the Supreme Court?
body:
You have been selected by Hillsdale College to represent your state in a National Referendum on Religious Liberty.

This sponsored message from Reclaiming American Freedom is on behalf of Hillsdale College [](http://links.reclamingamericanfreedom.com/u/click?_t=5ec072595b1347f09b4bac0de82ea87d&_m=3ff7096718c14b15997071b5ec2055e8&_e=BnwjdBGuzBG8Exnb90Ta3BAYBiuvVPy3Vmq_nimAOQ-qE8Sco4u3t4e1zSWXmY6zq-hkmEx1QvlnCh8FXbtXIf8NllLJfS2NmMjbvRjbnbnca_K9rvngAhpWyJCmToLZsGP7iEFjgkE6PFBbHFCVSjYiLj15OfvRA57AMPVR45-zNTRNWpqvpnIyblwgkGyCi45ZwMQBOgwdrBaWZi9SelKBNKz0tGZw9fxyzI5dHJR0fqdVHg8ZOdsZvguzFb2hkzN-06hIGgO9iW-cOmSCMZewXlVsQhISpI6qih6n-PkDOwvGJOaYbJr4-PHs9iAkjUndtWqqWyzL1Af9ruEkCAVF99XSUO-Z3yS4h93F9yCCR-KpzXR3x9RAbanIbcj2-LMJG-GRql7yJKSa7ZPTZ-14SoJMyGvvCFqEAKf3Uag%3D) Dear Friend, You have been selected by Hillsdale College to represent your state in a **National Referendum on Religious Liberty.** [](http://links.reclamingamericanfreedom.com/u/click?_t=5ec072595b1347f09b4bac0de82ea87d&_m=3ff7096718c14b15997071b5ec2055e8&_e=BnwjdBGuzBG8Exnb90Ta3BAYBiuvVPy3Vmq_nimAOQ-qE8Sco4u3t4e1zSWXmY6zq-hkmEx1QvlnCh8FXbtXIf8NllLJfS2NmMjbvRjbnbnca_K9rvngAhpWyJCmToLZsGP7iEFjgkE6PFBbHFCVSjYiLj15OfvRA57AMPVR45-zNTRNWpqvpnIyblwgkGyCi45ZwMQBOgwdrBaWZi9SelKBNKz0tGZw9fxyzI5dHJR0fqdVHg8ZOdsZvguzFb2hVpu6d3cxwDyIWtbggdNi0hf6TOqwYwqLNjV6dIQqayXh5-dyAm3LKaC_DqsXUpc-5CbnwGHuNkk1fel_yOCaWHI0HSjdoh3uditXlq7j28tNdzQyn_GgscEFtjBfJmFDGKp2_UOOg6fpaBbPC6aSGsHeiT_Z-h4SYK1P26auZhk%3D) The referendum will only take a few moments of your time. Before you complete it, I want to remind you of some things we've seen (and lived through) in recent years--which we never thought we'd see in our country... Government mandates forcing Christian churches to close while casinos, strip clubs, and drug dispensaries remained open.
Far-Left activists targeting Christian business owners, Christian teachers, and Christian coaches through litigation.
The weaponizing of the Department of Justice and the FBI against Christian pro-life advocates who are defined as believing in "extremist ideologies" and having the potential for "domestic terrorism."
Christians in the workplace being forced to hide their faith while critical race theory and transgenderism are promoted. **That 's why I'm asking you to take the Hillsdale College National Referendum on Religious Liberty.** By completing this survey, you will help us more clearly understand the views of the American people on this critical issue. Your responses will help Hillsdale strengthen its nationwide educational outreach efforts on behalf of liberty--including religious liberty. Friend, you can complete the **National Referendum on Religious Liberty** by using the secure link below: [**https://info.hillsdale.edu/religious-liberty-survey**](http://links.reclamingamericanfreedom.com/u/click?_t=5ec072595b1347f09b4bac0de82ea87d&_m=3ff7096718c14b15997071b5ec2055e8&_e=BnwjdBGuzBG8Exnb90Ta3BAYBiuvVPy3Vmq_nimAOQ-qE8Sco4u3t4e1zSWXmY6zq-hkmEx1QvlnCh8FXbtXIf8NllLJfS2NmMjbvRjbnbnca_K9rvngAhpWyJCmToLZsGP7iEFjgkE6PFBbHFCVSjYiLj15OfvRA57AMPVR45-zNTRNWpqvpnIyblwgkGyCi45ZwMQBOgwdrBaWZi9SelKBNKz0tGZw9fxyzI5dHJR0fqdVHg8ZOdsZvguzFb2h8u7TNiBDj6iSBlAld9buB0R6OMGJ7f0NT4wu_JKtTUr47T8kCsZ6xmpSLQq6G5C9Y8A7qM1I1r3C0ABn_PASUWTahrWoQHEKo0Jlcv4ei1w9ADtDm-GHemBVoW7yFfBdSV_3q9NcueoTqiZMbJUlsHyDdpiRU5-xm84romvOxh8%3D) Like the founders of our nation, the founders of Hillsdale College understood the essential connection between faith and freedom. **The College was founded in 1844 to offer "sound learning" in order to preserve "the inestimable blessings...of civil and religious liberty."** Yet today's ruling class mocks people of faith as "backward," "bigots," and even "Christo-fascists." Politicians like Barack Obama claim that people like you "get bitter" and "cling to guns or religion." These so-called elites are full of contempt for you and for Christianity and will not protect your civil and religious liberties. You and I know that religious liberty in America is under serious threat. But we can reverse course. **The first step is ...EDUCATION.** Today's government policies that threaten religious liberty were conceived and implemented by people deceived by far-Left propaganda in our K-12 schools, colleges, and universities. That's why a vital part of this fight--in which Hillsdale College is a leader--is **reaching** and **teaching** millions of citizens the TRUTH about America's great heritage of religious liberty. We are heavily invested in our educational outreach efforts through our **Project to Save Religious Liberty in America.** These efforts include: **Teaching nearly 1,600 undergraduate and graduate students** --on our campuses in Hillsdale, Michigan, and Washington, D.C.--who bring their traditional faith with them as they join the fight to restore liberty and constitutional government in our country.
**Distributing _Imprimis_** , America's leading digest of liberty, which is mailed to more than 6.6 million homes and businesses ten times per year. Previous issues include "Inside the Transgender Empire" by Christopher F. Rufo, "The Most Important Decision in Life" by Bishop Robert Barron, and "The Politicization of the Department of Justice" by Harmeet K. Dhillon.
**Producing and promoting free online courses** for any citizen who wishes to learn on topics such as Christianity, the Constitution, American history, and free market economics. Several courses (including "The Genesis Story: Reading Biblical Narratives," "Ancient Christianity," and "C.S. Lewis on Christianity") consider the important connection between faith and liberty.
**Providing a classical K-12 curriculum** with a solid grounding in American history, constitutional government, and economics to schools that educate thousands of students nationwide. After everything we've seen in recent years, the need for Hillsdale's educational outreach efforts is undeniable. So please share your views by completing the **National Referendum on Religious Liberty** and returning it to me as soon as possible. It will only take a few moments of your time. Your completed survey will help us to develop the most effective strategy to **reach** and **teach** millions of Americans on behalf of liberty. As you might know, Hillsdale College refuses to accept ONE PENNY of government funding--not even indirectly in the form of federal or state student grants or loans. **This independence frees the College from burdensome and corrupting government regulations. Thanks to our generous donors, we remain true to our original mission, even as many other colleges and universities reject religious liberty and embrace radical and dangerous ideologies.** I hope you will partner with Hillsdale College by making a generous tax-deductible gift today. You can easily do so after completing the **National Referendum on Religious Liberty** , which you can access at this secure link: [**https://info.hillsdale.edu/religious-liberty-survey**](http://links.reclamingamericanfreedom.com/u/click?_t=5ec072595b1347f09b4bac0de82ea87d&_m=3ff7096718c14b15997071b5ec2055e8&_e=BnwjdBGuzBG8Exnb90Ta3BAYBiuvVPy3Vmq_nimAOQ-qE8Sco4u3t4e1zSWXmY6zq-hkmEx1QvlnCh8FXbtXIf8NllLJfS2NmMjbvRjbnbnca_K9rvngAhpWyJCmToLZsGP7iEFjgkE6PFBbHFCVSjYiLj15OfvRA57AMPVR45-zNTRNWpqvpnIyblwgkGyCi45ZwMQBOgwdrBaWZi9SelKBNKz0tGZw9fxyzI5dHJR0fqdVHg8ZOdsZvguzFb2hyGE_TyxqLUqiD3CPszcdpmQ4zeI73QJLktLe07-RSTSDgZ1ymZ69uVJHMhzb9pBkJlVNwBOtCO7O7zWdiYlTiitdae-ZCrNXfP1xqi-yQ5IUP3NMtkBf13yHYf_l3lRRoUenKUl20jOkomTMiJHRJNIZEhil6NZZ5n5nnDTLgRk%3D) Thank you for your support of Hillsdale's educational outreach efforts on behalf of religious liberty. Warm regards, Larry P. Arnn President, Hillsdale College Pursuing Truth and Defending Liberty Since 1844 |

| Reclaiming American Freedom

clean_body: You have been selected by Hillsdale College to represent your state in a National Referendum on Religious Liberty. This sponsored message from Reclaiming American Freedom is on behalf of Hillsdale College Dear Friend, You have been selected by Hillsdale College to represent your state in a National Referendum on Religious Liberty. The referendum will only take a few moments of your time. Before you complete it, I want to remind you of some things we've seen (and lived through) in recent years--which we never thought we'd see in our country... Government mandates forcing Christian churches to close while casinos, strip clubs, and drug dispensaries remained open. Far-Left activists targeting Christian business owners, Christian teachers, and Christian coaches through litigation. The weaponizing of the Department of Justice and the FBI against Christian pro-life advocates who are defined as believing in "extremist ideologies" and having the potential for "domestic terrorism." Christians in the workplace being forced to hide their faith while critical race theory and transgenderism are promoted. That 's why I'm asking you to take the Hillsdale College National Referendum on Religious Liberty. By completing this survey, you will help us more clearly understand the views of the American people on this critical issue. Your responses will help Hillsdale strengthen its nationwide educational outreach efforts on behalf of liberty--including religious liberty. Friend, you can complete the National Referendum on Religious Liberty by using the secure link below: Like the founders of our nation, the founders of Hillsdale College understood the essential connection between faith and freedom. The College was founded in 1844 to offer "sound learning" in order to preserve "the inestimable blessings...of civil and religious liberty." Yet today's ruling class mocks people of faith as "backward," "bigots," and even "Christo-fascists." Politicians like Barack Obama claim that people like you "get bitter" and "cling to guns or religion." These so-called elites are full of contempt for you and for Christianity and will not protect your civil and religious liberties. You and I know that religious liberty in America is under serious threat. But we can reverse course. The first step is ...EDUCATION. Today's government policies that threaten religious liberty were conceived and implemented by people deceived by far-Left propaganda in our K-12 schools, colleges, and universities. That's why a vital part of this fight--in which Hillsdale College is a leader--is reaching and teaching millions of citizens the TRUTH about America's great heritage of religious liberty. We are heavily invested in our educational outreach efforts through our Project to Save Religious Liberty in America. These efforts include: Teaching nearly 1,600 undergraduate and graduate students --on our campuses in Hillsdale, Michigan, and Washington, D.C.--who bring their traditional faith with them as they join the fight to restore liberty and constitutional government in our country. Distributing Imprimis , America's leading digest of liberty, which is mailed to more than 6.6 million homes and businesses ten times per year. Previous issues include "Inside the Transgender Empire" by Christopher F. Rufo, "The Most Important Decision in Life" by Bishop Robert Barron, and "The Politicization of the Department of Justice" by Harmeet K. Dhillon. Producing and promoting free online courses for any citizen who wishes to learn on topics such as Christianity, the Constitution, American history, and free market economics. Several courses (including "The Genesis Story: Reading Biblical Narratives," "Ancient Christianity," and "C.S. Lewis on Christianity") consider the important connection between faith and liberty. Providing a classical K-12 curriculum with a solid grounding in American history, constitutional government, and economics to schools that educate thousands of students nationwide. After everything we've seen in recent years, the need for Hillsdale's educational outreach efforts is undeniable. So please share your views by completing the National Referendum on Religious Liberty and returning it to me as soon as possible. It will only take a few moments of your time. Your completed survey will help us to develop the most effective strategy to reach and teach millions of Americans on behalf of liberty. As you might know, Hillsdale College refuses to accept ONE PENNY of government funding--not even indirectly in the form of federal or state student grants or loans. This independence frees the College from burdensome and corrupting government regulations. Thanks to our generous donors, we remain true to our original mission, even as many other colleges and universities reject religious liberty and embrace radical and dangerous ideologies. I hope you will partner with Hillsdale College by making a generous tax-deductible gift today. You can easily do so after completing the National Referendum on Religious Liberty , which you can access at this secure link: Thank you for your support of Hillsdale's educational outreach efforts on behalf of religious liberty. Warm regards, Larry P. Arnn President, Hillsdale College Pursuing Truth and Defending Liberty Since 1844 | | Reclaiming American Freedom PO Box 461 Fort Collins, CO 80522

I watched Trump's inauguration so you wouldn't have to--and here are the results. Donald Trump has been sworn in agains the 47th President of the United States. His inauguration address laid out his vision for the next four years. And while he leaned heavily into words like freedom and peace, his stated actions indicate domestic and international conflict and violence. Here's the breakdown and key points from his first remarks after being sworn in. **Let 's Address This.**
